bio: Mark Cuban, a native of Pittsburgh, PA, a graduate of Indiana University, and now a Dallas, TX resident has always been an entrepreneur.From selling and trading baseball cards, selling garbage bags and magazines door-to-door, to starting a business buying and selling stamps at age 16, there have been few years in his life when he wasn’t starting or running a business. After graduating from IU, a friend suggested he come to Dallas. Off Mark went to live in “The Village” with five roommates, getting only the floor in the corner of a bedroom as his new “home.” Starting as a bartender, he was not there long before he got a job at one of Dallas’ first retail software stores, Your Business Software.He spent nine months doing everything from learning how to code, supporting and installing every type of business software and of course, making sure the store opened on time. That went well until he made the executive decision to turn over the store opening duties to a peer so he could pick up a check for a sale.He was fired.With no money and still sleeping on the floor, Mark was relentless until he found his first customer, Architectural Lighting, who fronted him the money for the software they needed. That advance was all Mark needed. Over the next seven vacationless years, MicroSolutions became a national leader in Systems Integration and custom applications for local and wide area networks, making it near the top of LAN Magazine’s LAN 100. Growing to 80 employees, never having a losing month of operations and nearly $36M in annualized sales, in 1990, MicroSolutions was sold to CompuServe, a subsidiary of H&R Block for $6M. At that point Mark “retired ” to investing in public and private companies.  His knowledge of the networking industry led to success and brought returns of 80 percent and more each year. He used that track record to briefly build a hedge fund, for which he got an offer he couldn’t refuse and sold within 12 months. After a brief stint in LA to have fun taking acting classes and doing fortunately long-forgotten commercials and B movies, he returned to Dallas.During a lunch with a college friend, Todd Wagner, they discussed how they might use “this new thing called the internet” to listen to broadcasts of IU basketball games in Dallas–at that time, an impossibility. Using his knowledge of networking and Todd’s extensive background on law and business, they started Audionet.com, the first commercial streaming company in the world.AudioNet grew quickly and soon became Broadcast.com, adding video and much more, going public in July 1998 with what would be the largest first day jump in share valuation in the history of the stock market! Broadcast.com was the largest multimedia site on the internet, without a close second. After being pursued by multiple possible acquisitors, Broadcast.com was sold for $5.7B in stock to Yahoo Inc. Once the deal closed, Mark became concerned that the internet stock boom could become a bust, so he hedged his stock, putting on a long short collar that would go on to be called “one of the top 10 stock market trades of all time.” It was at the same time Mark purchased the Dallas Mavericks for $285M. The Mavs would have the second-best record in the NBA during his ownership tenure, got to the NBA Finals in 2006, and won the World Championship in 2011.Mark changed the nature of ownership of a professional team. He sat with fans. He took a vocal stand on officiating, getting fined millions of dollars. He also led to significant changes in how teams were operated. He was the first to bring in Player Development coaches. At the time he was criticized, but it is now done by every team. He had the NBA change the clear path rule to 2 shots and the ball after showing the league the math behind the incentives to foul. He added mics to the rim of the American Airlines Center, expanded in arena entertainment and fan engagement. He considers his greatest failure during his tenure as Governor of the Mavs to be his inability to help the NBA improve the officiating, despite spending more than $3M in fines. Mark sold majority control of the Mavs in 2023, but continues to be actively involved with the team. In addition to the Mavs, Mark continued to be active as an entrepreneur and investor. Along with Phil Garvin, in 2001, they started HDNet and HDNet Movies, the world’s first all High-Definition TV network. That started the path for Todd Wagner and Mark to focus on building the first fully integrated media and entertainment company. Adding Landmark Theaters, starting 2929 Productions, HDNet Films and Magnolia Pictures, Todd and Mark set a new entertainment path.

Andrew is co-founder and CEO of Cerebras Systems. He is an entrepreneur dedicated to pushing boundaries in the compute space. Prior to Cerebras, he co-founded and was CEO of SeaMicro, a pioneer of energy-efficient, high-bandwidth microservers. SeaMicro was acquired by AMD in 2012 for $357M.Before SeaMicro, Andrew was the Vice President of Product Management, Marketing and BD at Force10 Networks which was later sold to Dell Computing for $800M. Prior to Force10 Networks, Andrew was the Vice President of Marketing and Corporate Development at RiverStone Networks from the company’s inception through IPO in 2001.Andrew holds a BA and an MBA from Stanford University.

Pioneer of modern AI and deep learning, inventor of convolutional neural networks (CNNs), Turing Award laureate, NYU professor, and former Chief AI Scientist at Meta, where he founded FAIR. Has consistently advanced open science and long-term research, shaping how AI is built, shared, and applied worldwide.

Anton Osika co-founded Lovable in 2023, an AI software engineer that lets anyone build applications up to 20x faster than writing code. Under his leadership, Lovable achieved $4 million in annual recurring revenue (ARR) within four weeks of launch and $10 million ARR in two months, making it Europe's fastest-growing startup. Before Lovable, Anton has a background in Particle Physics working at CERN, Deep Learning research as the first employee at Sana Labs and serving as CTO Depict, a YC startup that raised $20m.

Arthur Mensch is a French entrepreneur and scientist.In 2023, Arthur Mensch, along with Guillaume Lample and Timothée Lacroix, founded Mistral AI with the mission of making generative AI ubiquitous and pioneering a new approach to AI - one that is more open, portable, independent, and accessible to all.

Mati Staniszewski is the Co-Founder and CEO of ElevenLabs, the AI research and product company building audio tools for businesses, developers, and creators. The company’s two platforms span creative work for speech, music, and sound generation, and a platform for voice agents that listen, talk, and act. Before founding ElevenLabs, Mati worked at Palantir as a Deployment Strategist with Fortune 500 companies and governments, and at BlackRock, where he helped launch the Aladdin Wealth platform

Aaron Ginn is the co-founder and CEO of Hydra Host, one of the largest GPU management platforms in the world and the defining operating system for AI factories. A pioneer in both technology and public policy, Aaron previously held growth and product leadership roles at Everlane and StumbleUpon, and co-founded the Foundation for American Innovation and Fabius Labs to bridge the gap between Silicon Valley and Washington. A Forbes 30 Under 30 honoree, he is a contributor to The Wall Street Journal, Fox News, and Washington Post. He is a recognized speaker on artificial intelligence, decentralized infrastructure, and national tech policy.

Abel is a founding partner at >commit, the first transatlantic VC focused on investing on Commercial Open Source companies. He started coding and building projects at a young age, before becoming an AI engineer deploying deep learning models (BERT, etc.) in large enterprises. He later spent six years at Red River West investing in infrastructure software, while building the data team and the firm's data platform. He holds engineering and business degrees.

Abhishek Das is co-CEO of Yutori, where he's building AI agents that work autonomously in the background. A former research scientist at Meta's Fundamental AI Research (FAIR),Das worked on deep neural networks as a part of the Open Catalyst Project. He earned his PhD from Georgia Tech, where his thesis on agents that can see, talk, and act received the AAAI/ACM SIGAI Doctoral Dissertation Award runner-up. His PhD research was supported by fellowships from Facebook, Adobe, and Snap.

job_title: Head of West Coast Technology Investment Banking
bio: Akhil runs the Enterprise team within the Technology Investment Banking group. He overseas BofA’s coverage of companies in the Software-Defined Infrastructure, Cloud Computing, Data and Analytics software, and AI sectors.Akhil joined BofA’s Technology Investment Banking team in late 2022. Prior to that he ran the Enterprise team at Barclays from 2019 to late 2022, was a senior member of Wells Fargo’s Technology investment banking team from 2011 to 2019, and began his investment banking career with JPMorgan’s technology investment banking group in 2002 through 2011. Akhil earned his BS from the University of California at San Diego and then his MBA from the Stanford Graduate School of Business.

Alain Wilmouth from a two-year university degree in Economic Sciences. Self-taught and an entrepreneur, he has been building for more than 25 years his technical expertise and his reputation in the IT world. His numerous experience in management positions in the IT sector have allowed him to acquire a solid managerial experience in management and business administration while sharpening his technical background. After several company creations, he founded 2CRSi in 2005.

job_title: Head of Digital Enterprise Solutions and Group CIO
bio: Alessandro de Luca is the Group Chief Information Officer of Merck KGaA, a global science and technology leader with over 66,000 employees, headquartered in Darmstadt, Germany and he has been appointed to the roleof Head of Digital Enterprise Solutions, driving the company’s global digital agenda by integrating Data, Technology and Processes into one single function, DES, a global function with @6,000 employee worldwide. As Head of DES and Group CIO, Alessandro leads Merck digital transformation, with the mission to deliver high-impact digital innovations and scalable enterprise services that support the company’s business growth and long-term aspirations. His leadership has been instrumental in redefining how Merck operates and engages with customers—by embedding cutting-edge AI advanced solutions and digital capabilities, enhancing operationalefficiency, and enabling data-driven decision-making across all business sectors. Previously, Alessandro served as CIO for Merck Healthcare, overseeing a comprehensive technology portfolio spanning R&D, Commercial, Marketing & Sales, and Global Franchises. Earlier in his Merck career, when he joined in 2011, he was responsible for global supply chain operations within Merck Healthcare, including end-to-endplanning, physical distribution, and warehousing. Before joining Merck, Alessandro spent two decades at Procter&Gamble, where he held a series ofleadership roles across Innovation, Manufacturing, Engineering, and Supply Chain Management in the fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) sector. His experience at P&G laid the foundation for his deep expertise in leadingcomplex transformations and scaling global operations. Alessandro has a degree in Nuclear Engineering as well as Civil Engineering from La Sapienza Università di Roma.​

Alex Atallah is the co-founder and CEO of OpenRouter, the first and largest gateway for large language models like GPT-4, Claude, Llama, DeepSeek, and more. With over 4 million developers and 250K apps on the platform, OpenRouter now provides access to the most models, the best performance and uptime, and the most insights about how to use them, emphasizing transparency, choice, and performance at scale. Before diving into the AI space, Alex co-founded OpenSea, the first and largest NFT marketplace, and helped build the early foundations of Web3. Originally trained in computer science at Stanford, Alex has spent his career working on tools that make the internet more open, creative, and accessible. These days, he's focused on helping people get the most out of AI, without the complexity or closed ecosystems. Alex has been recognized by Forbes 30 Under 30 and has been featured in The New York Times, Bloomberg, and The Wall Street Journal.

Alex Banks is a founder, creator and investor. He reaches over 1 million people on social media each week with insights into the world of artificial intelligence.He is the founder and writer of The Signal, a weekly newsletter that cuts through the AI noise to over 55,000+ readers.Alex is also an active early-stage investor and advisor to startups across the AI and technology landscape.

Alex Bouzari, Co-Founder and CEO of DDN, is a visionary leader shaping the future of AI and data intelligence. For over two decades, Alex has redefined how organizations harness data to drive innovation, pushing the boundaries of what & possible in AI, high-performance computing, and analytics. His early recognition of the symbiotic relationship between data-centric computing and AI has positioned DDN as the technology partner of choice for companies tackling humanity’s greatest challenges--from pioneering space exploration to breakthroughs in personalized medicine. Under Alex’s leadership, DDN has contributed to the transformation of HPC from a niche field into a foundational pillar of enterprise AI and data-driven decision-making across industries. His relentless focus on innovation, scalability, and customer impact has driven DDN to power AI factories, complex simulations, and financial modeling for over 85 of the Fortune 500, including 7 of the top 10 global financial services firms and 6 of the top 10 automotive companies. Visionary customers such as NVIDIA and Elon Musk’s xAI trust DDN’s data intelligence platforms to accelerate their AI pipelines and reduce time-to-market for groundbreaking products. Together with co-founder Paul Bloch, Alex has spearheaded initiatives that empower enterprises to unlock new business models through AI. His thought leadership on AI-driven business transformation has inspired a generation of data leaders, cementing DDN’s role as the preeminent force in AI data intelligence. Through a combination of strategic foresight, product excellence, and a passion for customer success, Alex Bouzari continues to shape the future of AI, ensuring thatorganizations across the globe can fully realize the potential of their data to drive human progress and prosperity.

Alex Mashrabov is the Co-Founder and CEO of Higgsfield AI, leading generative video AI company for social media.  Before Higgsfield, Alex co-founded AI Factory, the startup behind Snapchat’s viral Cameos and Face Filters, which reached billions of users and was acquired by Snap in 2020. At Snap, he served as Director of Generative AI, leading teams that shaped the company’s early AI strategy.

Alex Saroyan is CEO and co-founder of Netris, the leading network automation platform for AI cloud operators. With 25+ years of experience in large-scale data center and telecom networking, Alex brings deep expertise to AI infrastructure challenges. Before Netris, he held engineering leadership roles at Lycos, Orange (France Télécom) and Ucom, where he architected a global transit network and scaled carrier infrastructure nearly 100x. Alex co-founded Netris in 2018 to bring cloud-provider-grade network automation and multi-tenancy to every AI infrastructure operator.

Alex LeBrun is a serial entrepreneur who has spent two decades at the frontier of AI — before it was a buzzword. He founded one of the earliest conversational AI companies, VirtuOz (acquired by Nuance/Microsoft), and Wit.ai (acquired by Meta). He later co-founded Nabla, bringing AI to the high-stakes world of medicine. A graduate of École Polytechnique, Alex's career is defined by a single obsession- closing the gap between AI research and the real world.

Alexey Milovidov is co-founder and CTO of ClickHouse, Inc. Alexey has 17 years of experience designing, developing, and operating data-intensive applications. He started work on ClickHouse in 2009 and initiated its release in open source in 2016. Before ClickHouse, he created the data processing engine of the world's 2nd largest web analytics system. His area of interest is data processing algorithms and technologies.

job_title: Group Chief Tech, Data & Digital Officer
bio: Alice has been Sodexo’s Group Chief Tech, Data & Digital Officer and a member of the Global Executive Team since 2023.With over 20 years’ experience in management consulting (EY, CapGemini, Accenture) and in Information Systems Departments such as inthe Préfecture de Police de Paris, Alice has led major digital and IT transformation missions and has developed strong knowledge in organization transformation thanks to operational implementation of information systems.More recently, Alice was Group Chief Digital & Information Officer at Saur Group.Since 2022, Alice has also been Vice-Chairwoman of Cigref, the associationrepresenting the largest French companies using digital solutions and services.Alice holds a Master Degree in Computer Science of the University of Technology in Compiègne (France).

Ameya serves as VP, Field CTO at Braintrust, the AI observability platform for shipping quality AI products. He has spent his career using AI to help people be more effective and stay safe, and has driven multiple 0→1 initiatives to internet-scale, shipping intelligent systems for Dropbox Dash, Microsoft Office, Facebook, Windows, and major web browsers. At Dropbox, he led ML Engineering for universal AI search in Dash (search, discovery, recommendations, agentic conversational AI). Before that, he led Microsoft’s Office AI team, shipping AI features across Word, Excel, and PowerPoint, and created Office Copilot.

Amin Vahdat is a Fellow and the Chief Technologist for AI & Infrastructure at Google, where his team is responsible for delivering industry-leading infrastructure which spans custom silicon, data centers, network, and supply chain and operations. This infrastructure serves Alphabet, Google and the world, and Artificial Intelligence technologies that empower ML developers and solve customers’ most pressing business challenges. In the past, he was Vice President and General Manager for Google's compute, storage, and network hardware and software infrastructure. Until 2019, he was the Technical Lead and Vice President for the Networking organization at Google. Before joining Google, Amin was the Science Applications International Corporation (SAIC) Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at UC San Diego (UCSD). He received his doctorate from the University of California Berkeley in computer science, and is a Fellow of the Association for Computing Machinery (ACM). Amin has been recognized with a number of awards, including the National Science Foundation (NSF) CAREER award, the UC Berkeley Distinguished EECS Alumni Award, the Alfred P. Sloan Fellowship, the Association for Computing Machinery's SIGCOMM Networking Systems Award, and the Duke University David and Janet Vaughn Teaching Award. Amin was awarded the SIGCOMM lifetime achievement award for his contributions to data center and wide area networks.  He was inducted into the National Academy of Engineering in 2023 for his contributions to the design and implementation of datacenter and planet-scale networks that power cloud computer systems.

As president, chief product officer, and chief operating officer, Amit Zavery leads ServiceNow’s platform, products, engineering, cloud infrastructure, and user experience. He also runs enterprise-wide operations to ensure business rigor — from innovation to execution.Previously, Zavery was vice president & general manager and head of platform for Google Cloud, where he was responsible for defining product strategy, running engineering, streamlining business operations, and building the business application platform business. At Google Cloud, Amit was part of the leadership team that grew the company into the fourth largest enterprise software company in the world while increasing Google Cloud’s annualized revenue from more than $7B to over $41B.Before Google, Amit was executive vice president and corporate officer of Oracle Cloud Infrastructure and Middleware products. He led Oracle’s product vision, design, development, and go-to-market strategy for cloud platform, middleware, Java and analytics portfolio and oversaw a global team of more than 4,500 engineers generating more than $6B in annual revenue.Amit has a proven track record of designing and delivering innovative andmarket-leading enterprise products, scaling businesses, and building organizations by recruiting and retaining world-class talent.He holds a Bachelor of Science in Electrical and Computer Engineering from The University of Texas at Austin and a Master of Science in Information Networking from Carnegie Mellon University.

Amritansh Raghav is the Chief Technology Officer at Asana. In this role, he oversees the technical strategy and engineering execution that power Asana's vision for the Agentic Enterprise, including driving engineering excellence, scaling product delivery, and ensuring technology investments align with business outcomes.Amritansh is a multifaceted leader with 30+ years of experience spanning Engineering, Product, and Design, across both large companies and high-growth startups. Previously, he held leadership positions at Outreach, Skype, Microsoft, and Google. He is passionate about the craft of engineering and empowering teams to do their best work.Amritansh holds a Master's in Electrical Engineering and a Bachelor's in Electronics and Communication Engineering. Outside of work, he is a movie buff, enjoys collecting vinyl, traveling and arguing with his teenage children.

Anas Biad is a Partner at Sequoia. He focuses on the growth-stage (Series B+) across the U.S. and Europe and works with companies including Serval, Profound, Nominal, Avelios, Harvey, Pennylane, and Trade Republic. He joined Sequoia in 2021 from Silver Lake, where he was involved in several of the firm’s late-stage software investments. Prior to that, he was at Bain, advising on due diligence for tech companies in Asia and Europe. Anas grew up in Morocco and holds an MSc in Mathematics from École Polytechnique in France.

Anastasis Germanidis is a Greek AI researcher, software engineer and artist based in New York City. He is the co-CEO and co-founder of Runway. He previously was a machine learning researcher at IBM and a distributed systems back-end engineer.

Andrew Dieckmann is corporate vice president and general manager of the Data Center GPU Business Unit at AMD, where he leads the AMD Instinct™ accelerator business. In this role, he is responsible for business strategy and execution across product management, product marketing, business development, and business operations for AMD data center GPU products and solutions.Since joining AMD in 2022, Andrew has helped scale AMD Instinct from a high-performance computing accelerator portfolio into a rapidly growing AI accelerator franchise central to AMD’s data center and AI strategy. During this period, AMD has expanded the Instinct roadmap across multiple generations, including the AMD Instinct MI300 and MI350 Series accelerators, continued advances in the ROCm™ open software ecosystem and the introduction of rack-scale AI infrastructure such as AMD “Helios.” As demand for AI training, inference and agentic AI workloads accelerates, Andrew’s team works closely with cloud providers, OEMs, ODMs, enterprise customers and ecosystem partners to deliver open, high-performance AI solutions at scale.Andrew brings more than 20 years of marketing, engineering and business leadership experience in data center server and storage technologies. Before joining AMD, he held leadership roles at Microchip Technology’s Microsemi subsidiary, Microsemi and PMC-Sierra, where he helped build and scale enterprise storage businesses and led teams spanning product management, strategy, product marketing and applications engineering.Andrew holds an electrical engineering degree from Lakehead University in Ontario, Canada.

Before founding Maverick Silicon in 2024, Mr. Homan led Maverick Capital’s investments across the semiconductor, hardware, and software industries. Mr. Homan also serves on the external boards of Coherent Global, Empower Semiconductor, SiMa Technologies, TensorWave, and Upscale AI. Prior to joining Maverick in 2004, he was an Analyst in the Investment Banking Division, Financial Institutions Group at Goldman, Sachs & Co. Mr. Homan received his B.A. in Economics from Yale University, where he was a member of the heavyweight crew team.

Andrey Khusid is the Founder and CEO of Miro — the Human x AI Innovation Workspace that enables organizations of any size to build the next big thing. Funded by leading investors like Accel, Iconiq and others, Miro has more than 1600 employees in 14 offices around the globe, serves more than 90 million users worldwide and is used by 250,000 companies for the end-to-end innovation lifecycle, from Discovery to Delivery.

Anish Agarwal is the CEO and Cofounder of Traversal, the frontier lab behind the first and only AI SRE platform validated within the Fortune 100. Backed by Sequoia and Kleiner Perkins, Traversal helps enterprises autonomously prevent, diagnose, and heal production incidents at scale. A professor of Computer Science and Operations Research at Columbia University, Anish earned his Ph.D. in Computer Science from MIT, specializing in causal machine learning — teaching AI to understand cause and effect from data. His career also includes roles as a management consultant at BCG and research scientist at Amazon and Microsoft.

Anna Goldie is Founder and CEO of Ricursive Intelligence. She previously co-led the AlphaChip project, and co-founded the ML for Systems team at Google. She was an early employee at Anthropic and a Senior Staff Research Scientist at Google DeepMind, where she developed frontier models such as Claude and Gemini. At MIT, she double majored in EECS and Linguistics and earned a Masters in EECS. She also holds a CS PhD from Stanford. She was named one of MIT Technology Review's 35 Innovators Under 35, and her research has been widely covered in major media outlets and published in Nature, NeurIPS, ICLR, EMNLP, COLM, ISPD, ICLAD, MLCAD, MLSys, and ASPLOS.

job_title: Chief Information Officer & Chief Security Officer
bio: Anthony Moisant is Chief Information Officer (CIO) & Chief Security Officer (CSO) at Indeed. In his role he leads the global teams responsible for protecting Indeed’s marketplace, including cybersecurity, privacy, data protection, physical security, and enterprise systems. His work is centered on building trust while ensuring that Indeed’s services are resilient, reliable, and secure.Anthony is also driving Indeed’s evolution into an AI-first company. He focuses on bringing secure and responsible AI into everyday workflows so teams can work smarter, move faster, and deliver better outcomes for job seekers and employers.In addition to his role at Indeed, Anthony serves as an executive officer at Indeed’s parent company Recruit Holdings Co., Ltd., where he oversees security and information technology across the company’s global HR technology business.    Prior to joining Indeed, Anthony served as SVP & Chief Information Officer at Glassdoor.  He also held leadership roles at Tribune Media and Sony Gracenote.Anthony completed the CIO Executive Program at Boston University and is a graduate of the U.S. Navy Nuclear Submarine School. He credits his military service with shaping his leadership philosophy, instilling resilience, accountability, and a deep commitment to mission and teamwork.His first job was at a McDonald’s in Chicago where he learned the importance of discipline, customer service, and earning trust through consistent execution.

Operating out of Dubai (UAE) and the United States, Antoine is a visionary technology entrepreneur and investor. He is the co-founder and managing partner of Alpha Intelligence Capital (AIC), a $400m venture capital firm investing in deep Artificial Intelligence technology companies worldwide. Over the past 25 years Antoine has dedicated himself to the development of Artificial Intelligence. He was the CEO of Dejima, the company that powered DARPA’s foundational CALO project that evolved into Apple’s Siri. Antoine was also President and COO of Nasdaq-listed Zi Corporation, whose predictive text input software was embedded in hundreds of millions of devices. He was the co-founder and CEO of Sentient Technologies, a leading San Francisco Bay Area deep AI technology startup that pioneered massively-scaled Artificial Intelligence. Antoine has held senior leadership positions at Good Technology, Salesforce, and Sybase. He is an advisor to KKR, Pictet, and BITKRAFT Ventures. Antoine is also board chair of crypto.com, one of the world’s largest blockchain and cryptocurrency full-service platforms. In addition to his fund investments, Antoine has made over 30 personal investments in deep technology firms, including SpaceX, Epic Games, Jio Platforms, Boom Supersonic, DoubleDutch (acquired by Cvent), PeerIQ (acquired by Cross River Bank), GammaNow (acquired by Animoca Brands), GoShare, KeyMe, Evolv, K Health, Inworld and Alethea. He is a founding member and General Partner of Zinc, a London-based social impact-focused technology startup incubator. Antoine started to code at age 11. He is the author of 4 patents.

President of Groupe Projex and a graduate of the HEI engineering school, Antoine has built a rich and diverse career, combining a comprehensive understanding of economic issues with a deep sensitivity to environmental and social challenges. His experience, gained through large-scale projects, allows him to orchestrate innovative initiatives aligned with the sustainable performance expectations of businesses and communities.

Antoine Moyroud is a partner at Lightspeed Venture Partners and sits on their European investment team since 2022. Specializing in Machine Learning and Data topics, his work on the emerging AI space has brought Lightspeed Venture Partners to partner with Mistral.ai, leading their inaugural round in 2023.Raised in the Netherlands and proficient in 4 languages, Antoine started his career in the data ecosystem with operational roles at FlixBus and Dataiku, spanning Germany, France and the UK. He holds dual degrees in Business Administration and Languages from the University of Lyon III, along with a master's in Data Science from IE Business School in Madrid.

Apoorv Agrawal is a Partner at Altimeter Capital, where he invests in AI and Software. He led Altimeter’s investments in OpenAI, Glean, Parloa, Xbow, Revolut and Baseten and previously backed Slack, Redis, OneTrust, and Nuro. Apoorv began his career as a software engineer at Palantir Technologies, spending four years driving large-scale commercial deployments in energy, insurance, and healthcare. His hands-on product and go-to-market work there continues to shape his investing lens today. Raised between India and Singapore, Apoorv holds a degree in Computer Science from National University of Singapore and an MBA from Stanford University. He now lives in Palo Alto, California, where free moments are usually filled with long rides, neighborhood runs, and swims in preparation for his next triathlon.

As the CEO and co-founder of Navan, Ariel Cohen is focused on the company’s growth. This is the second company Ariel has founded with Navan Chief Technology Officer Ilan Twig — the other being StreamOnce, a business multimedia integration platform that Jive Software acquired.Before StreamOnce, Ariel led product management in a senior leadership role at Hewlett-Packard. Ariel earned an Executive MBA from the Kellogg School of Management at Northwestern University.

Ariel Shulman is an accomplished executive with extensive experience in technology management, business development, marketing, and strategy. Since joining Bright Data in 2021, Ariel has leveraged his networking, security, and Internet expertise to drive innovation to access high-quality public web data solutions at scale. He now serves as VP of Product, responsible for Bright Data’s AI-integrated product suite that leads innovation.Fluent in English, French, Spanish, and Hebrew, and with professional experience across multiple countries, Ariel plays a pivotal role in shaping Bright Data's global positioning.

Arjun Kharpal is CNBC’s Senior Technology Correspondent, based in London. Since joining CNBC in 2013, he has reported from across the world’s major innovation hubs, bringing viewers and readers inside the companies, trends, and technologies shaping the future. Arjun has interviewed many of the most influential leaders from the world’s biggest technology companies as well as entrepreneurs and policymakers driving global innovation. Arjun appears across all of CNBC’s major platforms, reporting on “Squawk Box Europe” to “Morning Call” on TV and writing for CNBC.com. Arjun is the host of “The Tech Download,” CNBC’s global tech podcast.

As Chief Technologist at AVEVA, Arti Garg leads the definition and advancement of our future-facing technology strategy. In this capacity, she focuses on the evaluation and application of emerging technologies, such as generative AI, to industrial manufacturing and critical infrastructure to ensure AVEVA remains at the forefront of technological progress.

Arvind Jain is the Founder and CEO of Glean, the Work AI platform connected to all your data that allows you to find, create, and automate anything. He oversees the overall direction and strategy of the company, aiming to transform the way knowledge workers interact with AI to enhance their productivity and creativity.Prior to Glean, Arvind co-founded and led R&D at Rubrik, one of the fastest-growing companies in cloud data management. Arvind also spent over a decade at Google as a distinguished engineer, where he led teams in Google’s Search, Maps, and YouTube products. Earlier in his career, Arvind held leadership positions at Akamai and Microsoft.He earned his BTech in Computer Science from the Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi, and his Masters in Computer Science from the University of Washington.

job_title: Head of Forward Deployed Research Engineering
bio: Ash Edwards is Head of Forward Deployed Research Engineering at Poolside, pioneering agentic systems inside global enterprises. He joined Poolside through its acquisition of Fern Labs, an AI R&D company he founded that focused on long-running multi-agent systems. Prior to founding Fern Labs, Ash worked at Palantir.

Ashutosh (Ash) Kulkarni has been CEO at Elastic since January, 2022. He is aninnovation-focused technology and business leader with broad experiences inthe areas of Security, Data Analytics, and Infrastructure software. Ash joinedElastic as Chief Product Officer in early 2021, to lead Elastic's engineering andproduct teams in their pursuit of creating the industry's leading search platform,addressing customer needs from Observability, to Security, and EnterpriseSearch.Previously, Ash held senior leadership roles in products and engineering atMcAfee, Akamai, Informatica, and Sun Microsystems. He holds a BE inElectronics Engineering from the University of Mumbai, an MS in ComputerEngineering from the University of Texas at Austin, and an MBA from theUniversity of California at Berkeley.

Ashwin is the president and co-founder of Decagon, the conversational AI platform powering concierge customer experiences at businesses like Avis Budget Group, Block, and Oura Health. Earlier this year, Decagon raised a $250M Series D round led by new investors Coatue Management and Index Ventures, tripling its valuation to $4.5B in less than six months. Before co-founding Decagon, Ashwin built Helia, which was acquired by Scale AI. He earned his undergraduate and master’s degrees in computer science from Stanford University.

Astrid is a Vice President at Headline, one of the most active investors in AI globally, where she partners with founders building the next generation of AI companies across Europe and the US. Previously, she helped launch her former fund’s Berlin office and spent time operating in the New York tech ecosystem.

Avi Golan is Chief Product & Engineering Officer at Alice, overseeing its GenAI safety and security product offering. With 25+ years of product and engineering leadership, he previously built ZipRecruiter’s Israel R&D hub and served as VP engineering at Checkout.com

Azalia Mirhoseini is Founder and CTO of Ricursive Intelligence. Previously, she co-led the AlphaChip project, and co-founded the ML for Systems team at Google.She is an Assistant Professor of Computer Science and founder of the Scaling Intelligence Lab at Stanford University. Previously, she served as a Senior Staff Research Scientist at Google DeepMind. Before that, she was an early employee at Anthropic, following several years at Google Brain. Her past work includes MoEs, now predominantly used in frontier LLMs such as ChatGPT, Gemini, and DeepSeek. She has also done pioneering research on inference-time scaling. Her contributions have been recognized through the MIT Technology Review’s 35 Under 35 Award, Okawa Research Grant, Gold Medal in the National Math Olympiad and publications in flagship AI & chip design venues.

Bar Winkler is the co-founder and CEO of Wonderful, an enterprise agent platform transforming how enterprises deploy AI. In just over a year since its founding, Wonderful has raised $286 million and has become one of the fastest-growing applied AI companies. Backed by world-class investors such as Insight Partners, Index Ventures, and Bessemer Venture Partners, Wonderful is already a trusted partner to leading organizations across financial services, telecom, healthcare, and other sectors.Before starting Wonderful, Bar founded Approve.com, which was acquired by Tipalti, where he went on to serve as General Manager.  Previously, he was one of the first employees at ironSource, helping launch the company’s flagship mobile product that became central to its growth and $11B IPO.
Barak Kaufman is the Chief Strategy Officer at Wonderful, one of the fastest-growing applied AI companies in the world, operating across 30+ countries and supporting 100+ enterprises.

Ben joined Accel in 2016 and focuses on enterprise software, AI, and consumer internet companies. Since joining the firm, Ben has helped source and lead investments in Ada, BetterCloud, BrowserStack, Checkr, Galileo, Guru, Jellyfish, Merge, MessageBird, Podium, Webflow, and UnitQ. In 2024, Ben moved to London to lead Accel's growth investing efforts across Europe and his recent investments include Lovable, n8n, Nuitee and Synthesia. Prior to Accel, Ben worked on Google's M&A and investment team. Before Google, Ben worked in finance and partnerships at Apple. Ben is from Vienna, Virginia and graduated from Brigham Young University.

Ben Richardson has served as CoreWeave's Vice President of Strategy since October 2025, and also as Managing Director of CoreWeave’s International business since January 2026. Previously, he served as CEO and Chief Corporate Development Officer of Trustmarque UK from 2020 to 2025, leading the firm through a private equity–backed carve-out, multiple acquisitions, and a transformational merger.From 2010 to 2020, Richardson ran a boutique corporate finance consultancy focused on investment strategy, business transformation, and delivering clean energy transactions. From 2002 to 2010 he served in senior roles, including Managing Director at Natsource Asset Management LLC, and at Arthur Andersen in London and Sydney. He holds an undergraduate degree in Geography from St John’s College, University of Cambridge, and is a member of the Institute of Chartered Accountants in England and Wales.

Bola Malek is a software engineer and product leader specializing in machine learning, developer tools, and infrastructure. Currently serving as Head of Baseten for Labs, he previously held roles as Product Lead and Forward-Deployed Engineer at Baseten.

Carmen Li is the Founder and CEO of Silicon Data and CEO of Compute Exchange, where she is building the data and exchange infrastructure for global AI compute markets. At Silicon Data, she leads the development of independent analytics and benchmark indices for GPU pricing, utilization, performance, and carbon impact. The company’s products—including SiliconNavigator, Silicon Index (Bloomberg-listed), SiliconCarbon, and SiliconMark—bring financial-grade transparency to AI infrastructure. SiliconMark provides standardized, third-party GPU performance benchmarking to support procurement, pricing, and risk assessment. In 2025, she also became CEO of Compute Exchange, which enables transparent price discovery and executable access to compute capacity. Previously, she worked at DRW and later served as Global Head of Strategic Alliances for Enterprise Data at Bloomberg. She holds a degree from the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign and an MBA from Harvard Business School.

Caroline Basyn is an experienced Global Executive & Advisor in Strategy, Transformation, IT, Shared Services, Data & Analytics, Digital and Diversity. Caroline joined The Adecco Group in August 2023, as Chief Digital and Information Officer. In 2025, she expanded her remit to include leadership of Global Business Services (GBS), further strengthening the Group’s digital, operational, and service excellence agenda. She is based in Zurich, Switzerland. Caroline is also serving on the Board of Proximus since April 2024.In a career that has spanned over 40 years, Caroline has an extensive track record as a senior business leader in global information, digital and business services. Before joining the Adecco Group, Caroline served as Chief Strategy & Transformation Officer, Europe at PepsiCo. She also previously held leadership positions as Global Business Services and Chief Information Officer at Mondelez and Group CIO at Bacardi. Prior to this, she had an over 25-year career at Procter & Gamble in IT, Digital and Global Services, leading large cross functional transformation programs in sales, marketing, supply chain, and marketing. Caroline is known for her strategic interventions, learning agility, creating a vision, developing organization structure, defining a master plan, and motivating her team & partners to deliver with excellence. Caroline is convinced that the digital and AI revolution is only at it's beginning and will become pervasive in all we do, everywhere, in everything at any time. Caroline is a global citizen, enjoying to work across multiple cultures, she has a passion to enable women to develop their careers.Caroline received her Candidatures et Licenses, Mathématiques from Universite Catholique de Louvain-La-Neuve in Belgium

Caroline is a Partner at Brunswick Group, and specialises in technology, artificial intelligence and media, providing strategic communications advice to boards and senior executives at both FTSE100 companies and tech start-ups. Her work includes advising on crisis situations, AI narrative development, IPOs, periods of intense change, financial transactions and stakeholder engagement. She is part of the global Brunswick AI Client Impact Hub. She spent 17 years at the Financial Times in leading editorial roles including as Editor of FT Weekend (2010-2016), Opinion Page Editor, White House correspondent and technology writer. She has previously made broadcast appearances on BBC, BBC Radio 4, Fox and Sky News, as well as been a frequent moderator at tech and geopolitical events including Brilliant Minds, WebSummit and CogX. Caroline was appointed as an Independent Adviser to the Editorial Guidelines and Standards Committee of the BBC from 2022 until 2025, and in 2021 was part of the Serota Review of BBC editorial processes, governance, and culture. She is member of the Trilateral Commission, a global non-governmental discussion group of leading executives and politicians; and is a Trustee of the UK’s leading non-fiction prize, the Baillie-Gifford Prize and of The Trampery, a social enterprise in London.

Cathy Gao is a Partner at Sapphire Ventures, where she invests in B2B AI and software. She backs founders building AI-native companies that become core to how work gets done, not tools that sit on top of it–AI that drives outcomes customers can measure, earns repeatable distribution, and compounds into workflow ownership over time. Cathy serves on the boards of EliseAI, Blue J, Jeeva AI, Sword Health, and Medable. Before Sapphire, Cathy invested at AXA Venture Partners and began her career at TA Associates and Blackstone. She also joined Gusto at the Series B, giving her firsthand experience inside a high-growth software company. Her perspectives on enterprise AI have been featured in Fortune, Bloomberg, CNBC, and The Wall Street Journal.

Cecilia joined Accel in 2021 and focuses on AI, fintech and consumer businesses. She led Accel’s investment in SolveAI and works closely with the teams at Fuse, Perceptic, and Viktor. Prior to Accel, Cecilia was at Revolut, working under the founder and CEO Nik Storonsky on Product Strategy and helping the company reach profitability. Previously, she worked at Citi in New York and London. Cecilia was born in Sweden and grew up in Hong Kong. She graduated from the University of California, Berkeley.

job_title: Executive Director, Technology & Analytics and Chief Privacy Officer
bio: Chantz Strong is the Executive Director, Technology & Analytics and Chief Privacy Officer at the CMPA, Chantz oversees multiple departments, including AI, business intelligence, web & application development, IT infrastructure, IT Security, and Privacy.  His current professional interest is in the safe and ethical deployment of AI technologies. He is the lead for AI implementation within his organization and is passionate about finding ways for AI to be deployed in healthcare in a way that benefits patients and providers.   Chantz holds an MSc in Systems Engineering from MIT (Boston, USA) and a BSc in Computer Science and Economics from McGill University (Montreal, Canada). He has extensive leadership experience in a variety of organizations, has led modernization efforts on machine learning, data sharing, develop research programs that impact national policy, and pioneered partnerships between public and private sectors.   Outside of work, Chantz enjoys volunteering to support sports and community organizations, spending time outdoors, and trying new things.

Charles Zedlewski is the Chief Product Officer at Together AI, the AI Native Cloud. He helps exceptional technologists build companies and products that realize their vision. As an operating executive, Charles has partnered with founders early on to build and scale two young, low/no-revenue open-source platform companies into $5B+ category leaders- Cloudera and Temporal.

Charlie Kawwas is President of the Semiconductor Solutions Group at Broadcom, responsible for the company’s 15 Semiconductor Divisions and the Brocade Storage Networking Division. In addition, Dr. Kawwas leads Global Operations and Intellectual Property for all Broadcom. Prior to this role, Dr. Kawwas served as Chief Operating Officer from December 2020 during which he drove the Global Operations, Sales and Corporate Marketing. He was also the Senior Vice President and Chief Sales Officer at Broadcom from May 2014. During his tenure at Broadcom, Dr. Kawwas oversaw the strategic growth of the hardware businesses from $4B to $30B+, driving strategic technology investments and operational excellence.

Chase's leadership experience at Crusoe has included building a high functioning team of more than 1,400 professionals encompassing engineering, technology, business development, manufacturing, finance, law, operations and administration. Chase has also held key roles in more than $1 billion of fundraising with venture, project and credit capital investors for Crusoe.Prior to founding Crusoe, Chase was a quantitative researcher and trader at Jump Trading and GETCO, where he developed and managed a portfolio of algorithmic trading strategies. Chase holds undergraduate degrees in math and physics from MIT and a masters degree in computer science from Stanford, where he specialized in artificial intelligence. He is currently on the board of CSforAll, an organization whose mission is to make high-quality computer science an integral part of the educational experience of all K-12 students and teachers. As a personal interest, Chase has climbed five of the “seven summits” including Mt. Everest.

Chetan Puttagunta is a General Partner at Benchmark. Benchmark is an early-stage venture capital firm focused on partnering with entrepreneurs building transformational companies. He currently serves on the boards of Elastic (NYSE- ESTC), Legora, LangChain, Starcloud, Reducto, Levelpath, Numeral, Duffel, Fomo, Modern Treasury, Airbyte, Summation, MindsDB, and Bezi. He also invested in Cursor. He previously led investments in and served on the boards of Manus (acquired by Meta), MuleSoft (NYSE- MULE, acquired by Salesforce), MongoDB (NASDAQ- MDB), Acquia (acquired by Vista Equity), Scout (acquired by Workday), and several others. Chetan has been featured on The Forbes Midas List, a ranking of the world’s top venture capital investors, for six straight years. Chetan holds a BS in Electrical Engineering from Stanford University.

Chirantan “CJ” Desai is the President and Chief Executive Officer of MongoDB, also serving on the company’s Board of Directors. Under his leadership, MongoDB is advancing its position as the default modern database platform powering AI, cloud, and real-time applications.CJ brings more than 25 years of experience leading global product and engineering organizations across cloud infrastructure, enterprise software, and cybersecurity. Through his experiences, he’s developed a deep understanding of growth at scale for both technology innovation and GTM execution. Before joining MongoDB, he served as President of Product and Engineering at Cloudflare and as President and Chief Operating Officer at ServiceNow, where he helped grow revenue organically from $1.5 billion to more than $10 billion. CJ previously held senior roles at EMC and Symantec and started his technology career at Oracle. He also serves on the Board of Directors at Cribl.CJ earned a Master’s in Computer Science and an MBA from the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign, where he is a recipient of the Distinguished Alumni Achievement Award.

Chris Clark is COO, co-founder and board director at OpenRouter, where he works on product strategy, business development & partnerships, finance, and marketing.Prior to OpenRouter, Chris was founder, board member, and CTO at Grove Collaborative (NYSE- $GROV), a leading sustainable consumer goods ecommerce company. Chris led Grove through major milestones from an early-stage venture-backed startup through IPO on the New York Stock Exchange in 2022.He lives in Charleston, SC with his wife and children.

Chris Wigley is Chief Operating Officer at PhysicsX, where he oversees global growth as the company scales its AI-native engineering platform across advanced industries.Chris brings deep experience at the intersection of frontier technology, data, and large-scale systems. He is the former CEO of Genomics England, bringing cutting-edge genomics research into routine clinical care for healthcare diagnostics and biotech research. During the pandemic, Chris was the national lead for the COVID data platform at NHSX and oversaw a major research program into the genomic drivers of COVID severity.Previously, Chris served as Chief Operating Officer at QuantumBlack, an advanced analytics and AI business, and as a Partner at McKinsey & Company. Earlier in his career, Chris worked for the UK Foreign Office in counter terrorism analytics in Afghanistan and Pakistan post 9/11.He lives in London with his wife Tara, three teenagers, and Molly the dog.

Claire Lebarz is Malt CTO for more than 3 years. She previously headed one of Airbnb's three data divisions, overseeing the traveler experience.Claire spent over 10 years in San Francisco building data-driven products at high-growth startups, and has advised dozens of founders and product teams on data strategy and machine learning.

Claire Thomas-Daoulas is a Partner at Brunswick Group, where she advises companies operating at the intersection of technology, regulation, politics and public trust. With experience across Europe and Silicon Valley, she works with businesses navigating fast-moving policy environments, including debates around digital infrastructure, AI, sovereignty and governance. Claire helps companies engage with governments and stakeholders to develop practical, mutually beneficial frameworks that support innovation while responding to public policy priorities.

Clement Andre leads the EMEA Digital Natives segment at OpenAI, where he works with a team supporting some of Europe’s most ambitious technology companies in adopting and scaling frontier AI. His work focuses on how AI is re-shaping products, teams, and business models, and how organizations can move from experimentation to real-world impact. Before OpenAI, Clement held leadership roles at Vanta, Salesforce, Slack, and LinkedIn.

Corinne Riley is a partner at Greylock, where she leads early-stage investments across AI infrastructure and applications.She has backed companies at the earliest stages that have since become category-defining businesses, including Baseten, Braintrust, Cogent, Fable, and Resolve AI. More recently, she led Greylock’s investment in Altara, along with several companies still in stealth.Corinne also built Greylock Edge, the firm’s three-month company-building program for founders working from pre-idea through early product-market fit, with flexible financing and hands-on company formation support. Since launching in October 2023, Edge has helped create seven companies, including Cogent Security, Fable Security, Highstock, and SuperMe.

Cory is the Chief Product and Technology Officer at NexGen Cloud, the company behind Hyperstack - one of Europe's fastest-growing AI cloud platforms. He leads product and technical operations, business processes and strategic direction, with over 20 years of experience in the IT industry spanning hands-on engineering through to full-time CTO roles.Cory has a proven track record of delivering complex technology programmes, collaborating with stakeholders, establishing strategic direction and building organisational capacity. At NexGen Cloud, he ensures Hyperstack is built for scale, security and innovation - advancing AI-ready cloud infrastructure that enables organisations to deploy with confidence.NexGen Cloud, founded in 2020, delivers sustainable AI infrastructure through Hyperstack, its GPU cloud platform designed to make advanced technologies accessible, efficient, secure and scalable.

Damien Wattez has a strong 25-year background in the digital sector, including the creation of two start-ups.He is currently Chief AI & Innovation at SNCF Voyageurs, where he leads high-impact AI projects in the travel industry and explores AI-first design as well as future ways of working with agentic teams

Dan Lahav is the Co-Founder and CEO of Irregular, the first frontier AI security lab built to protect the world in an era of increasingly capable and autonomous AI systems. He holds graduate degrees in Computer Science and Bioinformatics from Tel Aviv University, where he also lectures and has been recognized with the Outstanding Lecturer Award three times. His academic work includes collaborations with the Allen Institute for Artificial Intelligence (AI2) and research that appeared on the cover of Nature.

Dan Wright is an entrepreneur with deep experience driving growth at disruptive tech companies. As Co-Founder & CEO of Armada, he's focused on bridging the digital divide and bringing AI to the edge. Previously, Dan was CEO of DataRobot, named to CNBC's Disruptor 50 and Forbes' Cloud 100 list. He was also COO of AppDynamics, a leader in Gartner’s Magic Quadrant for seven years, and acquired by Cisco for $3.7 billion. Dan is an investor and advisor to startups like Abnormal Security, People.ai, and Quantum Metric, and a Co-Founder of the Jordan Zapper Memorial Scholarship.

Daniel Dines is Founder, Chief Executive Officer, and Executive Chairman of UiPath (NYSE- PATH), a leading enterprise automation and AI software company. Dines has also served as Chief Innovation Officer of the company. Dines started UiPath in 2005 with the goal of building a company that would help humans reduce the time and stress that result from menial, repetitive tasks. UiPath is building on its foundation as the world’s leading automation platform to become the leader in agentic automation by developing AI technology that mirrors human intelligence with ever-increasing sophistication, transforming how businesses operate, innovate, and compete. With a focus on security, accuracy, and resiliency, UiPath is committed to shaping a world where AI enhances human potential and revolutionizes industries.

Dan Thomas leads Third Bridge’s product strategy, focusing on making premium primary research accessible, intelligent, and integrated into client workflows. Since joining in May 2017, Dan has been instrumental in driving Third Bridge's evolution from a traditional expert network into a platform-agnostic intelligence layer based on a simple premise- rather than forcing investment professionals to seek out data, the data should come to them.

job_title: SVP of Growth, Strategy and Innovation
bio: Darrell Etherington is co-founder of AI-focused newsletter The Angle,  andSVP of Growth, Strategy and Innovation at SBS Comms, a technology-focused communications agency, where he leads a portfolio of roughly 40 clients spanning AI, fintech, clean energy, developer tools, enterprise software, and defence and space. His accounts include some of the most closely watched companies in tech, among them Cursor, Ramp, Vanta, and Astranis, and his work centres on the practice of comms engineering - building AI-powered tooling and repeatable systems that change how earned media actually gets made. Before moving to the agency side, Darrell spent 12 years as an editor at TechCrunch, where he covered AI, deep tech, semiconductors, gene editing, and enterprise technology, and developed a reputation for translating hard technical stories into plain terms. He is based in Toronto.

Dave has over 25 years of experience in technology, innovation and strategic business transformation. He joined Pearson from Accenture where he served as a Senior Managing Director. Dave helped to found and has served on several technology and industry boards including the Linux Hyperledger Foundation, Linux Open Wallet Foundation, Digital Dollar Project and the Global Business Blockchain Council. Dave earned a Master’s degree in Higher Education Administration from the University of Michigan and a degree in Psychology from the University of Pennsylvania.

For nearly three decades, David Bitton has built a career at the intersection of compute-intensive innovation and the infrastructure that powers it.He began his career in 1996 supporting visual effects productions and went on to co- found three media and entertainment companies, including 3vis, which grew intoCanada’s largest Autodesk M&E partner before its acquisition by Scalar in 2014. At Scalar and later Hypertec, David helped scale industry-focused business units, leading strategic growth initiatives before transitioning into product strategy at Hypertec Cloud. That journey — from render farms to AI factories — proved to be a natural precursor to the infrastructure revolution now underway. In April 2025, Hypertec Cloud spun out from Hypertec and, through the acquisition of 5C Data Centers, launched 5C Group—a dedicated AI infrastructure company bringing together cloud, data center, and infrastructure expertise under a single brand. Today, as VP of AI & Product Strategy,  David helps shape the company’s vision, product strategy, and market positioning as it designs, builds, and operates next-generation AI campuses spanning powerinfrastructure, advanced liquid cooling, modular data center architecture, and high- density compute environments.

David leads corporate development, mergers and acquisitions, capital markets, and legal functions, bringing a strong strategic lens to how we scale and invest in the next phase of our growth.David brings deep expertise in capital allocation, complex structuring, and global scaling. He began his career as a corporate lawyer at a leading New York firm before moving into senior investment roles at large global pension funds.Most recently, he held a private markets leadership role at AustralianSuper, following earlier roles at OMERS. His hands-on experience across infrastructure and energy including serving as a board director of a major electricity network, brings a practical understanding of the unique economics, power requirements, and scaling dynamics that underpin data center development.

Hammerspace co-founder and Chief Executive Officer David Flynn is a recognized leader in IT innovation who has been architecting disruptive computing platforms since his early work in supercomputing and Linux systems.David pioneered the use of flash for enterprise application acceleration as founder and former CEO of Fusion-io, which was acquired by SanDisk in 2014. He served as Fusion-io President and CEO until May 2013 and board member until July 2013. As Fusion-io CEO, David was responsible for the company’s strategic direction, oversaw all operations and was the visionary behind the company’s innovative technology and market strategy. Preceding his position as CEO, David was Fusion-io technical found and CTO, where he created the company’s foundational research and development efforts, as well as the development of short- and long-term technological roadmaps.Previously, David served as Chief-Architect at Linux Networx where he was instrumental in the creation of the OpenFabrics stack and designed several of the world’s largest supercomputers leveraging Linux clustering, InfiniBand, RDMA-based technologies. Also, David served as Project BlackDog’s Chief Scientist and Vice President of Engineering. He has also held positions Network Computer Inc and Liberate Technologies, a spin-off of Oracle Corporation, developing thin-client solutions.David holds more than 100 patents in areas across web browser technologies, mobile device management, network switching and protocols to distributed storage systems. He earned a bachelor’s degree in computer science at Brigham Young University and serves on boards for several organizations and startup companies

Dean Leitersdorf is the 27-year-old Co-Founder and CEO of Decart, an efficiency-first AI video research lab developing the world’s fastest generative video and world-modeling technology. He earned his PhD from the Technion at 23- then the youngest in the university’s history, until his brother Orian later broke the record. Decart has raised $153 million from leading investors including Sequoia Capital, Benchmark, and Zeev Ventures.

Debanjan Saha is the Chief Executive Officer of DataRobot, where he oversees the expansion of the DataRobot Agent Workforce Platform and drives continued innovation for the company's customers globally. Under his leadership, DataRobot has emerged as one of the largest independent AI ISVs, delivering substantial enterprise-scale revenue as the leading platform for building, operating, and governing AI applications.Debanjan is a visionary technologist with 20 years of product, engineering, and operational leadership experience at some of the most successful technology companies in the world, including Google, Amazon Web Services (AWS), and IBM. He is also co-author of over 50 patent applications, serves as a Fellow of the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE), and is a Distinguished Member of the Association for Computing Machinery (ACM).

Dena Almansoori is ADNOC’s Group Chief Technology & Innovation Officer. She leads ADNOC’s technology and innovation agenda, applying AI, robotics, data, IoT, and advanced analytics to build operational resilience, accelerate energy efficiency, elevate safety, and advance ADNOC’s mission to deliver more sustainable, smarter, future ready energy systems. Dena was previously Group Chief AI & Data Officer at e& where she led AI and data across 38 countries. She is the Founder of WhiteBox HR, an award-winning AI tech company.

Denis Skrinnikoff has been the Chief Technology Officer of IREN since October 2021. Mr. Skrinnikoff is a technologist with more than 20 years’ experience in the cloud and data center service provider space. Mr. Skrinnikoff has an extensive networking background, as well as experience operating and architecting a national fiber network, with a specialty in enterprise architecture. He has been involved in the design, construction and management of tier 3 data centers, acquisitions and mergers, and managing business units in a publicly traded company.

Des Traynor is Co-founder and Chief Strategy Officer of Fin, sitting on the company's Board of Directors. He has led many teams within Fin — including Product, Marketing, Customer Support, and Content — and today oversees Intercom's R&D team in the Dublin and London offices.Before Fin, Des was a UX consultant, a university lecturer in computer science, and a PhD researcher studying how to improve the quality of computer science education. He also co-founded Exceptional, a SaaS developer tools company acquired by Rackspace, with Eoghan McCabe.Des is an author and speaker on building startups, scaling businesses, and developing product strategy. He also invests in startups including Stripe, Miro, Notion, and Hopin.
Dillon Rolnick is the CEO of Nous Research

Dimitris Bountolos is the Chief Information and Innovation Officer at Ferrovial, a prominent global infrastructure group. With an extensive background spanning over 20 years, he is an expert in leading comprehensive change management and transformation programs across various industries.As an innovative entrepreneur, Dimitris has founded and partnered in several start-ups in the cutting-edge technology sectors, including ventures in space tourism, satellite micro-launchers, augmented reality, and drones.In the airline industry, his leadership roles have included Vice President of Customer Experience and Madrid Airport Director at Iberia Airlines, and Chief Digital Officer at Latam Airlines. Dimitris is widely recognized for his contributions to innovation, digital transformation, and sustainability within organizations. He has served as an advisor to NASA's Chief Innovation Officer for several years and as a Global Advisor to the strategic consultancy firm McKinsey, specializing in travel, transport, and logistics.Dimitris earned his master’s degree in civil engineering from the University of Granada and has graduated from elite senior management programs at Harvard, MIT, Stanford, IESE, and ESADE.He is a member of several advisory boards, bringing a broad, interdisciplinary, and cross-sectoral perspective to his work, including the Bankinter Foundation,  and as President of the Advisory Board of CIONET, and a member of the CIO Council of the Wall Street Journal, among others.Dimitris has a proven track record in developing and implementing high-impact projects, capturing new business opportunities, and introducing new business models across many sectors

Dmitry Panenkov is the founder of emma, a cutting-edge technology company based in Luxembourg. With over two decades of experience in the tech industry, Dmitry has held leadership roles at some of the most influential companies, including Arista Networks, Juniper Networks, and Allot.In 2019, Dmitry set out to reimagine the cloud computing landscape. His vision? A world where freedom of choice, seamless interoperability, and unrivaled efficiency are the norm—where organizations aren’t bound by a handful of major providers. That vision became emma.At emma, Dmitry has brought together an extraordinary team of experts to build an end-to-end platform that’s transforming how businesses manage their cloud environments. By leveraging a cloud-agnostic approach and AI-driven capabilities, emma is unlocking new possibilities and redefining the future of cloud computing.Under Dmitry’s leadership, emma has attracted significant investment from top-tier tech investors, driving the company’s mission to break down barriers in the cloud and set a new industry standard.

Don Barnetson is Senior Vice President of Product at Credo where he leads productdefinition and strategy across the company’s portfolio of high-speed connectivitysolutions. In this role, he partners closely with customers to align Credo’s solutions withrealworld deployment challenges and the rapidly evolving demands of AI infrastructure.Prior to this, Don served as Vice President of Product, AECs where he was instrumentalin advancing the company’s active electrical cables (AECs) to its market-leadingposition. He joined Credo as Senior Director of Product. Prior to Credo, Don was ChiefTechnology Officer at Lunera, an IoT platform company. Don received his BSEE fromthe University of Calgary and his MBA from UC Berkeley. He holds 26 US patents.

Doris Birkhofer, a Franco-German and committed European leader, has been President of Siemens France since October 1, 2021, and CEO of Siemens Smart Infrastructure France. Her geographical scope for both roles also covers the Benelux region and the Maghreb.A graduate of ESB Business School in Reutlingen and NEOMA Business School in Reims, she built her career in technology companies in Germany, the United States, and then France, where she has resided since 2009. She has held key operational and strategic roles at Saint-Gobain, Alcoa/Arconic, and Siemens, and has consistently championed initiatives to enhance the competitiveness of industry and infrastructure through technological innovation, digitalization, and the energy transition.Committed to strengthening Franco-German economic ties, she is a member of the Supervisory Board of the Franco-German bank Oddo BHF, a member of the Strategic Advisory Board of DEKRA e.V., and President of the Chambre franco allemande de Commerce et d'industrie (CFACI).Convinced that diversity is an asset and a driver of performance, Doris Birkhofer has long been involved in promoting gender diversity in scientific and industrial professions. She chairs the Siemens France Foundation, sits on the board of directors of Femmes@Numérique, and actively supports associations such as Elles Bougent and Femmes Ingénieurs. She is also a member of the strategic council of Paris Université Cité and has joined the Board of Directors of NEOMA Business School as an independent director.Doris Birkhofer is a Knight of the Legion of Honour.

Dr. Chaouki Kasmi is the Chief Innovation Officer at the Technology Innovation Institute (TII), is a cutting-edge UAE-based scientific research center. In his role, Dr. Kasmi spearheads the development and implementation of productization and industrialization strategies while overseeing the innovation pipeline, and forges key partnerships to harness new technologies and ideas.Previously Chief Researcher of the Directed Energy Research Center, Dr. Kasmi was responsible for building advanced research capabilities in the domains of high energy physics, electromagnetic technologies, innovative radar and sensing systems, laser technologies, and acoustic devices – from fundamental physics and theoretical problems to experimental physics. Under his leadership, the center has delivered strategic technologies to partners and clients.Specializing in unintentional and intentional electromagnetic interference, detection systems, device testing, statistical electromagnetics, computational electromagnetics, and signal processing, Dr. Kasmi has co-authored more than 200 scientific papers. He also is an Associate Electromagnetics Scientist and Researcher at the Faculty of Electrical Engineering at the Helmut Schmidt University / University of the German Federal Armed Forces. As part of his international scientific activities, he is Vice President for the Commission E – French Section and Vice Chair of the International Commission E of the International Union of Radio Science (URSI).Prior to joining TII, Dr. Kasmi worked as Director of the Mobile and Telecommunication Lab at DarkMatter, a cyber-security leader based in the UAE. He built DarkMatter’s entire telecommunication security function from the ground up. Before that, he was Deputy Head of the Wireless Security Lab at the French National Cybersecurity Agency (ANSSI). His earlier positions with the Agency included Head of Research and Electromagnetic Security Researcher. Among his responsibilities were research coordination, project advisory services, and team management.Dr. Kasmi has been recognized as a High-Power Electromagnetics (HPEM) Life Fellow by the SUMMA Foundation and has received two Young Scientist Awards from URSI. He also features in the Samsung and Sony Hall of Fame for reporting security flaws in smart TVs and smartphones.Dr. Kasmi holds a PhD in Electronics from Pierre and Marie Curie University, a Master of Science in Engineering in Embedded Systems from Grandes Ecoles d’Ingénieurs (ESEO), and an MSc in Microelectronics from the Institut National des Sciences Appliquées (INSA) de Rennes, all based in France

Dr. Vijay Balasubramaniyan is the CEO and Co-Founder of Pindrop, an AI security company focused on fraud prevention and deepfake detection across voice and video interactions. Pindrop was founded in 2011 based on Dr. Balasubramaniyan’s PhD research. Under his leadership, Pindrop has grown to serve some of the world’s largest banks, insurers, retailers, and healthcare providers, helping secure billions of critical interactions. Dr. Balasubramaniyan is based in Atlanta, where Pindrop is headquartered.

Dr. Vijay Shilpiekandula is an inventor-operator turned investor with 11 years of experience applying a systems-builder's lens to fundamental equity investing. In a prior decade, he was a researcher and operator, inventing and implementing hardware and software systems to tackle real-world constraints across compute, algorithms, and power. He received 14 patents for that body of work. He holds an Erdős number of 4 for designing and deploying parallelizable model predictive control algorithms for real-time factory automation. He is an active contributing member of the Value Investors Club, where he drew on his systems co-design background and was early to flag the memory wall and the optical-interconnect bottleneck as gating factors in scaling intelligence. He won the 2024 Sohn Idea Contest after a blind review process by a panel of investment industry leaders. In his 2024 presentation at the NYC Sohn Conference, he was one of the early voices in identifying the memory bottleneck and its link to lithography as a constraint in the AI infrastructure buildout. Prior to the January 2025 market selloff driven by DeepSeek fears, he pointed out that model distillation and open-source replication would be defining developments for the industry. He argued that generative AI differs from previous platform shifts such as the internet or cloud buildouts. This is because of his early variant view that hardware and physical infrastructure should become critical differentiators and accrue substantial value. Vijay holds a PhD in Mechanical Engineering from MIT, focused on mechatronics and systems co-design for nanometer-precision positioning, an MBA from Harvard Business School, and a B.Tech from IIT Madras. He resides in New York with his family, where, when not running in Central Park, he enjoys building fun science projects with his young children.

job_title: Managing Director EMEA & CEO Pro Design
bio: Since launching in 2013, Canva has grown to more than 265 million monthly usersworldwide and surpassed $4 billion in annualised revenue. From presentationsand videos to whiteboards and complex visual content, more than 40 billiondesigns have been created in Canva across nearly every country in the world.Based at Canva’s EMEA headquarters in London, Duncan serves as ManagingDirector, EMEA & Global Head of Pro Design, where he leads Canva’s businessacross the region while also overseeing the company’s professional creativeecosystem — including Affinity and other advanced design tools built forprofessional designers.Duncan also co-founded Flourish, the data storytelling platform acquired byCanva in 2022, where he continues to serve as CEO. Flourish powers billions ofviews of charts and maps each year and plays a key role in Canva’s broaderprofessional data visualisation offering.Before founding Flourish, Duncan was a data journalist at The Guardian,specialising in data visualisation and interactive storytelling. He also co-authoredThe Burning Question, a data-driven exploration of climate change described by AlGore as “fascinating, important, and highly recommended.”

job_title: Head of Product Marketing, Token Factory
bio: Dylan leads Product Marketing for Nebius Token Factory, helping bring high-performance AI infrastructure and open-source models to developers and enterprises worldwide. A Franco-Irish technical product marketer and builder, he combines hands-on knowledge of LLMs, inference, and developer tools with sharp go-to-market instincts. Passionate about technology, storytelling, and community, Dylan turns complex products into clear narratives, useful demos, and adoption in the real world.

Dylan is the Founder, CEO, and Chief Analyst of SemiAnalysis – the preeminent authority on all things AI and semiconductors. Through Dylan’s unwavering commitment to excellence, he has built the firm from the ground up as the recognized authority on the semiconductor supply chain to the cloud ecosystem, machine learning models, and all things in between. Since 2020, SemiAnalysis has transformed its business from a solo venture into a cohesive and focused team to provide breaking news and in-depth analysis for the most strategic, complex, and escalating challenges in the semiconductor industry.

Ed Huan, Co-founder & CTO at  PingCAP, the company behind  TiDB.Entrepreneur and engineer passionate about building scalable, cloud-native systems and databases.A true full-stack engineer with a hacker’s mindset and a builder’s heart.Focused on creating technology that powers the next generation of distributed systems.Building distributed systems and databases with ❤️Building companies with the principles of distributed systems.

Edwin Chen is the founder and CEO of Surge AI, the leading human data company powering the leading frontier AI labs. Since founding the company in 2020, Chen has grown it to over $1 billion in annual revenue in 2024, making Surge AI one of the fastest-growing, successful, and critical companies of the AI era.
