Under Chen’s leadership, Surge AI has become a critical piece of AI infrastructure, recognized for its unmatched data quality and ambitious vision for AGI. Before founding Surge AI, Chen studied mathematics, computer science, and linguistics at MIT, and held roles at Google, Facebook, and Twitter.

Eiman Ebrahimi, Ph.D. is the Co-Founder and CEO of Protopia AI, a company helping enterprises run AI on sensitive data without compromising privacy. Previously, he spent 9 years as a Senior Research Scientist at NVIDIA, where he focused on large-scale data access and scalable AI infrastructure. Eiman holds a Ph.D. in Electrical and Computer Engineering from the University of Texas at Austin and has authored award-winning research in computer architecture and AI systems.

A seasoned innovator at the intersection of AI and software development, Eiso has spent over a decade transforming how engineers build and deploy technology as the founder and CEO of multiple companies. His entrepreneurial journey includes founding Athenian, which transformed engineering operations through data-driven insights, and source{d}, which broke new ground as the first to harness AI for code analysis. This experience would later shape his early ideas for technical and applied research at his current company, poolside. As CTO of poolside, Eiso has conviction in the belief that the best route to AGI is through software development; and he's helped raise $623 million in funding to accelerate the company's mission of transforming software development through AI.

Dogus Cubuk is co-founder and co-CEO of Periodic Labs, building LLM agents for physical sciences, with physical experiments in the loop. Previously, he founded the materials science team at Google Brain and led the chemistry and materials science team at DeepMind. Outside of physics, he worked on data augmentation, semi-supervised learning, and model scaling. Examples include RandAugment and AutoAugment, which had applications in classification, object detection, and point clouds at Waymo and Google Cloud; ReMixMatch and FixMatch, which improved semi-supervised learning baselines; and power-laws as observed in predictions under distribution shifts and in model scaling. During his PhD, Dogus used machine learning and simulations to study supercooled liquids, glasses, and quantum materials.

Mr. Elad Raz founded NextSilicon in 2018, marking his fifth venture in over two decades in the IT industry. Prior to this, he founded and served as the CTO and Board Chair of Integrity Project, a notable software development company acquired by Mellanox Technologies (now a part of NVIDIA) in 2014. Under his leadership, NextSilicon has secured $300M in funding and has grown to over 400 employees. Beyond his entrepreneurial success, Mr. Raz has been granted two patents and actively contributes his expertise to several private advisory boards.

Eléonore Crespo is the co-CEO and co-founder of Pigment, an AI-powered business planning and performance management platform. Trusted by global companies including Coca-Cola, Anthropic, Unilever, and ServiceNow, Pigment empowers organizations to make better, faster decisions in an ever-evolving landscape. Prior to Pigment, Eléonore was an investor at Index Ventures, and a financial analyst at Google.

Enrique is a partner at Bain Capital Ventures, focused on early-stage infrastructure software companies with a specialization in cybersecurity. Prior to joining BCV in 2014, Enrique was the president and CEO of Symantec (Nasdaq- SYMC) and brings more than 27 years of executive experience in technology and security to Bain Capital. Through his 19 years at the global leader for security, backup, and availability solutions, Enrique also served as the EVP of Worldwide Sales and Marketing and President of Consumer Products. Before Symantec, he was president and CEO of Brightmail, the leading anti-spam software company acquired by Symantec in 2004. In 2011, Enrique was appointed to President Barack Obama’s Management Advisory Board. Select security investments include Dream, Halcyon, Rubrik and Armis. Enrique was named 2004 Entrepreneur of the Year by Ernst & Young, and 2007 Corporate Executive of the Year by Hispanic Net.

Eric Simons is the creator of Bolt.New, the viral website and app builder that empowers anyone to build through a visual interface. He launched Bolt in October 2024 with a single tweet asking, what do you want to build? and has since scaled the company to over five million users. A serial entrepreneur, Eric is also the founder of StackBlitz, Thinkster, and ClassConnect.

Erik Bernhardsson is the Co-founder and CEO of Modal, the high-performance serverless cloud for AI teams. Previously, he was CTO at Better.com and spent nearly a decade at Spotify, where he led the teams behind its music recommendation and data infrastructure systems. He shares perspectives on engineering culture, scaling systems, and the future of cloud computing, bringing a unique combination of hands-on technical depth and executive leadership to the conversation on AI infrastructure.

Erwan Menard is the Senior Vice President of Product Management at Crusoe, where he leads the innovation and scaling of Crusoe Cloud to advance sustainable AI infrastructure solutions.Before joining Crusoe, Menard was the Director of Product Management for Cloud AI at Google Cloud. In this role, he led the outbound product management for the Vertex AI portfolio, which included the Vertex AI Platform, Search, and Agentspace application products. His team was pivotal in enabling customers to build advanced AI and GenAI-powered experiences and in shaping Google Cloud's AI partnerships strategy.Prior to his time at Google, Menard was the CEO of Elastifile, a software-defined storage company that was acquired by Google. The technology from Elastifile is now integral to the Google Cloud Filestore product line. He also played a key role in building Google Cloud Solution Engineering, developing programs for migration, application modernization, and data center transformation.Earlier in his career, Menard held executive positions at several global startups and had leadership roles at Alcatel and Hewlett-Packard, where he was the VP & GM of HP's worldwide telecom business unit.

Euro Beinat is Global Head of AI at Prosus and Visiting Faculty on AI Governance in the IDP program at Insead Business School. At Prosus, a leading global technology company focused on lifestyle ecommerce, he leads a team of AI professionals, providing strategic guidance and solutions to the companies in the Prosus portfolio. Before joining Prosus he was VP of Technologies at Zebra Corp., Chicago. Euro has invested in and created/led several technology start-ups and currently serves on the Board of Directors for three companies. He holds an MSc in Computer Science and a PhD in Economics, and was awarded an Honorary Professorship in Economics from the University of Stellenbosch, South Africa.

Eve Bodnia is Founder & Chief Executive Officer at Logical Intelligence.A mathematician and physicist, Bodnia earned a Ph.D. in quantum information and algebraic topology from the University of California, Santa Barbara.At UCSB, her thesis was under her chair advisor Dirk Bouwmeester, Jon McCammond, Ben Bazin and Michel Devoret, who shared the 2025 Nobel Prize in Physics for “the discovery of macroscopic quantum mechanical tunnelling and energy quantisation in an electric circuit.”She also worked on neuroscience and brain development at UCSB with collaboration of Google Quantum AI, which helped inspire her pursuit of AI reasoning that does not rely on the text-based models used in many LLMs.She also built expertise in topology, the study of geometric properties and spatial relations, which has proved essential in understanding how to handle large information sets of almost infinite size.Bodnia earned her undergraduate degree in physics from the University of California, Berkeley, where she studied with the physicists Robert Cahn and Daniel McKinsey. She considers Cahn, a global leader in elementary particle theory, to be her “true mentor.”Bodnia is the author or co-author of 22 academic papers on dark matter, quantum mechanics, and particle physics.

Farshad Farahbakhshian is Chief Strategy Officer at Fundamental, an AI company focused on enterprise decision-making. Before joining Fundamental, Farshad served as Global GenAI GTM Leader at AWS, where he worked closely with leading GenAI startups and developed a clear view of where current LLMs fall short — strong at reasoning, coding, and analysis, but limited when it comes to forecasting outcomes from structured data.That gap led him to Fundamental and NEXUS, its Large Tabular Model (LTM) built to generate predictions from the structured data at the core of enterprise operations. In early 2026, Fundamental emerged from stealth with $275M raised from tier-one investors, a deep partnership with AWS, and the launch of NEXUS.Farshad holds a master's degree in electrical and computer engineering and has worked across the full AI stack — from GPUs to large-scale model deployment.

Jeremy Fraenkel is a leader who possesses a rare combination of technical depth and commercial execution. Jeremy earned a graduate degree in Machine Learning from UC Berkeley, providing him with the rigorous technical grounding required to lead a frontier AI research lab. However, Jeremy is more than just a technologist; he brings a level of enterprise and financial literacy that is uncommon in the world of AI research. Having built a strong finance background through roles at JPMorgan and Bridgewater, Jeremy understands the mission-critical nature of the data Fundamental aims to transform.

Franck Greverie is Capgemini’s Chief Technology & Portfolio Officer.In this role, Franck-Leads the portfolio of offerings and product management, and orchestrates the Centers of Excellence to drive Capgemini’s growth by packaging, launching and enabling innovative service offeringsLeads the technology management and innovation, including the innovation labs, client innovation programs, and ventures.He also oversees 6 Business Lines worldwide- on data & AI (Insights & Data), Business Services, Digital Customer Experience, Application Development & Maintenance, Cloud & Custom Applications, and Package-Based Services.Franck has been a member of the Group Executive Board since 2020.Prior to this appointment, Franck served as the Cloud & Cybersecurity Leader of Capgemini. He played a pivotal role in developing these activities and positing Capgemini as a market leader.

Frank Hutter is the co-founder and CEO of Prior Labs, the company behind Tabular Foundation Models. He is also a PI at the ELLIS Institute Tübingen and Director of the ELLIS unit Freiburg, where he is a Full Professor of Machine Learning (currently on leave), and a three-time recipient of grants from the European Research Council (ERC). He holds a PhD from the University of British Columbia and is a Fellow of EurAI and ELLIS. Before founding Prior Labs, Frank pioneered the field of automated machine learning (AutoML), co-authoring the first AutoML book, the first AutoML MOOC, and the widely-used AutoML systems Auto-WEKA and Auto-sklearn, as well as organizing 20+ workshops on AutoML-related topics and co-founding the AutoML conference. In recent years his focus has shifted to tabular foundation models.

job_title: Senior Director of Data Acquisition, QA, and Platform
bio: Fred de Villamil is Senior Director of Data Acquisition, QA, and Platformat NIQ Digital Commerce, where he leads large-scale web data collectioninitiatives powering digital shelf intelligence. His work focuses onbuilding and operating systems that collect and process more than 10billion products daily to support data-driven decision-making.

Gabriel Hubert is the co-founder and CEO of Dust, a platform that helps fast-moving teams build and deploy AI agents connected to their company's knowledge and tools. Co-founded in 2023 with Stanislas Polu (formerly OpenAI) and backed by Sequoia, Dust is used by companies like Datadog, Vanta, Qonto, and Clay.Gabriel and Stan have been building together for over 20 years. They previously co-founded TOTEMS, acquired by Stripe in 2014, where Gabriel spent five years on geographic expansion and product. He then joined Alan as Chief Product Officer, leading a 60-person unit focused on health products for members and employers.Dust was born from a simple conviction- the biggest opportunity in AI isn't building models, but deploying them effectively inside real organizations. Since 2025, Gabriel has been a member of France's National AI and Digital Council, advising on AI policy in the public interest, and is generally enthusiastic about product topics and tsundoku.

Ganesh Venkat is the co-founder and CEO of DensityAI, which is a full-stack AI startup creating hyper-efficient AI solutions using innovative architectures.Prior to founding DensityAI, Ganesh started and scaled the AI Hardware group at Tesla and delivered the path-breaking Full Self-Driving (FSD) computer used in millions of Tesla vehicles and Optimus Robots. He also conceived of the novel Dojo AI supercomputer and led the team to create multitude of innovative technologies to deliver first of its kind fully liquid cooled AI compute Datacenters.Prior to Tesla, Ganesh was in various leadership roles at AMD for 15 years, where he delivered the first x86-64 processor Opteron-64,  Industry’s first dual-core x86 processors and pivotal Zen/K12 processor designs. He holds a master's degree in integrated electronics from IIT Delhi and a bachelor's in engineering from the University of Bombay.  Ganesh holds about 30 patents in the field and is always on the lookout for innovative ways to create high-performance products.

Gardar Björnsson Rova leads QuantumBlack, AI by McKinsey, across Northern Europe, helping organizations harness AI to drive business transformation and create sustainable competitive advantage. A pioneer in applied AI, he co-founded QuantumBlack in the Nordics and has led more than 20 AI-powered technology builds, spanning product strategy, software architecture, AI platforms, and enterprise transformation. Gardar has advised some of the world’s leading investors on AI strategy and the future of software, and works closely with global technology and services companies on designing next-generation AI agent products and platforms.

Gareth is Chief Product Officer at Auth0, where he leads global product strategy and innovation. With over 15 years in identity, data, and marketing tech, he’s passionate about building secure, scalable platforms for developers and enterprises. He previously led product at TransUnion (via Neustar), held leadership roles at LiveRamp, founded two startups, and started his career at Google and DoubleClick. Originally from the UK, Gareth now lives in the Bay Area with his wife and children.

Gautier Cloix started his career at JPMorgan before spending nearly a decade at Palantir, where he held senior leadership roles. He opened and scaled Palantir France, followed by sensitive deployments and first commercial customers. He later worked with Palantir CEO from New York on company-wide scaling.He is now CEO of H Company, world leader in Computer Use for Enterprise, the third generation of AI for Enterprise, moving beyond tools and first-wave agentic systems to AI that delivers measurable business outcomes and ROI, through state-of-the-art B2B computer-use systems embedded directly in operations, not just LLMs wired to APIs. H Company raised Europe’s largest seed round, backed by Accel, Eric Schmidt, AWS, Samsung, and others.Gautier is a frequent speaker at events such as the World Economic Forum (Davos), HEC Paris, Yale University or SciencePo, and advises multiple AI startups across the US and Europe.

George Kurian is chief executive officer at NetApp and a member of the Board of Directors. George joined the company in 2011, bringing his passion and relentless focus on execution to his leadership roles at NetApp. He was named CEO in June 2015.  Previously at NetApp, as executive vice president of Product Operations, George was responsible for the strategy and development of NetApp’s product and solutions portfolio. Before that, George was senior vice president of the Data ONTAP Group overseeing development of the Data ONTAP operating system.  George joined NetApp as senior vice president of the Storage Solutions Group, where he was responsible for the solutions portfolio and joint partner technology integration.  Prior to joining NetApp, George was vice president and general manager of the Application Networking and Switching Technology Group at Cisco Systems. George’s diverse background also includes the role of vice president at Akamai Technologies, management consulting at McKinsey & Company, and leading software engineering and product management teams at Oracle Corporation.  George is also an independent director on the Board of global health services company Cigna Corporation. He holds a Bachelor of Science degree in electrical engineering from Princeton University and an MBA from Stanford University.

George Nazi brings a wealth of international executive leadership experience to his role as CEO at the Saudi Company for Artificial Intelligence (SCAI). With a career spanning over three decades, he has been instrumental in driving transformative growth and operational excellence within the AI, TMT, and cloud industries.Before joining SCAI, George served as the Global Vice President of Telecom, Media & Entertainment, and Gaming industries at Google Cloud, where he spearheaded visionary strategies across four regions, developed high-performing teams from the ground-up, and expanded revenue. Prior to his tenure at Google, George held significant roles at Accenture, a Fortune Global 500 professional services company, where he served as Senior Managing Director for the Global Comms & Media Industry. There, George oversaw large professional services organizations. During his tenure, he also devised new solution offerings and developed targeted go-to-market strategies.George's career also includes senior executive positions at Alcatel-Lucent and British Telecom, where he led large-scale transformation programs, optimized operational efficiency, and managed complex portfolios of products and services. George holds bachelor’s and master’s degrees in electrical engineering where he specialized in AI and Artificial Neural Networks from the University of Tulsa, Oklahoma in the USA.

Gideon Mendels is CEO and co-founder of Comet, an AI observability and evaluation platform. A computer scientist and ML researcher by training, he studied language modeling and speech recognition at Columbia before joining Google to work on hate speech and deception detection. He previously co-founded GroupWize, where his team deployed over 50 NLP models across 15 languages at billions-of-messages scale. That firsthand experience with fragmented ML tooling led him to build Comet — a unified platform for tracing, evaluating, and improving AI applications from development through production.

Gil Feig is the co-founder and CTO of Merge, the leading provider of customer-facing integrations and agent tools for frontier LLMs, Fortune 500 organizations, and B2B SaaS companies. Previously, Gil was the Head of Engineering at Untapped and worked as a software engineer at Wealthfront and LinkedIn. A graduate of Columbia University, he now lives in San Francisco.

Giordano (Gio) Albertazzi has been Chief Executive Officer of Vertiv since January 1, 2023. His priority is to leverage innovation excellence and a high-performance culture to further solidify Vertiv’s position as a critical digital infrastructure industry leader with continuously enhanced operational and financial performance. Gio joined Emerson Network Power (now Vertiv) in 1998 and held leadership roles in manufacturing, marketing and product management, services, and sales. He was formerly Vertiv president of EMEA, president of Americas, and COO. Gio holds a bachelor’s degree in mechanical engineering from the Polytechnic University of Milan and a master’s degree in management from Stanford University.

Gleb Budman is a co-founder and the CEO of Backblaze (NASDAQ- BLZE).  Backblaze offers high-performance cloud object storage that accelerates AI workflows, powers data-heavy applications, streamlines media management, and protects critical data. The company enables over 500,000 customers to reach and serve hundreds of millions of end users in 175 countries around the world.

Gleb Budman is a co-founder and the CEO of Backblaze (NASDAQ- BLZE).  Backblaze offers high-performance cloud object storage that accelerates AI workflows, powers data-heavy applications, streamlines media management, and protects critical data. The company enables over 500,000 customers to reach and serve hundreds of millions of end users in 175 countries around the world.Prior to Backblaze, Mr. Budman served in various senior roles at SonicWall, Inc., MailFrontier, Inc. (acquired by SonicWall, Inc.) and Kendara, Inc. (acquired by Excite@Home, Inc.). Mr. Budman also previously founded two other startup companies. Mr. Budman earned a M.B.A. and B.S. in Mechanical Engineering from the University of California, Berkeley.

Gorkem Yurtseven is the co-founder and CTO of fal, the generative media company. Born out of a shared passion for AI and a desire to address the challenges in AI infrastructure. Operating at the forefront of AI Inference, particularly in generative media, fal empowers developers to create the next generation of creativity tools. Previously, he worked at Amazon and is a UPenn graduate.

As a Partner based in Lux’s New York City office, Grace invests in companies at the intersection of the physical world and software, LLM applications, and infrastructure software. Prior to joining Lux, Grace was a Principal at Canvas Ventures. Before that, she worked on the LP side at the Stanford Endowment and in Product at Handshake.She is a generalist at Lux, which has $7 billion under management, and has been a first check  and early partner to category-defining companies across AI, automation, biotech, compute, defense, energy, infrastructure, robotics, and beyond — including Together, Sakana, Modal, Runway, Cognition, and Physical Intelligence. Lux has created and co-founded more than 20 de novo companies through Lux Labs, and multiple Lux investments are publicly traded companies and major divisions acquired by Amazon, Cisco, Databricks, J&J, Meta, Palo Alto Networks, and more. She earned a Bachelors of Science and a Masters of Science in Management Science and Engineering from Stanford, where she was a Mayfield Fellow and served as Co-President of Stanford Women in Business. In addition, she is on the board of the Stanford Technology Ventures Program.Grace is originally from Connecticut, is fluent in French, has lived in Tokyo, and aspires to re-learn Japanese. She’s an avid runner and cyclist.

Grant Lee is the CEO and Co-Founder of Gamma, the AI-powered presentation and website design tool that has scaled to 100 million users and $100M in ARR with only 50 employees. A Stanford-trained engineer with a background in finance and operations, Grant spent much of his early career creating countless slide decks, a pain point that inspired him to found Gamma and reimagine how people share ideas. Before Gamma, he served as COO of ClearBrain (acquired by Amplitude) and CFO of Optimizely.

job_title: Head of Marketing, Business and Product Strategy Leader
bio: Greg Matson brings over 20 years of memory industry experience across multipletechnologies. Greg’s experience spans NAND, NOR, MRAM, and 3D XPoint for the mobile,embedded, and SSD markets. Prior to Greg’s role at Solidigm, he held leadershippositions at Intel, where he shaped business and product strategy for data center SSDs,creating the blueprint for their growth from less than $1B to over $4B. Greg also ledAvalanche Technology, Inc.’s marketing practice as their Vice President of Marketingand co-founded ConGo Technology, where he developed corporate and productstrategy for a mobile content start-up.  Greg received both his MBA and his Bachelor of Science from the University ofCalifornia, Davis

Grzegorz (Greg) Ombach, PhD, is a senior deep tech executive and board member recognised for scaling businesses and organisations through complex industrial transformations. He brings strategic clarity, operating judgment, and execution discipline to fast moving, complex environments. He serves as Senior Vice President at Airbus, where he sets direction and governance for advanced technology and innovation strategy, and for venture and portfolio oversight across next generation industrial platforms. His role spans strategic direction, portfolio level decision making, and collaboration across global ecosystems in civil, defence, and dual use domains. Alongside his executive responsibilities, he serves as Chair of AdvEn Industries and as a board member of ProLogium, Thread Innovations, and Acubed. His board work focuses on scale up governance, capital discipline, technology licensing, capital allocation, M&A oversight, and public market readiness. He also advises and invests in More.ai, supporting the scaling of physical AI capabilities for robotics and machine intelligence. Earlier in his career, he held senior leadership roles at Qualcomm, Siemens, and Dräxlmaier Group, building and scaling global technology driven businesses across automotive and consumer electronics.

job_title: Director, France Startups and Digital Natives
bio: Grégoire is Google Cloud’s Director, France Startups and Digital Natives, leading the company's French Go To Market strategy. In his role, he is ensuring an excellent customer experience for founders at all stages of maturity, collaborating closely with Venture Capital firms as Accelerators and Incubators. With 25 years of experience in Technology and Go To Market leadership roles, Grégoire has a proven track record in scaling fast-growing businesses. Outside of work, he is an avid ski touring enthusiast in the Alps.

Guillaume Princen leads Anthropic's sales efforts across Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. Prior to Anthropic, he served as CEO of Mooncard, a European expense management platform.Guillaume was Stripe's first employee outside the United States when he joined in 2014. During his seven years there, he built and scaled Stripe's European operations, opening 12 offices and establishing EMEA as a key growth driver for the company. His career began at McKinsey & Company, developing expertise in business strategy. Guillaume holds an MBA from Harvard Business School and master's degrees from Collège des Ingénieurs and CentraleSupélec.An active angel investor and advisor in the European technology ecosystem, Guillaume specializes in AI, fintech, B2B SaaS, and climatetech ventures, bringing 20+ years of experience across revenue, product, operations, and strategy to his advisory work with founders and investment funds throughout Europe and the United States.

Guillermo Rauch is the founder and CEO of Vercel, where he leads the company’s mission to enable the world to ship the best products. Originally from Argentina, Guillermo became a self-taught developer at the age of ten, and has been a passionate contributor to the open-source community ever since. He is the designer and co-author of Next.js, the world's most popular React framework and is also the creator of socket.io and Mongoose.js. Prior to founding Vercel, Guillermo co-founded LearnBoost and Cloudup, where he served as CTO through its acquisition by Automattic in 2013. You can find him on X engaging with the developer community.

Guy-Laurent Arpino is Chief Information Officer at Louis Dreyfus Company (LDC), a leading global merchant and processor of agricultural goods with net sales amounting to US$50.6 billion in 2024.Guy-Laurent leads the Group’s overall Digital & AI strategy across all business platforms, functions and regions, supporting LDC’s strategic goals and ambitions. He joined the Group in 2016 from Bacardi-Martini Ltd., where he was CIO for Europe since 2011, with additional responsibility for global business intelligence and digital marketing services. Prior to this, he worked at Procter & Gamble for 15 years, most recently as Global IT & Shared Services Associate Director for the Braun Division. He holds an Engineering degree in Electronics and Signal Processing from “Ecole de Chimie, Physique, Electronique” (CPE Lyon, France).Guy-Laurent is also on the Board of Directors of Covantis, a joint venture between LDC and industry peers, providing a global digital platform supporting agricultural post-trade operations.

Hanel Baveja is a Partner at Creandum, an early stage venture firm that has backed companies like Lovable, Modal, Black Forest Labs, Spotify, and Klarna. She was previously an investor at Insight Partners and Union Square Ventures in NYC before moving to London four years ago to help Creandum establish its London office.

Haseeb Budhani is the CEO and co-founder of Rafay Systems. He previously co-founded and led Soha Systems, which was acquired by Akamai Technologies, where he later served as Vice President of Enterprise Strategy. Haseeb has also held executive and leadership roles at Infineta Systems, NET, and several other technology companies. He holds an MBA from UC Berkeley’s Haas School of Business and a B.S. in Computer Science from the University of Southern California.

job_title: Managing Director, Head of Venture Capital & Crossover Coverage, Investment Banking
bio: Helena Chao is a Managing Director, Head of Venture Capital & Crossover Coverage, Investment Banking at Bank of America, where she leads Crossover & Sustainability Investor Coverage. At the intersection of capital and innovation, she works with visionary founders and forward-thinking investors to accelerate breakthroughs in AI, deep tech, and beyond.

Hoshik Kim is Senior Vice President and Fellow at Memory SystemsResearch, SK hynix Inc., where he leads research and pathfinding in memorysystems architecture and software solution for data centers at scale as well as edge devices. His current research interests focus on next-generationmemory systems for AI and HPC, which include memory expansion, tiering,pooling and sharing as well as computational memory/storage solutions suchas PIM/PNM, custom HBM, etc. He is also interested in advanced packagingand emerging interconnect technologies for better compute and memoryintegration.Prior to joining SK hynix, he worked for Intel and LG Electronics, where hegained broad experiences in architecture, design, verification and electronicdesign automation (EDA) for microprocessors, SoC’s and IP’s. Hoshikreceived B.S. from Yonsei University, Seoul, Korea and M.S. and Ph.D. fromUniversity of Southern California, all in Electrical Engineering. He is alsoserving on the Board of Directors for Semiconductor Research Corporation(SRC).

Iain Martin is a senior editor with Forbes and covers AI, startups, and edits the Midas List ranking of the world’s best venture capital investors. Prior to joining Forbes, Iain worked for the journalism startup Storyful (acquired News Corp) as its Asia Editor from its Hong Kong bureau, and started his career as a financial journalist in London.

Ioannis Antonoglou is the co-founder and Chief Technology Officer of Reflection, where he leads the development of superintelligent coding agents that are transforming software development through robust code understanding. As a founding engineer at Google DeepMind, he was instrumental in creating some of the most revolutionary AI systems in history, serving as a core architect behind AlphaGo (the first AI to defeat a world champion Go player), AlphaZero (which mastered multiple games through pure self-play), and MuZero (which learned to plan in unknown environments). He also led RLHF training for Google's flagship LLM, Gemini. These breakthroughs fundamentally redefined the boundaries of artificial intelligence and demonstrated how AI could transcend human intuition through computational creativity.

Ion Stoica is a Professor in the EECS Department at the University of California, Berkeley, where he holds the Xu Bao Chancellor Chair and directs the Sky Computing Lab. He is also Executive Chairman of Databricks and Anyscale, and has co-founded several successful infrastructure companies including Conviva, Databricks, Anyscale, and Arena. Ion's research focuses on AI systems, distributed computing, and cloud infrastructure. He has helped lead the development of widely used open-source tools including Apache Spark, Ray, vLLM, SkyPilot, and Chatbot Arena.

Ishan Mukherjee is the Co-Founder and CEO of Rox Data Corp, a Sequoia-backed AI sales productivity platform. He previously served as Chief Growth Officer at New Relic, where he scaled the self-serve business from $0 to $100M ARR, and founded Pixie Labs (acquired by New Relic). Ishan has also held leadership roles at Apple, Amazon Robotics, and other high-growth technology companies.

Ivan Burazin is the co-founder and CEO of Daytona, one of the fastest-growing infrastructure companies of its generation. Daytona is building agent-native cloud infrastructure that enables AI agents to securely run, fork, and manage stateful runtime environments at scale. Backed by $31M, including a $24M Series A led by FirstMark Capital, Daytona powers millions of sandboxes per day for startups and Fortune 500 companies building autonomous AI systems. Previously, Ivan co-founded Codeanywhere, one of the first cloud IDEs (2009), and created Shift, Europe’s leading developer conference, acquired by Infobip in 2021. He later joined Infobip’s executive board as Chief Developer Experience Officer.

Jacob Wallenberg is the Vice President of International at Ramp, a financial operations platform designed to save companies time and money. Prior to this role, Jacob was the first hire on Ramp's Risk team and led the People & Talent function as the company scaled from 100 to 1,200 employees. Before joining Ramp, he worked in consulting at McKinsey and co-founded and worked at startups in New York. A University of Pennsylvania graduate, Jacob was born in Sweden and also serves on the board of EQT.

Jake Stauch is the co-founder and CEO of Serval, the AI-native automation platform that lets enterprise teams turn a single sentence into a published workflow. Serval has raised $127 million from Sequoia, Redpoint, and General Catalyst, reaching a $1 billion valuation in under 18 months.

Jimmy Day leads VAST Data's AI Alliances business across EMEA, driving strategic partnerships with customers, technology partners, cloud providers, and systems integrators to accelerate enterprise AI adoption across the region.With more than 20 years of experience building and scaling technology businesses, Jimmy has held leadership roles across VAST Data, NetApp, Nebulon (acquired by NVIDIA), SolidFire (acquired by NetApp) and Hewlett Packard Enterprise. He has successfully launched new markets, built high-performing teams, and delivered consistent revenue growth across UK, EMEA, and global organisations.

A physicist by training, James Krellenstein is the CEO and co-founder of Alva Energy and serves on its Board of Directors. He leads the company’s strategy and execution end-to-end—setting technical and commercial priorities, building and managing the team, and driving the partnerships and transactions that turn engineering into deployed projects. James oversees Alva’s fundraising and investor relationships, hiring and organizational development, engineering delivery and technical roadmap, and commercial deal flow across utilities, vendors, and strategic partners.James’s expertise spans nuclear reactor design analysis, nuclear regulation/licensing, and nuclear project delivery. He has studied the construction experience of 100+ nuclear power plants in the U.S. and globally, with a focus on how project cost and schedule outcomes are shaped by reactor technology, nuclear vendors, EPC execution, and utility project management structures. His peer-reviewed research at Alva has been published in Nuclear Engineering and Design, and he has contributed to reports published by the National Academy of Engineering, the U.S. Department of Energy, and the National Laboratories.Previously, James served on the White House’s Nuclear Deployment Task Force, advising the executive branch on U.S. nuclear energy policy and helped shape the U.S. Department of Energy’s advanced nuclear strategy. He has supported private-sector nuclear clients on reactor licensing, project financing, and nuclear fuel acquisition.

James Leaverton is a startup ecosystem leader, founder, investor, advisor, and operator with deepexperience helping early-stage technology companies scale. He currently leads Flamethrower,the startup program from Backblaze, focused on supporting high-growth AI, media, and data-intensive startups. Over the course of his career, James has worked across startups, cloudinfrastructure, partnerships, and go-to-market strategy, while mentoring and advising foundersacross the venture ecosystem. He is passionate about helping startups grow intelligently, avoidcostly infrastructure mistakes, and build sustainable businesses at scale.

Jan Oberhauser is the founder and CEO of n8n, a fair-code workflow automation platform that empowers users to connect and automate anything—from simple tasks to complex systems. With a background in software engineering and visual effects, Jan combines technical creativity with a strong belief in open, community-driven technology. Since founding n8n in 2019, he has led its growth into a global automation platform used by developers and enterprises worldwide. Based in Berlin, Jan is passionate about making powerful automation accessible to everyone.

Jane is an investment partner at CapitalG where she focuses on enterprise software. She brings over fifteen years of operating experience and a passion for company building to her role. Since joining CapitalG in 2024, Jane has led CapitalG investments in Clay, Kalshi, OpenRouter, and Pennylane.Jane has spent her career building and scaling go-to-market teams. Before joining CapitalG, Jane was the CMO of Carta where she led marketing, channel sales, and product-led-growth (PLG). Prior to that, she led sales teams at Salesforce and RelateIQ.Jane attended Stanford Business School and Harvard University.

Dr. Jaroslaw “Jarek” Kutylowski is the founder and CEO of DeepL, the Cologne-based AI product and research company building secure, intelligent solutions to complex business problems. Born in Poland and having spent a large part of his life in Germany, Jarek brings an international perspective and a lifelong passion for technology - a developer at heart, he began coding at just 10 years old, building tools he found useful in daily life. He holds a PhD in Computer Science with a focus on mathematics and has held roles at several tech companies prior to founding DeepL.Under his leadership, DeepL has grown rapidly, scaling Language AI platform to offer highly accurate, human-like translation in both written and spoken formats, as well as a contextual AI writing assistant. Today, over 200,000 business customers - and millions of individuals across 228 global markets - rely on DeepL for secure, seamless and effective communication. The company also has broadened its scope beyond language, with innovations including DeepL Agent, an autonomous AI assistant designed to transform the way businesses and knowledge workers get work done.
Jason Calacanis is a technology entrepreneur, fund manager, host of This Week in Startups podcast and co-host and executive producer of the All-In Podcast.

Jean-Michel GARCIA is an IT visionary leader and the Group CTO of BNP Paribas. With a Telecom engineering degree, he has carved its career path in the Banking industry.He started in equity derivatives with network design and support to then lead its infrastructure operations. His leadership skills earned him, in 2019, the role of Group CTO after leading key positions such as UK CTO and EMEA CIB CTO. His journey is a testament to his dedication, innovation, and ability to transform challenges into efficient and industrial successes.

Jeehoon Kang is the Chief Research Officer at FuriosaAI and a leading expert in high-performance computing systems. Prior to joining FuriosaAI, he was a professor at KAIST, where his research focused on parallel computing systems, compilers, and programming languages. He has received multiple Distinguished Paper Awards at premier academic conferences, including PLDI and POPL. At FuriosaAI, he leads the design and development of the company's next-generation compiler stack and software architecture, driving performance, efficiency, and programmability across Furiosa RNGD (pronounced “renegade”) and upcoming third-generation chip.

Jeff is Co-founder at VAST, with a specialization on product marketing, product management and alliance building. Jeff brings over two decades of experience with advanced computation and massively scalable big data and cloud storage technologies to VAST. Prior to joining VAST, Jeff held management roles at DDN, CTERA Networks, and Cluster File Systems. In five short years, Jeff graduated with a Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration from Trenton State College.

Jeff Tatarchuk is a pioneering entrepreneur and seasoned B2B growth strategist, currently serving as the Co-Founder and Chief Growth Officer at TensorWave. With a focus on democratizing AI compute, Jeff has been instrumental in positioning TensorWave at the forefront of the AI industry since its inception in December 2023. Jeff's diverse experience across various sectors highlights his versatility and adeptness at driving business growth and development in emerging markets.In addition to his role at TensorWave, Jeff has a background in entrepreneurship and investment. He has been a Startup Advisor and Angel Investor at Fruition Lab since March 2012, where he mentors and supports emerging startups. His keen eye for innovation and growth has led to several successful ventures.Jeff's notable past roles include serving as the Chief Business Development Officer at Lets Rolo, where he played a key role in the company's acquisition by LifeKey.A skilled public speaker and leader, he holds a strong presence in the Las Vegas Metropolitan Area and is well-regarded for his contributions to the tech and startup communities.

Jennifer Johnson is a London-based columnist for Breakingviews, where she covers European tech, media and telecoms. She was previously a reporter for the Investors’ Chronicle and she holds a master’s degree in financial journalism from City, University of London.

job_title: Head of Data center & Energy Infrastructure Research
bio: Jeremie Eliahou Ontiveros leads Datacenter & Energy Infrastructure research at SemiAnalysis. He specializes in tracking Hyperscaler and Neocloud infrastructure and large-scale AI clusters, cooling & electrical systems, and power sources. He connects systems-level architecture with real-world deployment to decode AI’s backend complexity. Previously, he focused on long-only investments in public equities, where he honed his expertise in deep data analysis and financial modeling.

Jim Fan is the co-lead of the Generalist Embodied Agent Group at NVIDIA. GEAR’s mission is to build generally capable physical AI. Jim's team is spearheading Isaac GR00T, NVIDIA’s moonshot initiative at building the AI brain for humanoid robotics. Previously, he obtained his Ph.D. at Stanford Vision Lab. His publications have won awards and nominations, including Outstanding Paper at NeurIPS 2022. He spearheaded multiple works on robot foundation models, proficient Minecraft players, and general-purpose agents that learn to operate a computer. Jim's works have been widely featured in news media such as the New York Times, Forbes, MIT Technology Review, TechCrunch, WIRED, and VentureBeat.

John is the Executive Director & GM for our CSP segment. This role has the extremely important task of ensuring we are meeting the unique needs of our Cloud Service Provider customers. Before taking on this role, John held multiple positions over his 20-year tenure within both IBM and now Lenovo.  In his last position, he was the Business Line Executive for Lenovo ISG Server and Option general purpose portfolio. Prior to joining IBM in 2005, John worked in both Technical Sales and Operations positions with a long track record of building effective teams and driving change for his customers.

Jonathan Corbin is the Founder and CEO of Maven AGI, where he’s redefining customer experience with enterprise-grade AI agents. With over two decades of leadership at Hubspot, Adobe, Marketo, and Sprinklr, Jonathan has built scalable customer success systems across industries. His vision for Maven was born from firsthand experience navigating the complexity of fragmented tools, siloed data, and the growing demand for personalized, efficient support. Now, he’s leveraging generative AI to solve those challenges at scale.

Jonathan is General Partner at Headline and leads the firm’s AI investment strategy. He launched Headline’s Paris office and has been instrumental in building one of Europe’s most active AI investment platforms. His investments include category-defining companies such as Mistral, Black Forest Labs, AMI, Fundamental, Attio, Swile, Homa, Vizzia, and Payflows, among others.

Joon Sung Park is the CEO and Co-founder of Simile, an AI company building human-grounded simulations that allow organizations to deeply understand the motivations, emotions, and behaviors of any group or individual.Spun out of his pioneering Ph.D. research at Stanford University on generative agents, Joon’s work has been featured on the TED AI stage and in top-tier publications including The New York Times, Science, Nature, Forbes, and Wired.Joon holds a Ph.D. in Computer Science from Stanford, where he was a Microsoft Research Ph.D. Fellow, alongside degrees from UIUC and Swarthmore College.

Jordan Nanos is a Member of Technical Staff at SemiAnalysis focused on Neoclouds. He leads ClusterMAX and related projects, bringing hands-on experience to our research on AI system performance. Previously, Jordan was a Distinguished Technologist at HPE, where he worked on the design and implementation of some of the largest supercomputers in the world. Jordan holds a Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering from Queen’s University.

Jordan Topoleski is the Chief Operating Officer at Cursor, where he oversees go-to-market and operations for one of the fastest-growing AI developer platforms. Cursor is used by over 60% of the Fortune 1000, partnering closely with CIOs and CTOs at leading global enterprises. He studied the history of technology at Harvard and previously built and exited a software development firm before co-founding companies in financial services and AI.

Josh co-founded Lux Capital to support scientists and entrepreneurs who pursue counter-conventional solutions to the most vexing puzzles of our time in order to lead us into a brighter future. The more ambitious the project, the better—like, say, creating matter from light.Josh is a Director at Aera Therapeutics, Cajal Neuroscience, Eikon Therapeutics, Impulse Labs, Kallyope, Osmo, Variant Bio, and helped lead the firm’s investments in Anduril, Echodyne, Planet, Hadrian, Osmo and Resilience. He is a founding investor and board member with Bill Gates in Kymeta, making cutting-edge antennas for high-speed global satellite and space communications. Josh is a Westinghouse semi-finalist and published scientist. He previously worked in investment banking at Salomon Smith Barney and in capital markets at Merrill Lynch. In 2008 Josh co-founded and funded Kurion, a contrarian bet in the unlikely business of using advanced robotics and state-of-the-art engineering and chemistry to clean up nuclear waste. It was an unmet, inevitable need with no solution in sight. The company was among the first responders to the Fukushima Daiichi disaster. In February 2016, Veolia acquired Kurion for nearly $400 million—34 times Lux’s total investment.Josh is a columnist with Forbes and Editor for the Forbes/Wolfe Emerging Tech Report. He has been invited to The White House and Capitol Hill to advise on nanotechnology and emerging technologies, and a lecturer at MIT, Harvard, Yale, Cornell, Columbia and NYU. He is a term member at The Council on Foreign Relations, a Trustee at the Santa Fe Institute, and Chairman of Coney Island Prep charter school, where he grew up in Brooklyn. He graduated from Cornell University with a B.S. in Economics and Finance.

Joshua Shirazi is a Partner at Alkeon Capital Management, leading investments in AI & Software. Alkeon Capital is a global investment firm that backs transformative companies across their lifecycle from the private to the public markets for over two decades. Prior to joining Alkeon, Joshua was a growth equity technology investor at Sageview Capital and an investment banker in Goldman Sachs’s TMT group. He received a B.S. in Business Administration from the University of California, Berkeley.

Julia Andre joined Index in 2019 to focus on B2B Saas and Fintech across Europe. She is especially interested in businesses that increase transaction efficiency and enable retail and business commerce to shift digitally. Prior to joining Index, Julia worked at Palantir, where she led commercial deployments and analytics for enterprise customers in multiple industries including Banks, Oil & Gas, Financial Services, and Supply Chain. Julia holds an MS in Engineering from Stanford University, where she led two years of mathematical research in Anomaly Detection, and an MS in Engineering from Ecole Nationale des Ponts et Chaussées.

Julien Bek is a partner at Sequoia Capital, and part of the early team. He works closely with a number of companies including Rillet, Dust, Magentic. He grew up in Switzerland, and later studied in the UK at the University of Cambridge and Warwick Business School. He first tried his hand as a founder before switching sides initially at GFC & Accel where he partnered with companies like Revolut, Miro, Attio, and Melio.

June Paik is the CEO of FuriosaAI, setting out a vision to build a new foundation for AI compute with the company's novel Tensor Contraction Processor architecture. FuriosaAI takes a full-stack approach, integrating hardware and software to improve performance, efficiency, and programmability for AI inference. Since co-founding the company in 2017, June has led the development of two generations of AI accelerators, including Furiosa RNGD (pronounced renegade) which is in production with global customers like LG AI Research and Samsung SDS.

Juras Juršėnas has spent nearly two decades building products and engineering teams that turn hard problems into scalable infrastructure. As COO at Oxylabs — a global leader in proxy networks and web data collection — he oversees the full product and engineering organization, from scraping infrastructure to AI-powered data products. He's spent his career addressing the kinds of challenges that don't have a playbook — and building the organizations that figure it out anyway.

Dr. Justin Dustzadeh is a senior executive with global experience in technology & management roles across software, networking, mobile, cloud, video and payment industries. He is known for his passion and ability to build world-class teams and bring new technologies to market with rapid execution from vision to results.Justin Dustzadeh is Equinix’s EVP & Chief Technology Officer. In this role, he drives the company’s technology vision, strategy and architecture as well as the overall technical innovation and focus on software transformation and developer community.Prior to Equinix, Mr. Dustzadeh served as Head of Global Network and Software Platform at Uber where he led the team responsible for the architecture, engineering and operations of Uber’s global network infrastructure, as well as software platforms enabling Uber’s business worldwide.Justin’s prior experience includes executive roles at Visa, Huawei, Ericsson, AT&T, a Silicon-Valley technology startup (where he was a founder), and two leading service providers in France.Justin is a sought-after keynote speaker and an industry-recognized thought leader on software-defined infrastructure and next-generation technologies. He has been advising startups, CEOs and the investment community on technology and market opportunities.Justin holds a Ph.D. in Computer Science and has graduated from two top schools in France.

Jyoti Bansal is a multi-unicorn founder and serial technology entrepreneur. He is the founder and CEO of Harness, the $5.5B AI Software Delivery Platform automating everything after code — testing, deployment, security, and compliance — for the largest enterprises in the world. Previously, he founded AppDynamics (acquired by Cisco for $3.7B), launched startup studio BIG Labs, and co-founded VC firm Unusual Ventures. He has been recognized as EY Entrepreneur of the Year, Forbes' Best Cloud Computing CEO, SiliconANGLE's Most Innovative Tech Startup Leader, and ranked No. 24 on Fortune's Most Innovative Companies list. He holds a BS in Computer Science from IIT Delhi and over 25 U.S. patents.

Kaoutar Sghiouer is  the Global Head of Data & AI at Sanofi, she is spearheading the company’s data democratization strategy and accelerating its ambition to become the first AI-powered biopharma at scale. Her work spans the full value chain, from drug discovery and clinical development to manufacturing and commercial operations, delivering measurable impact on innovation and performance.Beyond Sanofi, Kaoutar has played a key role in numerous international Data & AI initiatives, contributing to the development of advanced technologies and holding multiple patents in data processing and AI methodologies.She holds a PhD in Artificial Intelligence from Université de Technologie de Compiègne (UTC), an Executive Management Program certification from HEC Paris, Harvard University and IMD.Kaoutar is widely recognized for her pioneering contributions to digital health and for her leadership in applying AI at scale to improve patient outcomes, operational efficiency, and decision-making across the pharmaceutical industry.

Kareem Amin is the co-founder and CEO of Clay, the best way to turn any growth idea into reality.Kareem studied Electrical Engineering and Physics at McGill University where he briefly pursued graduate studies in neuroscience, building Brain-Machine Interfaces.In his free time, he likes to make ambient music, explore theory of mind, and experiment with different movement therapies.

Karen is a columnist at Reuters News' international commentary brand, Breakingviews, writing about global technology and venture capital sectors. She writes in-depth analysis about artificial intelligence, fintech and semiconductor companies, as well as deals in the Middle East region and mining sector. She is based in London.

Karim Jalbout is the founder of Konstellation Advisory, focusing on executive leadership, scaling, and AI go-to-market strategy. He partners with private equity and venture-backed founders and CEOs from many investor portfolios, including Cinven, Eurazeo, Apax, Ethos Capital, Soho Square, and Creandum.Karim previously served as Chief People Officer at Lilium, an electric mobility (eVTOL) company. He also spent 11 years at Egon Zehnder as a Partner and Lead for the private equity technology practice. His career began in marketing and commercial roles at Diageo and Sony Ericsson.As a moderator and podcast host, Karim has interviewed some of the world’s most influential leaders, including Michelle Obama and Amal Clooney, as well as founders and leaders from Signal, Hugging Face, Uber, and AMD. Additionally, he’s an active angel investor with a portfolio of 20+ early-stage companies.

Kartik Sheth is Chief AI Officer at Deutsche Telekom, where he leads the teams turning AI into products, services, and experiences at Telekom scale as well as bringing the right suite of AI tools in the hands of every Telekom employee. His work focuses on making AI visible and useful in the moments that matter — from smarter customer support and self-service to AI-powered consumer experiences such as Magenta AI and voice-based assistance.

job_title: Chief Information Officer for Cyber, Data, Risk and Resilience
bio: Katherine Wetmur is a Managing Director and the Chief Information Officer for Cyber,Data, Risk and Resilience at Morgan Stanley; in this role, she oversees global efforts tostrengthen cybersecurity, enhance data governance, and advance operationalresilience; she leads the Firm’s first-line defenses for cyber and fraud risk and isresponsible for the Firmwide Data Office, Non-Financial Risk Technology, and ourInternational Technology officesKatherine partners with business and technology leaders worldwide to address evolvingthreats, ensure regulatory compliance, and safeguard the Firm’s data integritySince joining Morgan Stanley in 2002, Katherine has held several senior technologyleadership roles, including International Chief Information Officer and Head of Reliability& Production Engineering for the Firm’s Institutional and Corporate businessesKatherine holds a B.S. in Materials Science from Rice UniversityKatherine co-chairs the Firm’s Pride and Ally Network; she was a founding member ofWomen in Technology (WIT) and is an active mentor, championing LGBTQ+ visibility,and expanding access to STEM education for underrepresented groupsBeyond the Firm, she serves on the Board of Sheltered Harbor, Analysis & ResilienceCenter (ARC) and SustainableIT.org.Her leadership has been recognized by Financial News’ Top 100 Women in Finance(2019, 2020) and Fast Company’s Queer50 list (2023, 2024)

Katrin Lehmann has been the Chief Information Officer (CIO) of Mercedes-Benz Group AG and Mercedes-Benz AG since April 2024, with a global responsibility for IT across all divisions, brands and markets.Previously, she was Vice President IT Sales/Marketing & Home of Tech at Mercedes-Benz AG. The focus of her work was on the digitalization of all sales processes, including IT-based marketing, sales and after-sales activities.She started her professional career in the SAP sphere in 2001. Since 2004, she has held various management positions in consulting as well as in solution and business development within the SAP Group. In 2015, she took on the role of Program Lead in Strategic Program Management and became the Global Chief Data Officer in 2017. Afterward, she was appointed as Senior VP of S/4 HANA Cloud Data Development. In her last position as Senior VP of Customer Innovation & Maintenance at the Product Engineering Group at SAP SE, she oversaw customer and industry-specific software development, software maintenance and innovation from 2020 to 2023.Katrin Lehmann holds a Master of Business Administration (MBA) from Mannheim Business School.

Kavitha leads Rubrik’s efforts to accelerate enterprise transformation and deepen executive engagement, with a focus on expanding Rubrik’s footprint across the Global 2000 and public sector decision-makers. She partners across the GTM organization, shaping Rubrik’s CXO narrative, championing value economics, and unlocking new revenue streams. Prior to Rubrik, Kavitha was EVP of Customer Experience & Transformation at Zscaler, where she built and scaled the company’s CXO and Transformation Practice. She holds a B.Eng. in Communication Engineering from the Royal Melbourne Institute of Technology, Australia, and an M.S. in Cybersecurity Risk and Strategy from NYU School of Law and Tandon School of Engineering.

Kayla Miele is a Partner at McKinsey & Company, where she advises technology companies, founders, and private equity investors on growth strategy, AI transformation, and value creation across the investment lifecycle. Based in London, she works extensively with high-growth technology businesses on scaling operations, launching new products, preparing for fundraising, IPOs, and exits, and translating AI into competitive advantage. With deep expertise across consumer technology, marketplaces, media, and private capital, Kayla helps organizations accelerate growth through technology-enabled transformation and strategic innovation.

Keiron Shepherd is a cybersecurity leader with more than 20 years of experience spanning technology vendors, systems integrators, distribution, and the UK Armed Forces. This diverse background gives him a broad perspective on the commercial, technical, and operational challenges organisations face when protecting their people, data, and infrastructure.Specialising in cybersecurity and artificial intelligence, Keiron helps organisations develop practical, resilient security strategies that address emerging threats while supporting wider business objectives. He is particularly interested in how AI is changing both the threat landscape and the tools available to security teams.Keiron is also an experienced public speaker and regularly presents at industry conferences on cybersecurity trends, evolving threats, AI, and security strategy. He works with industry analysts and the media, providing expert commentary on current challenges and the future direction of cybersecurity.Combining technical knowledge with strategic and commercial experience, Keiron brings a pragmatic, well-rounded perspective to the complex security issues facing organisations today.

Kent has 19+ years’ experience in investment banking and financing in infrastructure, power & renewables. Kent was most recently Vice President at First Solar, leading ~$8bn+ of investments and financings in the US and Asia Pacific.

Kevin Cochrane is a 25+ year pioneer of the digital experience space. Kevin co-founded his first start-up, Interwoven, in 1996, pioneered open source content management at Alfresco in 2006, and built a global leader in digital experience management as CMO of Day Software and later Adobe. Kevin has also held senior executive positions at OpenText, Bloomreach, and SAP. Now at Vultr, Kevin is now working to build Vultr global brand presence as a leader in the independent Cloud platform market.

Kiran Patel is Chief Technology Officer at WGSN, where he leads global product, engineering, data, AI, security, and platform strategy. He is focused on building enterprise-grade digital and AI capabilities that help organizations move faster, make better decisions, and create long-term competitive advantage. With deep experience modernizing technology platforms, scaling global teams, and translating emerging AI into real business value, Kiran works at the intersection of technology transformation, product innovation, and executive leadership.

Kristin Zwez is Managing Director at Nscale, leading strategic partnerships, building joint ventures, and accelerating growth where clean energy, infrastructure, and technology meet. Previously, Kristin worked in the American renewable company T1 Energy and prior to that for the Norwegian Government's investment promotion agency where she supported international investors in establishing energy-intensive industries in Norway.

Labib Tazwar Rahman is the President of Voltai, a Sequoia-backed company building AI agents that learn, plan, experiment, and interact with the physical world, pushing hardware into a realm previously limited by human cognition. Starting with semiconductors and electronics, Voltai is developing AI to transform how complex hardware systems are designed, invented, and deployed.Labib's work focuses on advancing sovereign AI capabilities and the infrastructure required to support them. In addition to his role at Voltai, he serves as a Principal Investigator at the Stanford Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence (HAI) and as an Advisor to the Stanford Graduate School of Business.

Larissa Holzki heads the Artificial Intelligence team at Handelsblatt, Germany’s premier business journal. Under her guidance, they introduced the “Handelsblatt KI-Briefing”, a weekly AI-focused newsletter highlighting its impact on life and business. Beginning her journey at Süddeutsche Zeitung, Larissa transitioned to Handelsblatt in 2019, where she has since shaped the newspaper’s tech reporting.

Laura Connell is a Senior Partner at Atomico, the founder-built European venture capital firm. Laura is Head of Investments and leads Growth stage investments at Atomico, working with founders from the early scale-up stage through to the public markets. Prior to joining Atomico in 2022, Laura was an investor at  Marcho Partners, a hedge fund investing in public global technology companies. Before this, she was an investor at Balderton Capital, investing in private late stage global technology companies. Laura started her career at Goldman Sachs, first in Investment Management and subsequently in M&A. Laura was a Fulbright Scholar and holds an MBA from Harvard University, a Sir William Paddy Scholar at University of Oxford where she read Philosophy and Economics (PPE) and an Assistant Researcher in the Brain Sciences Team in the Department of Medicine at Imperial College.

Laura Modiano is the Head of Founder Experience, Global at OpenAI, where she works with founders, VCs, accelerators, startup teams and the developer community globally to help startups build, grow and scale on OpenAI from Day 1 - supporting them throughout their founder journey.Before OpenAI, Laura worked with leading US and EMEA venture capital firms and their portfolio companies at AWS, where she led strategy and business development for GenAI startups and previously led Healthcare and Life Sciences Startups in EMEA. Earlier in her career, Laura was a consultant at Monitor Group, worked on AI partnerships at Boston Scientific, led the UK’s NHS national strategy for genetic testing and digital transformation initiatives at UCLH.

Laurent Chemla is a serial entrepreneur, investor, and digital transformation pioneer with more than three decades of experience spanning technology, marketing, media, and enterprise innovation.He began his career leading global digital initiatives for Club Med, helping drive some of the earliest large-scale e-commerce and digital marketing programs. He later founded IVY, one of the first digital marketing agencies, which was subsequently acquired by a leading international advertising group.Today, Laurent invests in, advises, and helps scale a portfolio of AI, marketing technology, and emerging technology companies, while serving in executive and board roles across the technology sector. Throughout his career, he has worked with global brands, technology companies, and investors through successive waves of transformation—from the emergence of the commercial Internet and digital marketing to the reinvention of enterprise operating models through AI.His current work focuses on advising Fortune 500 and leading tech ventures how AI is reshaping enterprise operating models across the marketing value chain—from content creation and media activation to customer engagement, loyalty, commerce, and organizational design.Laurent also serves on the boards of several technology companies, including Stanga1, one of Europe’s leading technology and digital production organizations.Originally from France and now based in Miami, Laurent brings a global perspective on the practical implications of AI for business leaders, operators, and investors.

Leif Abraham is co-founder and co-CEO at Public, the investing platformbuilt for those who take investing seriously. Public has built the technologythat makes constructing a multi-asset portfolio, fast, secure, and frictionless.Founded in 2019, Public has gone on to raise over $400 million frominvestors such as Accel and other prolific investors such as Maria Sharapova,Tony Hawk, Zoe Saldana, and NFL star J.J. Watt.Prior to Public, he was the co-founder and CEO of AND CO, which Fiverracquired (NYSE- FVRR) in 2018. He also co-founded Pay with a Tweet, afirst-of-its-kind social payment system that was acquired by Hanse Venturesin 2012.Additionally, he was a partner at the venture studio Prehype and has heldsenior roles in product strategy at R/GA and West, where he worked onproduct experience for companies like Rdio, Google, and Venmo. He is adouble Cannes Lions Grand Prix winner and holds a MTV OMusic Award.

Lieven is a senior partner and leads QuantumBlack, AI by McKinsey, globally and in Europe. A doctor by training, he is a member of our Life Sciences Practice and serves clients across Europe, North America, Asia, and Africa.Lieven has extensive experience in designing and executing ambitious change journeys, with a particular focus on pharmaceutical companies and biotech as well as public health institutions. For many years, he has led McKinsey’s digital and analytics work across the value chain in life sciences, as well as our global team supporting R&D organizations. He has helped some of the world’s most preeminent institutions leverage the full potential of AI and data while fundamentally changing how teams operate, innovate, and collaborate.Before joining McKinsey, Lieven qualified as a doctor at the Katholieke Universiteit Leuven and completed an MSc and MPhil in neuroscience at Columbia University. He also worked as a fellow with Columbia’s technology transfer office and as an intern with the Belgian Embassy in the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

Lin Qiao is the CEO and co-founder of inference platform Fireworks AI. Fireworks AI empowers developers and businesses like Uber, Verizon, Cursor, and Notion to build highly optimized and production-ready genAI applications.Prior to founding Fireworks, Lin was the co-creator and head of Meta’s PyTorch.

Linz Phillips is Chief Information Officer at Croda, responsible for global IT, digital transformation, data, cybersecurity, and AI across a complex, multinational FTSE 100 manufacturing organisation. Linz’s work sits at the intersection of strategy and delivery—translating ambition into measurable outcomes while leading large‑scale change across the business. With extensive experience across the technology spectrum, Linz is known for combining clear strategic direction with disciplined execution in modernising enterprise platforms, technology portflio’s and operating models. A strong advocate of pragmatic technology adoption, Linz focuses on ensuring innovation is implemented responsibly, securely, and at scale, delivering tangible business value.
Lionel Laurent is a Bloomberg Opinion columnist writing about the future of money and the future of Europe. Previously, he was a reporter for Reuters and Forbes.

Liran co-founded WEKA in 2013. Today, he guides the company’s long-term vision and strategy as its Chief Executive Officer. He previously served as WEKA’s Chief Technology Officer.Before establishing WEKA, Liran co-founded and led research and development at social startup Fusic, where he was responsible for the engineering, design, and development of its rich social media application portfolio. Prior to Fusic, Liran was the principal software architect and a founding member of the technical team at XIV Storage Systems before its acquisition by IBM in 2007; he then managed XIV’s hardware and platform teams at IBM for several years.Before joining XIV, Liran served as a Software Engineer with the rank of Captain in the Israeli Defense Forces for five years. He earned a Bachelor of Science degree in Mathematics and Computer Science from Tel Aviv University.

Loredana Crisan is the Chief Design Officer at Figma, where she leads design, UX writing, brand studio and research. Prior to joining Figma, she spent nine years at Meta, where she held product and design leadership roles across Messenger, Instagram and GenAI initiatives. Before Loredana’s career in product and design, she was a musician and sound engineer.

Louis is an investor at Katko Capital, a long only equity fund managing assets for a few families. He is based in Paris. Prior to joining Katko Capital in 2023, he was an analyst at Aiconic Capital Management B.V., a public and private technology fund based in Amsterdam. Louis graduated with a master’s in management from ESCP Business School.

Luboslava Uram is an international executive leading scalable technology, operations, and transformation, including AI-enabled platforms, in regulated environments. She is currently Chief Operating & Technology Officer at Solvd Group, a subsidiary of Allianz Group.With roots in Slovakia, she has built an international career across Europe with organizations such as SAS, Capgemini, and UniCredit, combining platform thinking with operational execution to deliver impact at scale while keeping people engaged and accountable.At Solvd, she is responsible for enterprise technology, operations, and transformation, accelerating AI-enabled, scalable platforms that support mission-critical services for clients and partners.

Lukas Gentele is the CEO & Co-Founder of vCluster Labs, a venture-backed company that enables organizations building and operating AI infrastructure to run their GPU data centers like a hyperscaler, from bare metal to AI-ready environments.

Madhur Deora is President and Group Chief Financial Officer at Paytm, India's leading digital payments and financial services company. He has been associated with Paytm since 2016 and is responsible for the company's long-term financial and commercial roadmap, investor relations, and governance. Prior to joining Paytm, Madhur spent over 17 years at Citigroup Investment Banking, where he was a Managing Director advising technology, media, telecommunications and private equity clients. He graduated from The Wharton School, and cum laude from The Phillips Exeter Academy.

Mahdi is the President of Radiant. An entrepreneur, investor and telecommunications executive, he was most recently the founder and CEO of Ori Industries. Prior to founding Ori Industries, he was CEO of Sama Telecom, one of the largest carrier networks in the Middle East. He is currently a limited partner in Episode 1 Ventures.A proud patron of the arts, Mahdi sits on the board of the Royal Court Theatre and formerly served on the Tate Museum of Modern Art’s Acquisition Committee. His passion led him to found Room One, an organisation in London that was dedicated to fostering collaboration between the technology and the arts sectors.

Maia Josebachvili is a technology executive and company builder. She is currently Chief Revenue Officer of AI at Stripe. Before this role, Maia held several leadership positions at Stripe, including GM of the Enterprise business, Global Head of Strategy & Operations, M&A, and HR.Before Stripe, Maia was the Founder and CEO of Urban Escapes, an e-commerce travel company, where she was named one of Inc Magazine's Top 30 Entrepreneurs Under 30 years old. Following the successful sale to LivingSocial, she took on the role of GM of New Business Initiatives, leading global teams and helping to scale the company 15x in two years. After LivingSocial, Maia joined as a founding member of Greenhouse and helped grow the company to successful $1B+ sale, serving in various roles from CMO to VP of Strategy & People. She started her career as a derivatives trader on Wall Street and also had a short stint as a professional skydiver.Maia serves on the Board of Trustees at Dartmouth College, her alma mater. She’s also active in the startup community, serving as an advisor to Google Ventures, Board Director of Brightwheel, and member of the Operator Collective.

Mamoon Hamid is a Partner at Kleiner Perkins, where he backs founders building the next generation of transformative technology companies. Over the past two decades, he has been an early investor in and served on the boards of category-defining companies including Figma, Slack, Rippling, Glean, Netskope, and Box. He began his career as an engineer at Xilinx, a Kleiner Perkins-backed company, before moving into venture capital in 2005 at USVP, where he became a Partner. In 2011, he co-founded Social Capital, and in 2017, he joined Kleiner Perkins to lead its next generation. Mamoon grew up in Frankfurt, Germany and moved to the United States to attend Purdue University, earning a BS in Electrical and Computer Engineering. He also holds an MS from Stanford University and an MBA from Harvard Business School.

As Snyk's Chief Innovation Officer and Chief Marketing Officer, Manoj Nair is spearheading the company's evolution from its leadership in Developer Security towards the future of AI Trust. He leads a dynamic organization of founders, builders, and innovators focused on charting Snyk's next horizons. This involves incubating cutting-edge ideas, forging deep strategic partnerships with key customers and technology collaborators, and identifying transformative opportunities in areas like Generative AI and intelligent agents to accelerate Snyk's growth and deliver exceptional customer value. In parallel Manoj is leading a fully AI native Integrated Marketing organization transforming both the external brand of Snyk and internal functioning of Marketing for an AI Native Era. Previously, Manoj served as Snyk's Chief Product Officer from 2022 to 2024. In this role, he was instrumental in scaling the company for widespread enterprise adoption and driving significant growth for Snyk's Developer Security mission. His leadership was key in ensuring Snyk's platform readiness for deployment within the world's largest and most complex organizations, including a majority of the Fortune 500. Before joining Snyk, Manoj was Chief Cloud Officer at Commvault (NASDAQ- CVLT). There, he incubated and scaled the Metallic SaaS venture, a cloud data protectioninnovator launched in close partnership with Microsoft, achieving $100M ARR within three years. Prior to Commvault, Manoj co-founded and served as CEO of HyperGrid, a Cloud Management Platform company. His earlier career included impactful product leadership roles at Hewlett Packard Enterprise, Dell EMC, and RSA Security. Manoj is also an inventor, holding more than a dozen patents in information management and security.

With 25+ years of experience in networking, Mansour founded and served as CEO of Apstra, a Intent-Based Networking company. Later, he held the role of GVP of Products for the Data Center division at Juniper Networks. He holds a PhD in Electrical Engineering from Stanford University.

Maor Shlomo is an accomplished entrepreneur and technology leader who has redefined what a modern tech success story can look like. He is the CEO of Base44, an AI building platform that allows users to create custom, full-stack software applications using natural language. After less than 6 months of founding Base44, he led the company to an acquisition by Wix for $80 million.Before Base44, Maor was the Co-Founder and CTO of Explorium. He helped pioneer innovation in the prepackaged software space, leveraging his deep expertise in data science to drive business impact. Named a Forbes 30 Under 30 honoree, Maor has over a decade of experience at the intersection of data, software, and leadership. He is passionate about emerging technologies and their power to transform industries.

Marc is a tech entrepreneur that for 35+ years has successfully started and scaled software companies from idea to $4B.
