He’s led teams as CEO, COO, GM, President & CRO and global businesses spanning SMB to major enterprise, with product led, sales led and hybrid models for infrastructure, security and engagement solutions.   Today he leads GTM as CRO of Nebius, a full stack AI infrastructure company. He founded and financed four software companies resulting in successful exits including Passlogix to Oracle (NYSE- ORCL), Whiptail to Cisco (NASDAQ- CSCO) and Authy to Twilio. Marc continues his passion for start-ups and building companies as an outside director for Asana (NYSE- ASAN), OneSpan (NASDAQ- OSPN), and SVT Robotics, and an advisor for Browserstack, Glasswing Ventures and Trustle as well as an active angel investor.  Prior to Nrbius, as President of Revenue at Cloudflare from November 2022 to August 2024 he led the turn around of go to market, reaccelerating sales growth with record setting results. As CRO at Twilio from February 2017 through August 2022, he delivered 10x revenue growth after leading the authentication business from February 2015 to February 2017 following the acquisition of Authy where he was President and COO.

Marcelo Claure is a global entrepreneur and investor. He is a Partner and Co-Chairman of Brightstar Capital Partners, a multi-billion investment firm focused on deploying AI to transform businesses.  He also serves as CEO of Claure Group, a multi-billion-dollar investment firm deploying proprietary capital across various sectors, Chairman of Bicycle Capital, Latin America’s leading growth equity fund, Vice Chairman of SHEIN, the world’s largest on-demand e-commerce platform, and Co-Chair of the D^3Institute at Harvard, a global research center providing insights on how to use AI and digital technologies to advance business and society. Claure is also an active board member and the largest independent shareholder of T-Mobile. A passionate football investor, Claure owns Club Bolívar (Bolivia) and co-owns Girona FC (Spain) andNew York City FC (USA) with City Football Group. Previously, he was CEO of SoftBank Group International, where he managed a portfolio of over $450 billion in assets. He also led the turnaround of Sprint as CEO and its landmark merger with T-Mobile, creating the world’s most valuable telecom company. He began his career by founding Brightstar, which under his leadership became the world’s largest wireless distribution and services company before he sold it to SoftBank.

Marco is the firm’s chief information officer. He is a member of the Management Committee, the Firmwide Technology Risk Committee, the Firmwide Client Franchise Committee and the Firmwide Enterprise Risk Committee. Marco joined the firm as a partner in 2019. Prior to joining Goldman Sachs, Marco served as vice president of technology of Amazon Web Services (AWS) since 2013, where he started and ran several AWS businesses, including mobile, serverless computing, Internet of Things, and augmented and virtual reality. Before that, he spent several years at Nokia Corporation, where he was senior vice president and global head of Developer Experience and Marketplace from 2011 to 2013, with responsibility for Nokia’s developer ecosystem and app store across the company’s entire product portfolio. Earlier in his career, Marco was a board member and chief executive officer of internet and mobile company Dada S.p.A., as well as a board member, executive vice president of strategy development and chief technology officer of Canadian e-commerce solutions provider Microforum Inc., where he founded Internet Frontier Inc., an internet publisher and e-commerce retailer. He previously founded and sold Dreamware S.r.l., a software development firm, to Microforum Inc. Marco serves on the Board of Advisors for the Fred Hutchinson Cancer Center and is a board member emeritus of Pancreatic Cancer Action Network. In addition, he is a member of the Board of Trustees of Carnegie Hall. Marco earned a master’s degree in Informatics Engineering from Università di Pisa – Facoltà di Ingegneria and a Professional Engineering qualification in Management Engineering from Politecnico di Milano.

Mark Hura is President of Oracle’s Global Field Operations, executing the company’s go-to-market strategy and AI transformation across all industries and customer segments.Mark has more than 25 years of experience leading organizations and developing executive partnerships ranging from cloud-native ISVs and AI startups to the world’s largest enterprises and governments. Most recently, he led North America sales integrating the applications and infrastructure businesses bringing a simpler, unified experience to Oracle customers. Prior to that, he led Oracle’s North America Cloud Infrastructure business. Under his leadership, the organization delivered record growth, deepened customer adoption of Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, and established Oracle as a leading hyperscaler in the market.In previous roles, he led Oracle’s Energy & Water portfolio helping installed-base customers extend the value of their technology investments. Prior to joining Oracle in 2013, Mark was general manager of sales for GE’s Digital Energy business.

Mark is a leading voice in Industrial AI and one of the sector’s most credible technology leaders, recognized for delivering growth and building high‑performance teams that perform at scale.Under his leadership, IFS is setting the pace in Industrial AI, cutting through complexity, applying AI in ways that drive productivity, resilience, and growth in the industries that power the global economy.He is obsessed with delivering high-quality outcomes for customers and IFS. He has created a strong sense of followship amongst the IFS team of 6000+ employees he is responsible for and is passionate about nurturing and empowering diverse and strong talent.Mark joined IFS in 2022 as Chief Customer Officer, where he set bold targets across growth, profitability, and quality. He streamlined the organization, strengthened financial performance, and drove tangible results for customers and strategic partners, laying the foundation for IFS’s next phase of AI-led growth.Prior to IFS, Mark held senior leadership roles at PwC, most recently as Chief Technology Officer and Technology Consulting Leader for PwC’s UK and EMEA Consulting business. He was responsible for the strategy and direction of the technology offering and for building a strong, ecosystem-led growth model. He began his career in finance, qualifying as a Chartered Accountant, and is a member of the ICAEW.Outside of work, Mark spends his time with his wife and three sons, and trains to compete in amateur triathlon. He lives in central London.

job_title: Chief Technology Officer & Executive Vice President
bio: Mark Papermaster is Chief Technology Officer and Executive Vice President responsible for Advanced Micro Devices’ (AMD) technical direction and product development. He led the re-design of engineering processes at AMD and the development of the award-winning “Zen” high-performance x86 CPU family, high-performance GPUs and the company’s modular design approach, Infinity Architecture.His 40+ years of engineering experience includes significant leadership roles managing the development of a wide range of products, fro microprocessors to mobile devices and high-performance servers. Before joining AMD in October 2011 as Chief Technology Officer and Senior Vice President, Papermaster was the leader of Cisco’s Silicon Engineering Group, Apple Senior Vice President of Devices Hardware Engineering responsible for iPod and iPhone products and held multiple IBM leadership roles in technology and product development.In October 2025, Papermaster was inducted into the prestigious National Academy of Engineering for leadership in the design and production of complex integrated circuit processors.Papermaster is an active member of several boards and committees, including the Global Semiconductor Alliance Board of Directors, IEEE Industry Advisory Board, The University of Texas at Austin Cockrell School of Engineering Advisory Board, The University of Texas President’s AustinInnovation Board, and Purdue University Semiconductor Degrees Leadership Board.Papermaster received his bachelor’s degree from the University of Texas at Austin and master’s degree from the University of Vermont, both in Electrical Engineering.

Mark Porter is a technology executive with a career spanning NASA/JPL, Oracle, Amazon Web Services, Grab, MongoDB, and dbt Labs. At AWS  he led Amazon RDS, Aurora, and the Database Migration Service. He went on to serve as CTO of MongoDB and CTO of dbt Labs, and has served  on the boards of GitLab, MongoDB, and other technology companies. He holds a BS in Engineering and Applied Science from Caltech and has  been coding since the age of 11. He has five magnificent children and a soulmate
Mark Surman has spent three decades building a better internet, from the advent of the web to the rise of artificial intelligence.

María Ridruejo is the AI Services lead for the WW Advisory and Professional Services AI and data segment at HPE. She leads a global team of solution architects, owns the strategy for HPE AI Services portfolio, and drives the innovation road map that brings new AI capabilities and offerings to enterprise customers around the world. Previously, María served as the technical lead for establishing the AI & Data Global Center of Excellence in Madrid. In this role, she defined the COE’s technical blueprint, implemented best practices across data engineering, AI use case development, and MLOps, and built and mentored the founding teams. With more than 20 years of IT experience across diverse technology domains and every stage of the solution lifecycle, María specializes in operationalizing AI at scale, ensuring responsible and trustworthy AI practices, and delivering measurable business outcomes for global enterprises.

Mashrur Haider is currently leading Post-training Product at Nebius, Token Factory where he focuses on building and refining AI platform capabilities that help teams post-train, and deploy large language models in production. He works closely with engineering and product teams to translate complex ML workflows into practical dev tools and experiences.

job_title: Senior Director, NeoCloud/NCP Partnerships, Sales, and GTM, Americas
bio: Mat Torgow leads the Americas go-to-market team for NVIDIA Cloud Partners (NCPs), enabling ecosystem partners to build and deploy the foundational infrastructure for AI-driven intelligence at scale. During his tenure at NVIDIA, he previously led the Healthcare and Life Sciences and Industrial/Manufacturing industry verticals before building the company’s Venture Capital Alliance and leading worldwide VC Business Development. Prior to NVIDIA, Mat held various positions at Cisco, working with their largest global customers.

Matan Grinberg is Founder & CEO of Factory, an AI company building an autonomous AI platform to optimize the software development lifecycle. Prior to Factory, Matan was a theoretical physicist at UC Berkeley and Princeton University working on string theory.

Matt Fitzpatrick is the CEO of Invisible Technologies, leading the company’s mission to make AI work. Since joining as CEO in January 2025, he has raised $100M in capital, expanded R&D to advance Invisible’s core AI software platform, and accelerated AI adoption across industries from sports to consumer and government.Previously, Matt was a Senior Partner at McKinsey, where he led QuantumBlack Labs, the firm’s AI R&D and software development arm. He holds degrees from Princeton University and the Wharton School.

Matt Miller is the Founder & Managing Partner of Evantic Capital, a globally focused venture firm based in London. Evantic aims to invest in and partner with the best rising AI companies in the world. It does so by aligning founders with exceptional talent through its community of 150 Legends. Evantic's investments include Eleven Labs, Fireworks, Harvey, Listen Labs, Lovable, n8n, Profound, Serval and Synthesia.Before launching Evantic, Matt spent 13 years as a Partner at Sequoia Capital, where he founded and co-led Sequoia’s European office in London. Before moving to London in 2021, Matt spent 14 years in Silicon Valley at Sequoia and Goldman Sachs TMT Investment Banking Group.Matt speaks fluent Swedish, splits his time between London, Silicon Valley and Europe, and considers the BA SFO–LHR route his unofficial home. His side passions include a love for the arts and creativity, such as design, fashion and music.

Matt is a Partner at FirstMark, investing in enterprise software and AI in the US and Europe. He organises Data Driven NYC, hosts the MAD Podcast and publishes the MAD Landscape. Previously he co-founded TripleHop Technologies (an enterprise AI search startup acquired by Oracle), was an Executive at Oracle and helped start Bloomberg Ventures. He can be followed on X at @mattturck.

Matthieu Caillat holds a Master of Science in Mathematics from ENSTA ParisTech and a Master’s degree in Economics & Geopolitics from Sciences Po Paris Graduate School. Matthieu started his career 22 years ago in AXA’s division dedicated to Large Commercial Risks, AXA Corporate Solutions (AXA CS). After launching AXA’s Space activity in 2008 and heading AXA CS in the UK until 2015, Matthieu spent 3 years heading AXA CS in France while acting as Deputy Chief Underwriting Officer for AXA CS globally. When AXA acquired XL Catlin in 2018, Matthieu joint the Leadership Team of the newly created AXAXL company as Chief Operating Officer, covering IT, Data & AI, Innovation as well as Operations and Transformation. Since December 1st, 2025, Matthieu has been Group Chief Technology & AI Officer, CEO of AXA Group Operations and has joined AXA’s Management Committee.

Max Cook is a Sector Head at Coatue Management leading investing in Energy & Industrials as well as AI, Cloud, and Software. He is based in the New York Office. Previously he worked at Advent International and Bain & Company. Max graduated from Yale College with a B.A. in Global Affairs and History.

Max Junestrand is the CEO and co-founder of Legora – the AI workspace for legal professionals, serving over 600 customers across 50 markets. Backed by leading investors including Bessemer Venture Partners, ICONIQ, General Catalyst, Benchmark, Redpoint Ventures, and Y Combinator, and supported by a world-class team of engineers and lawyers, Max is driving a new era of collaborative AI in the legal industry.

May Habib is the CEO and co-founder of WRITER, the leading agentic AI platform for the enterprise. Under May’s visionary leadership, WRITER has become a global enterprise AI powerhouse, transforming work at hundreds of pre-eminent organizations, including Accenture, Prudential, Vanguard, Mars, and Uber, and earning recognition as a World Economic Forum Unicorn, Forbes AI 50 company, and CNBC Disruptor. A Harvard graduate with high honors in Economics, May is an expert in natural language processing and AI-driven language generation. She is a World Economic Forum Young Global Leader, a Fellow of the Aspen Global Leadership Network, an Inc. Female Founder Award recipient, and one of Worth’s Groundbreaking Women for 2025.

Meghan McKenna is the Founder and CEO of the Female in Finance Collective (FIF), a high-impact, invite-only network uniting senior women across finance, technology, and venture capital. What began in June 2023 as a career placeholder between banking roles quickly evolved into a leading platform for professional advancement under McKenna’s leadership—connecting thousands of senior women to executive hiring opportunities, board placements, speaking engagements, and strategic relationships.With nearly two decades of experience at institutions including J.P. Morgan, HSBC, and Stifel, McKenna brings deep expertise in capital markets, corporate finance, and the structural barriers women face in the industry. In 2024, she left her executive banking career to lead FIF full-time—doubling down on her mission during a moment of widespread DEI retrenchment. The bet paid off- FIF celebrated its first anniversary on the floor of the New York Stock Exchange and its second at Amazon’s NYC headquarters, growing to more than 2,000 members with a roster of premier partners including Ramp, PwC, CFGI, Rillet, Tabs, and Abacum.McKenna leads with a mission-first ethos, deliberately choosing sustainable, community-driven growth over traditional venture-backed scaling. FIF’s membership model includes a distinctive allyship tier, inviting male members—fathers, brothers, and advocates—to participate meaningfully in advancing women’s leadership. The result is a community that doesn’t just talk about change, but actively builds it.A Bronx native and former Division I athlete, McKenna attributes FIF’s early momentum to discipline, resilience, and an unwavering support system. She is known for creating curated, high-caliber spaces where relationships are built, careers accelerate, and women’s voices are amplified. Equally at home, on stage at a summit, hosting an upscale dinner, or leading a karaoke night, she has earned a reputation as a true “Chief Connections Officer.”McKenna serves on the Advisory Boards of Turbine, Slice Global, Preql, Zenskar, Capitol AI, WalkTru and Panax, among others. Named a Finance Innovator to Follow in 2025 and featured multiple times in Forbes, she is redefining inclusive leadership—one curated room at a time.

job_title: Vice Chairman & Global Head of Capital Markets
bio: Michael Harris is the Vice Chairman and Global Head of Capital Markets for NYSE Group, a part of Intercontinental Exchange, Inc. (NYSE- ICE). Harris is responsible for attracting the world's leading companies to list on the NYSE, the largest and most liquid trading venue. He leads a global team focused on driving dialogue and building strategic relationships with key stakeholders within the private company ecosystem including founders/CEO’s, venture firms, private equity firms, advisory firms and market makers. Prior to joining the NYSE, Harris spent over four years at Citadel Securities as the Head of Capital Markets and Business Development, where he was responsible for building out corporate relationships and increasing Citadel’s presence with financial sponsors, VCs, corporate clients and equity advisors. Earlier in his career, Harris served as Deputy Chief Investment Officer at the U.S. Treasury where he helped wind down remaining TARP investments. In addition, he served in a variety of senior investment banking and advisory roles at UBS and JPMorgan. Harris earned a BA in Economics and a BS in Social Policy from Northwestern University. He currently serves on the board of TriState Capital Bank, an independently chartered banking subsidiary of Raymond James, offering commercial, industrial and private banking solutions.
Michael Hurlston joined Lumentum as President and CEO in 2025.

Michael Koch is one of the most sought-after minds in Artificial Intelligence and the creator behind the next evolution of technology Hyperlocal Intelligence.  He is redefining how global brands deploy hyperlocal marketing through Al-powered precision, speed, and scale.A true pioneer of Enterprise Al, Michael has built a career turning bold ideas into high-performance platforms that shape the future. His leadership fuses visionary thinking with elite execution, enabling rapid scale across complex enterprise ecosystems for the top brands in the world.  Michael founded and exited four successful tech companies. His teams have delivered cutting-edge solutions in 79 countries and fueled growth for over hundreds of thousands of locations worldwide.At HubKonnect, Michael is building more than a platform, he's building a new category of AI Hyperlocal Intelligence. Recognized as a top leader in Al, he continues to push the boundaries of what's possible in technology, Al ethics, and global impact.

job_title: Operating Partner at Team8, former Director of the NSA
bio: Admiral Michael S. Rogers is an Operating Partner at Team8, where he advises Cyber businesses.He supports various companies in the private sector, speaking to business groups and working internationally in the cyber and national security arenas.Before joining Team8, Michael spent 37 years serving in the US Navy, where he rose to the rank of four-star admiral. He held positions focused on cyber, intelligence, maritime operations, and maritime security, culminating in a four-year tour as commander of the US Cyber Command and Director of the NSA. His extensive service included duty with the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, the Joint Chiefs of Staff, and US Indo-Pacific Command. He commanded at the unit, fleet, and service component levels in the Navy.Admiral Rogers is a graduate of Auburn University. He holds a MS in National Security and is a graduate of the National War College and Naval War College. He is an MIT Seminar XXI fellow and a Harvard Senior Executive in National Security alum.

Coming from the financial sector, Michel founded and invested early on in innovative HPC companies, set up new data centers across Europe, and was an early adopter of innovative liquid cooling concepts. As managing director of the cloud division of a publicly listed company, he successfully built up the AI cloud business. Today, Michel brings his extensive experience to his role as CEO of Polarise, where he is developing the first sustainable and sovereign AI factories from Europe and for Europe - for an independent and sustainable future.

Michele Catasta is President & Head of AI at Replit, where he is shaping the future of software creation through AI agents. In 2024, he architected and led the launch of Replit Agent, which has driven over two orders of magnitude in company revenue growth to date. Previously, he was Head of Applied Research at Google X and Google Labs, focusing on Large Language Models and contributing to the coding capabilities of PaLM and PaLM 2.‍Earlier in his career, Michele was a Research Scientist and Instructor at Stanford University, pioneering the application of Transformer architectures to source code. He earned a Ph.D. in Computer Science from EPFL and held research positions at MIT Media Lab, Google, and Yahoo Labs.‍Beyond his operating roles, Michele is an active angel investor with more than 100 investments in the AI space, and serves (or has served) as an advisor to Bessemer, Coatue, Fellows Fund, and Obvious.

Mike Butcher MBE is Founder and Editor of Pathfounders, the new tech media outlet and podcast which dives deeper into the AI startups revolution. Most recently the long-time Editor-at-large of TechCrunch, he has been named one of the most influential people in European technology. He has interviewed Tony Blair, Lily Cole, Pavel Durov, Jimmy Wales, and many other celebrities. Mike is a regular broadcaster and podcaster; has advised UK Prime Ministers and the Mayor of London on tech startups; and has been a judge on The Apprentice UK. He is a co-founder of the non-profits Techfugees, TechVets, and Startup Coalition. Mike was awarded an M.B.E. by the Queen in 2016 for services to the UK technology industry and journalism.

Milin Desai currently serves as the CEO of Sentry. Used by more than 4 million developers and 150,000 organizations worldwide, Sentry helps every developer detect, understand, and fix broken code, fast. Milin joined Sentry from VMware where he helped build out multiple lines of business from networking (0->1b+) to multi cloud (0->100M+ SAAS). Earlier in his career, he held engineering and product roles at Veritas and Riverbed.

Mr. Mohsen Moazami served as a member of the Office of the CEO and President of International at Groq until shortly after the $20 billion transaction with Nvidia, the largest transaction in the history of Nvidia.Prior to this role, he served as the Founder and Managing Partner of Seif Capital, a technology investment and advisory firm based in Silicon Valley.Currently, Mr. Moazami serves as a Senior Advisor to Blackstone and is an Operating Partner at Kestrel Partners in London, which focuses on driving value creation in under-researched, business-critical software companies listed on the UK Stock Exchange.He also served as Chairman of Astrea Acquisition Corp (NASDAQ- ASAX) and continues tonserve as a board member or senior advisor to several venture-backed startups, including X-Energy, Axiom Space, Ethernovia (backed by AMD and Marvell), and others.Mr. Moazami began his career as the Founder and CEO of Stanford Business Systems (acquired by Accenture/KSA). Subsequently, he served a 12-year tenure at Cisco, where he spent his final four years in executive leadership of the Emerging Markets division, overseeing $6 billion in revenue.He later co-founded CNTP, a global multi-stage technology investment firm with $300 million in assets under management (AUM), focusing on cybersecurity, big data analytics, gig economy platforms, and software as a service (SaaS).Notable early-stage investments include Matterport (IPO), DoorDash (IPO), Bina Technologies (acquired by Roche), and Bracket Computing (acquired by VMware).Serving on the board of Global Capacity, a telecommunications company that was successfully revitalized and subsequently acquired by GTT for $150 million.Mr. Moazami holds a master’s degree in mechanical engineering from Stanford University and a Bachelor of Science degree in Engineering Sciences with a minor in Economics from UC San Diego. In recognition of his contributions to the US, he was awarded the Ellis Island Medal of Honor in 2010.

Molly is a high-profile American podcaster based in Los Angeles who has interviewed CEOs like Alex Karp, Vlad Tenev, Palmer Luckey, and Dara Khosrowshahi as part of her newsletter and podcast, Sourcery. She's also venture capitalist.

Molly Presley is Chief Marketing Officer at Hammerspace and host of the Data Unchained podcast. She is a technology marketing leader focused on AI infrastructure and modern data platforms, with experience building product marketing organizations, global user communities, and customer advisory boards for leading innovators. Molly founded the Active Archive Alliance, co-authored three books on putting data to work in research, analytics, and AI, and previously served on the board of the Storage Networking Industry Association (SNIA).

job_title: Senior Managing Director & Co-Head of Flagship Fund
bio: Monti Saroya joined Vista Equity Partners in 2008 and is Co-Head of the Vista Flagship Fund and sits on its Investment Committee.Additionally, Monti serves as a member of Vista’s Executive Committee, the firm’s governing and decision-making body for matters affecting its overall management and strategic direction, and Vista’s Private Equity Management Committee, the firm’s decision-making body for matters affecting Vista’s overall private equity platform. Monti is also the Co-Chief Executive Officer of VistaOne, Vista’s evergreen private equity vehicle, and serves on the Investment Committee.He currently sits on the boards of Allvue Systems, Cloud Software Group, Cvent (NASDAQ- CVT), Finastra, Gainsight, iCIMS, Infoblox, Mindbody, Olive, OneAdvanced, Pipedrive, Salesloft, Solera and Xactly and previously sat on the board of PowerSchool (formerly NYSE- PWSC). Monti is also actively involved in the firm’s investments in Jio and Vivid Seats (NASDAQ- SEAT) and was actively involved in the firm’s investments in Apptio, Datto (formerly NYSE- MSP), Marketo, Pluralsight, SumTotal, The ACTIVE Network, Transfirst, Turaz and Zywave.Prior to Vista, Monti worked as a Senior Research Analyst for JMP Securities, where he provided research for buy-side clients on public on-demand (SaaS) companies. Monti previously worked as an Associate for the enterprise software/applications team. Before JMP, Monti worked at Siebel Systems in a sales capacity for the CRM On Demand division. Prior to Siebel, Monti worked for Cisco Systems in various operations roles.

Nancy Wang is Chief Technology Officer at 1Password, where she leads technology and product strategy across identity security, security for AI, applied AI, and developer platforms. Previously, she built and scaled foundational infrastructure and security platforms at Google, Rubrik, and AWS, where she founded AWS Data Protection into a multi-billion-dollar business protecting exabytes of enterprise data worldwide. Nancy is a leading voice on the intersection of AI, identity, and security, with a focus on how organizations can safely adopt AI agents by extending identity, access, and governance models beyond humans to autonomous systems. She is also a Venture Partner at Felicis, advising founders building the next generation of infrastructure, security, and AI-native companies.

Neil Zeghidour is the co-founder and CEO of Gradium. Neil founded Gradium after a decade of building and leading frontier generative audio teams at Meta and Google DeepMind. Being frustrated by slow and brittle voice assistants , he built the engineering teams that developed the first neural audio codecs  and introduced the first audio LLMs, such as AudioLM, at Google. He later created Kyutai to launch Moshi, the world's first real-time, full-duplex conversational AI , and Hibiki, the first simultaneous speech-to-speech translation system. Today, Gradium is focused on helping developers build natural, real-time voice agents by providing ultra-low latency streaming APIs that transition these breakthroughs from the research lab to production.

Nelson Griggs is President of Nasdaq, leading Nasdaq's Capital Access Platforms division, which is comprised of our Data & Listings, Index, and Workflow & Insights businesses. In this capacity, he is responsible for leading a business that serves over 10,000 corporate clients and 5,000 investor clients across the investment management ecosystem. Nelson served as Executive Vice President of Nasdaq's Corporate Platforms business in his prior role. In this capacity, he oversaw new listings and capital markets, our governance and investor relations  solutions business, and led global business development and relationship management with over 4,000 of our listed companies around the world.  He served previously as Senior Vice President of New Listings and Capital Markets in the U.S. and Asia, attracting some of the highest-profile IPOs and transfers to Nasdaq. Since joining our company in 2001, he has held a range of leadership roles within Listing Services unit including- Senior Vice President, Listing Services, where he was responsible for our U.S. listings business and corporate solutions sales; and Head of Asia Pacific. Prior to joining Nasdaq, Nelson worked at Fidelity Investments and a San Francisco-based start up. He graduated from Denison University in Granville, OH.

Nico Laqua is the co-founder and CEO of Corgi Insurance, an AI-native insurance carrier built for startups. Based in San Francisco, Laqua focuses on rebuilding legacy financial infrastructure with modern software, using artificial intelligence to automate underwriting, claims processing, and policy management.Before founding Corgi in 2024, Laqua co-founded Basket Entertainment, a gaming publisher that grew to more than 150–200 million monthly active users across a portfolio of mobile games. His background combines deep technical expertise with startup building, having previously worked on projects ranging from computational biology research to early AI and virtual reality systems.Laqua studied neuroscience at Columbia University, where he was a Rabi Scholar recognized among the university’s top STEM students. His work today centers on applying large language models and modern software infrastructure to traditionally slow, paper-heavy industries such as insurance.

job_title: Founder Rosberg Ventures| 2016 F1 World Champion
bio: Nico Rosberg is the 2016 Formula One World Champion and founder of Rosberg Ventures, a technology investment platform with more than $200 million in assets under management. After retiring from Formula One just days after winning the World Championship, he spent years building a second career in venture capital, backing leading technology companies and funds across Europe and Silicon Valley. Today, he is an investor, entrepreneur and commentator on innovation, technology and high performance.

Nikhil Benesch has spent over a decade working on database systems. He was an early engineer at Cockroach Labs, focused on distributed SQL, and went on to serve as CTO of Materialize, where he led development of a streaming database built on incremental computation. He is an active open-source contributor, with projects spanning SQL tooling, systems programming in Rust, and developer utilities. Nikhil is now CTO of turbopuffer, a vector and full-text search engine built on object storage and used by companies like Anthropic, Cursor, and Notion.

Nikita Vdovushkin is Product Director, Token Factory at Nebius, based in Amsterdam. He brings a unique blend of cybersecurity and product leadership experience, having previously served as General Manager of the CyberThreat Research Division at BI.ZONE, where he led a 40-person team and grew the division's client base eightfold while turning it profitable.

Nina dos Santos is a Director at Freuds in London. Prior to joining the communications agency, she was a television anchor and correspondent for CNN for more than a decade, reporting from Davos to DC and Brussels to Berlin. She has also worked for NBC News, Sky News and Bloomberg Television.Nina has twice won the Alfred duPont Award from Columbia University and she has also been shortlisted for a GLAAD and an Emmy.

Nitzan is the Chief AI Officer at eBay where he runs the company-wide strategy for artificial intelligence and technology innovation. An R&D scientist by training, Nitzan has spent his career developing machine intelligence systems, directly integrated into mission-critical products. Having led enterprise AI organizations across multiple industries, including healthcare, financial services, and ecommerce, Nitzan is a thought leader in the delivery of transformational impact through real-time AI at scale, changing companies’ business models and core value propositions to their customers. Nitzan received his PhD from the University of Chicago, and currently resides in New York as the GM of eBay NYC.

Noam Salinger is the CEO and Co-founder of Impala AI, a company building enterprise infrastructure to run large language models at unlimited scale. Previously, he served as VP of Product at Granulate, which was acquired by Intel for a reported $650M, where he led product strategy and analytics. Under his leadership, Impala AI emerged from stealth with an $11M seed round led by Viola Ventures and NFX. Noam brings deep experience in building and scaling AI and infrastructure products for enterprise environments.

Oliver leads Lakestar’s investment team and focuses on transformative technology companies, with a particular eye on bridging European innovation with the US market. He has been instrumental in shaping the firm’s transatlantic thesis and plays an active role in scaling companies across both continents. His investment focus spans deep tech and healthcare, having led or co-led investments in standout companies such as Oscar Health, Cedar Health, Auterion, Public.com, Rhino, Nelly, Harry’s, Aetion Health, Neuralink and Onodrim.

Married, father of two children, entrepreneur, and diagnosed with ALS in 2020, Olivier Goy now fights for research and for the inclusion of disability, wearing two hats. Ambassador of the Paris Brain Institute — the world’s second-largest neuroscience research center. Thanks to all the initiatives around the film Invincible Été, the book Invincible, and the concert Invincibles Ensemble, €4 million have already been raised for research in France and Canada. After its theatrical release in France in May 2023, followed by Canal+ and M6, the documentary directed by Stéphanie Pillonca is pursuing an international career and the film is now on Apple TV and Prime Video (Amazon) in France, Spain, and Japan. Sponsor of “Les Invincibles”, a joyful patients’ association created after the release of the film. He is also the co-founder of the photo4food foundation, which fights poverty through photography. He has been named Chevalier of the National Order of Merit and Chevalier of the Legion of Honor. In 2024, he co-authored the book Invincible with Anne Fulda, published by Éditions de l’Observatoire.

Olivier is a founding partner at >commit, the first transatlantic VC focused on investing on Commercial Open Source companies. He is also a GP at Red River West, an early growth fund that helps European startups growing in the US. Olivier spent a decade at Orange and then as CFO of two startups before crossing to venture, where he co-founded C4 Ventures and backed companies like Graphcore, Riskified and Via (the last two IPO'd on NYSE). General Partner at Red River West. Polytechnique Engineer who grabbed an accountancy degree along the way (confuses people at times). French and British, lived in Pretoria, Washington DC and London, now sharing his time between Geneva and Paris. Plays the saxophone in a band. Black belt in Judo, the dojo hasn't seen him in a while, world traveler.

Prior to founding Datadog, Olivier Pomel built data systems for K-12 teachers as a VP, Technology for Wireless Generation, where he grew the development team from a handful of people to close to 100 of the best engineers in New York until the company’s acquisition by News Corp. Before Wireless Generation, Olivier held software engineering positions at IBM Research and several internet startups. Olivier is an original author of the VLC media player and holds a MS, CS from the Ecole Centrale Paris.

Omar Sanseviero leads Developer Experience at Google DeepMind, where he works on launching models such as Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana, and Gemma, Google’s family of open models. He is responsible for building developer products, including the Gemini API and Google AI Studio.Previously, Omar led Platform and Community at Hugging Face, where he directed on-device and DevRel Engineering, working at the intersection of open source, products, research, and technical communities. He is the co-author of “Hands-On Generative AI with Transformers and Diffusion Models” with O'Reilly.

Since his appointment as CEO in 2018, Lenchner works to advance Bright Data’s innovative data collection products, while also leading the industry in ethics, transparency and self-regulation.Lenchner is a sought-after thought leader in the future of AI, ethics and public web data. He has participated in regulatory events that will shape the future of legislation that protects public web data in the public domain.Lenchner joined Bright Data in 2015 as a product manager and was pivotal in the design of Bright Data’s first products — driven by a firm belief in a transparent, compliance-driven web environment that serves both the public and the business sector alike.Today, under Lenchner’s leadership, Bright Data serves thousands of prominent organizations.

job_title: President of Lightning AI and Former CEO of Voltage Park
bio: Ozan Kaya is the President of Lightning AI, former CEO of Voltage Park, leading the company's mission to democratize AI compute and build infrastructure for innovators and enterprises alike. Prior to Voltage Park, he served as President of CarLotz until its merger to Shift Technologies. Prior to that role, Ozan held positions at ShopRunner (CFO and COO – acquired by FedEx), Kapow (CFO), and Milestone Aviation Group (Treasurer). Before his operating career, Ozan worked in Investment Banking at Deutsche Bank and Wachovia Securities. He also contributes to academia as a member of the Integrated Systems Engineering Advisory Board at The Ohio State University where he has an endowment for academic scholarships. Ozan holds a BS in Industrial and Systems Engineering with a minor in Business from The Ohio State University.

Paolo Guglielmini is Vice President EMEA at NVIDIA, where he leads the regional ecosystem to drive NVIDIA’s growth agenda and help governments, enterprises and partners across Europe, the Middle East and Africa deploy AI at scale. This includes sovereign AI infrastructure, AI factories and accelerated computing platforms that are reshaping EMEA’s industries and public institutions. A strategic, global technology leader with over 20 years of executive experience, Paolo combines clear strategic vision with empathetic, high-integrity leadership to build high performing teams that execute with pace and precision. Before joining NVIDIA, Paolo served as Group CEO of Hexagon, a global leader in digital reality solutions, where he led the company’s pivot toward vertical software and 3D digitization across a broad portfolio spanning manufacturing, construction, geospatial, mining and agriculture. Through bold innovation and global M&A, he developed deep expertise at the intersection of engineering software, sensor fusion, autonomous systems and robotics — the technologies now central to NVIDIA’s platform. Earlier in his career, Paolo contributed to the engineering infrastructure for CERN’s Large Hadron Collider, launched in 2009 — one of the most ambitious scientific projects in human history. He holds a Master’s degree in Mechanical Engineering from the University of Genoa and an MBA from IMD.

Parag Agrawal is the founder of Parallel Web Systems, a company unlocking the web for AI agents. Previously, he spent 11 years at Twitter, where he joined as an engineer before serving as CTO, and then CEO. Parag has a PhD from Stanford University in Computer Science and a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science and Engineering from IIT, Bombay.

job_title: Senior Vice President, Global Sales, Cloud
bio: Parimal Pandya is Senior Vice President of Global Cloud Sales at Akamai, where he leads the company’s global cloud sales strategy. His responsibilities include driving new customer acquisition, expanding relationships with existing customers, overseeing account management, and managing Akamai’s cloud partner ecosystem, including ISVs, SIs, and VARs.

Pat Gelsinger is a visionary engineer and values-driven leader with over 45 years of experience shaping Silicon Valley as a CEO, CTO, and technologist. He has played a pivotal role in advancing foundational technologies across semiconductors, microprocessors, and industry standards through leadership roles at Intel, EMC, and VMware.Today, Pat is a General Partner at Playground Global, a deep tech venture firm with $1.2 billion under management. He serves as Executive Chair of xLight, which is reimagining free electron laser technology, and sits on the board of Snowcap Compute, a company developing the first commercially viable superconducting compute platform.Outside of Playground, Pat is Executive Chair and Head of Technology at Gloo, a platform serving organizations in the faith and flourishing ecosystem. He is also a speaker, author, inventor, and investor—as well as a husband, father of four, and grandfather of eight.

Peter Elstrom is an award-winning journalist and currently executive editor for Bloomberg News’ Global Technology group, based in London. Prior to joining Bloomberg, he worked at Businessweek magazine. Elstrom is the former president of the Foreign Correspondents Club of Japan and previously selected for the Michigan Journalism Fellowship. He holds an MBA from the University of Chicago and an undergraduate degree i economics from Northwestern University.

Petrina is responsible for shaping Equinix’s Emerging Tech (AI, Quantum & Edge) and Emerging Ecosystems program globally. She spent the last 4 years curating Equinix's global connected, autonomous vehicles and smart transport strategy, including integrating emerging technologies that include IoT, cloud technologies, 5G, AI and complex data sharing.

Phil Kumin is Senior Vice President of Global Sales at Credo where he is responsiblefor setting and executing worldwide revenue strategy for the company’s high-speedconnectivity solutions for AI infrastructure. Phil previously led the PCIe Product groupand was instrumental in the launch of Credo’s PCI Express 6 /7, CXL 3.x retimers, andAEC PCIe product line. Before joining Credo, he served as Senior Vice President ofGlobal Sales and Marketing at Morse Micro and prior to that, Senior Vice President ofGlobal Sales and Sales Operations for Synaptics. Phil holds a BSEE from the UMassLowell and currently serves on the West Coast Advisory Board for the UMass LowellFrancis School of Engineering.

Philip Johnston is Co-Founder and CEO of Starcloud, which builds data centres in space to address the AI energy bottleneck. Starcloud recently became the first entity to launch and operate an Nvidia H100 GPU (AI chip) in space, 100x more powerful GPU compute than has been on orbit before. Starcloud was also recently the first entity to train an LLM in space and to run a version of Gemini in space. Philip is a second-time founder who previously spent time at McKinsey and Company working on satellite projects for national space agencies. He has an MPA in National Security and Technology from Harvard University, an MBA from Wharton, an MA in Applied Mathematics and Theoretical Physics from Columbia University, and he is a CFA Charterholder.

Philip Rathle is the CTO of Neo4j, the graph database & AI leader valued at over $2 billion. Neo4j is trusted by more than 84 of the Fortune 100, thousands of startups, and organizations of all sizes in between. Since he joined Neo4j in 2012 to lead product, Neo4j has become one of the largest and fastest-growing new-generation database technologies, fueled by open source, open standards, and AI’s need for knowledge and context. Philip helped spearhead the creation of ISO GQL - the first new international database query language standard in nearly 40 years - cementing graph technology as a generational shift on par with the introduction of SQL. In his piece, The GraphRAG Manifesto, he bridges these standards with the future of GenAI, describing how practitioners can solve for AI accuracy and trust by using graphs alongside LLMs to address high-stakes problems. Based in Silicon Valley, Philip has spent 30 years working in data and databases, as a consultant, founder, and product & technology leader. Today he works at the intersection of knowledge & context, databases, and AI, to advance the state of the database layer inside the AI stack.

Philippe joined Accel in 2011 and focuses on AI, cloud and cybersecurity. His active investments include Algolia, BlaBlaCar, Chainalysis, Doctolib, Gloat, PayFit, and more recently Cyera, H, Synthesia, and Surf AI. His notable exits include DocuSign (NASDAQ- DOCU), PeopleDoc (acquired by Ultimate Software), UiPath (NASDAQ- PATH) and more recently Melio (acquired by Xero).Prior to Accel, Philippe was with Bessemer, based in Menlo Park, where he helped build the firm’s cloud practice, working on investments including Cornerstone OnDemand (NASDAQ- CSOD), Eloqua (NASDAQ- ELOQ) and Criteo (NASDAQ- CRTO). He started his career with McKinsey & Company.When the traction in Europe’s cloud ecosystem started accelerating in 2016, Philippe crystalised this momentum by publishing Accel’s Euroscape, an in-depth report on the region’s cloud ecosystem. With AI now redefining the way applications and software are written, and driving a new industrial revolution globally, Euroscape evolved into Globalscape in 2025. You can read the report and see Accel’s list of AI companies to watch across Europe, Israel and the US here- www.accel.com/globalscape.Philippe is from Paris and graduated from École Polytechnique and École des Mines.

Philippe Mizrahi is the co-founder and CEO of Linkup, building the search infrastructure that enables production-grade AI systems.AI has the potential to unlock transformative outcomes, accelerating research breakthroughs, driving productivity gains, fueling business growth. But realizing that potential hinges entirely on one thing- access to accurate, timely data. Philippe founded Linkup to solve exactly that- where traditional search was built for humans, Linkup delivers trusted sources, verified facts, and real-time context built for AI.At Raise Summit, Philippe will explore what it means for AI to have access to real, grounded context in the age of generated everything - and why reliable data access is what separates prototype demos from systems that can truly act knowingly.

job_title: Senior Vice President, Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer
bio: Philippe Rambach is the SVP, Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer of Schneider Electric. His mission is to drive AI innovation at scale, both internally and for customers, to provide greater overall efficiency and sustainability through data-based insights. Philippe is a graduate of Ecole Polytechnique in France and joined Schneider Electric in 2010 from AREVA. He has more than 20 years of experience in strategy, innovation, and business responsibility in many industries. He held various leadership roles in Energy Management and Industrial Automation. Most recently, as SVP Industrial Automation Commercial, where he led the commercial organization. He has an international career background and is currently based in France, reporting to the Chief Digital Officer.

A finance and marketing professional focused on emerging technologies, he has held marketing and PR roles at Lenovo, Intel, and more than 10 years at NVIDIA, first as Embedded AI Ecosystem Manager for EMEA, and since 2018 in his current role connecting startups, universities, research centers, and industry to help young companies scale across software, hardware, and architecture.

Pierre is a partner at Plural, the early-stage fund that backs the most ambitious founders on a mission to change the world through technology. Prior to Plural, Pierre spent over a decade at Uber, having joined in 2012 as one of the company’s earliest employees. There, he led one of the fastest international expansions in tech history, growing operations from the ground up in France to a multi-billion-dollar P&L and a team of thousands working across continents. He went on to become VP of International Mobility in 2019, before leading Uber Eats globally from 2020 as SVP of Delivery, and overseeing several of the company’s major strategic initiatives. His investments at Plural include Metaview and Validio.

Pim de Witte is the co-founder and CEO of General Intuition, an AI research lab focused on spatial-temporal reasoning. He previously founded gaming social platform Medal, creating the world's largest ground-truth action-labelled dataset, which now powers General Intuition's models. Before gaming and AI, he worked with Médecins Sans Frontières and ran the largest Runescape private server at 14.

Piotr Tomasik is the President and Co-Founder of TensorWave, bringing over a decade of experience in AI, Creator Economy, and SaaS platforms. He is also a General Partner at 1864 Fund, a seed fund investing in high-tech startups in Nevada and beyond. Before TensorWave, Piotr founded Lets Rolo (acquired by LifeKey) and was Co-Founder and CTO at Influential (acquired by Publicis for $500M in the summer of 2024). Piotr has scaled multiple companies from 0-1 and into growth stages and has been instrumental in fundraising at each stop. He holds a BS in Computer Science and has most often served as technical co-founder or CTO at companies he has been part of. He actively supports the tech community through StartUp Vegas, and has been recognized with awards including the 2017 Las Vegas Top Tech Exec Award, 2018 Vegas INC 40 Under 40, and 2020 UNLV Howard Hughes College of Engineering Alumni of the Year.

Head of PSG Labs, Paris Saint-Germain’s in-house, end-to-end Global Venture Capability. PSG Labs serves as the club’s innovation hub, identifying, launching and accelerating external innovations while aligning them with PSG’s brand and business objectives. Advisor to investors, clubs, leagues, federations, owners, and entrepreneurs navigating the intersection of sports, brands, IP, media, and tech.

Qasar Younis is the Co-Founder and CEO of Applied Intuition. Before founding Applied Intuition, Younis was a Partner and COO of Y Combinator, a prominent venture accelerator in Silicon Valley. Prior to Y Combinator, Younis founded a technology firm, which was later acquired by Google, and worked as an automotive engineer at General Motors and Bosch. Younis has a bachelor’s degree in mechanical engineering from Kettering University (formerly known as General Motors Institute) and an MBA from Harvard Business School.

Rachel Metz has been a technology journalist for two decades, chronicling the rise of many of the most consequential startups — from Facebook to OpenAI. For the past seven years she has focused on artificial intelligence, looking at the rapid evolution of machine learning and its consequences. She joined Bloomberg News to cover AI in early 2023; before that she was most recently a senior writer at CNN Business, where she covered AI as well. Earlier in her career, she wrote for MIT Technology Review and The Associated Press.

While in college, Raj founded Voxel, a cloud and hosting company acquired by Internap in 2012. His two great passions are observability and aviation; he got his private pilot's license almost 20 years ago and has completed his motorglider rating.

Dr. Ramin Hasani is the co-founder and CEO of Liquid AI.  Previously, he was jointly appointed as a Principal Machine Learning Scientist at the Vanguard Group and a Research Affiliate at MIT CSAIL.  Ramin’s research focuses on robust deep learning and decision-making in complex dynamical systems.  Prior to that, he was a Postdoctoral Associate at CSAIL MIT, leading research on modeling intelligence and sequential decision-making.  He received his Ph.D. degree with distinction in Computer Science from the Vienna University of Technology, Austria.  His Ph.D. dissertation and continued research on Liquid Neural Networks have been recognized internationally with numerous nominations and awards.

Raphaël Marichez previously occupied cybersecurity and operational responsibilities in the French Government for 10 years. Mostly, as the global Chief information security officer for the French Ministry of Interior, and as the head of the PM agency in charge of deploying and operating the federal communication network for all the ministries over the French territory mainland and overseas. He started his career 18 years ago as an infosec consultant and red-teamer.He joined Palo Alto Networks as regional CSO in 2020 to fasten cybersecurity transformation in large companies and public organisations in France and Southern Europe, increasing their information systems’ efficiency, business value and robustness, by advising CxO leadership teams and advocating Palo Alto Networks’ vision for cybersecurity. He also serves as a cybersecurity advisor to startups across Europe.

Raphaëlle d’Ornano is the founder of Decoding Discontinuity and is incubating amarket-neutral hedge fund whose investment process is powered by proprietaryframeworks designed for the agentic era.She analyzes AI as a structural discontinuity that resets competitive rules and valuecapture across tech and non-tech industries, rather than simply shifting existingcurves. Her Architectural Resilience Assessment Framework (ARAF™) and DurableGrowth Moat™ framework assess orchestration potential versus displacement risk.They identify which companies will control the coordination layers as AI agentsbecome autonomous actors and which will face repricing. These frameworks serveas the analytical engine for her fund, positioning long exposures in companiesnavigating the transition and short exposures in those that do not.Before founding Decoding Discontinuity, Raphaëlle spent more than a decadebuilding an international financial analysis and strategic advisory firm, working withinstitutional investors and executive teams across venture capital, growth, and privateequity on technology-driven transitions.She began publishing her thesis on generative AI as a discontinuity in 2023.Decoding Discontinuity is one of Substack’s leading technology publications, and theAGNT podcast extends the research through long-form dialogue with researchers,operators, and allocators.A former triathlete, Raphaëlle lives in New York with her husband and two children.

Ravi Prasher is the Chief Technology Officer of Bloom Energy. He also serves as an adjunct professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering at UC Berkeley. Prior to joining Bloom Energy, Ravi was the Associate Lab Director of Energy Technology Area at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory (LBNL). His responsibilities included managing research and development in a wide variety of areas, including fuel cells, hydrogen production, storage and transport, electrochemical and thermal storage, carbon capture, microgrids, and renewable energy among others. He was also a Senior Scientist at LBNL where he conducted research in thermal science and engineering. Ravi’s experience includes being one of the first program directors at US DOE’s high-risk high-reward funding agency, ARPA-E, and serving as the technology development manager of Intel’s thermal management group. Ravi has published 150+ archival papers in top science and engineering journals and holds more than 35 patents. He is a member of the US National Academy of Engineering and a lifetime Fellow of American Association for the Advancement of Science, the world’s largest general scientific society. Ravi obtained his B.Tech. from IIT Delhi and PhD from Arizona State University.

Retainna Lin is VP of Bitdeer AI Cloud, a full-stack AI cloud platform and preferred NVIDIA Cloud Partner, where she leads commercial strategy, partnerships and brand positioning. With a Master's in Digital Leadership from the National University of Singapore and over a decade of B2B marketing experience across Asia and the UK, she bridges technical innovation and commercial strategy, helping organizations navigate the generative AI era and shaping how the world will build and scale the backbone of intelligence.

Richard Rizk is co-founder and CTO of Blackbox AI. He architects the inference engine that runs Nemotron 3 Ultra faster and cheaper than any other provider on Earth — and the encryption protocol that makes Blackbox the only AI platform enterprises can actually trust.

Rick McConnell has been a successful technology executive for 30 years, establishing a reputation for scaling multibillion-dollar companies in competitive growth markets. Prior to joining Dynatrace in 2021, he was President at Akamai Technologies and General Manager of Akamai’s Security Technology Group, where he was responsible for growing the company’s security business from a few million dollars of revenue in 2011 to becoming a $1.3+ billion business. Prior to Akamai, Rick served in several senior executive positions at Cisco Systems and was part of the early leadership team that built Cisco’s multibillion-dollar Communications and Collaboration business. He joined Cisco in 2004 when the company acquired Latitude Communications, a provider of fully integrated voice and Web conferencing solutions, where he had served as President and CEO. Rick holds an MBA and a B.A. in Quantitative Economics, both from Stanford University.

Rob Cahill is Chief Business Officer at Infrascan, where he's bringing robotic inspection technology to some of the world's largest energy companies. Based in Baku, he's spent years at the intersection of deep tech innovation and commercial execution - and has a reputation for simplifying the complex. When he's not closing deals with global energy giants, he runs marathons and sings in a band.

Rob Wachen is the co-founder and president of Etched, a startup building AI chips that are more than 10x faster than GPUs. A Thiel Fellow and Harvard dropout, Rob previously co-founded Prod, a startup accelerator with a cohort valuation of $50B+.

Robin Rombach is co-founder and CEO of Black Forest Labs, a frontier AI research lab dedicated to advancing generative models for images and videos. Before founding Black Forest Labs, he was a research director at Stability AI and pursued his PhD at LMU Munich. Robin's academic journey began with a Master degree in physics at the University of Heidelberg (2013-2020). His research has centered around making generative models more efficient, contributing to the development of widely adopted models such as VQGAN, Latent Diffusion, Stable Diffusion and Flux. He is a proponent of open-source models.

Robin Van Aeken is the co-founder and CEO of Magentic, building AI for enterprise procurement and supply chain teams. He is an Economics & Management graduate of the University of Oxford and began his career at McKinsey & Company before founding Magentic.

Robin Wauters is a seasoned technology and media entrepreneur, ex-journalist, analyst, keynote speaker and event curator, with a wealth of knowledge about the European startup and investment ecosystem(s). He's currently an executive advisor at Sirris working on the ScaleUp Flanders scale-up acceleration program, COO and board member at the European Startup Network and a co-driver of the EU-INC initiative. Robin is also a founding member of Belgian angel investment collective and early-stage fund Syndicate One, a co-founder of BeCentral, a digital campus located in Brussels, and the founder of boutique strategic communications advisory firm Profoundo. Robin was a co-founder and formerly CEO of Tech.eu, the premier source of European tech news and market intelligence. He was previously the European Editor of The Next Web, and a senior editor at TechCrunch

Rod Evans is NVIDIA’s Vice President for Supercomputing, HER Cloud and AI for Europe, Middle East and Africa. He joined NVIDIA in 2018 and has overseen strong growth in the High-Performance Computing and Artificial Intelligence businesses at NVIDIA across Europe. Rod is a highly accomplished international business leader and draws on more than 30 years of experience in the IT industry. Before joining NVIDIA, Rod led the European direct sales force at Intel Corporation and previously held European Vice President Positions at Silicon Graphics, Rackable Systems, Copan Systems and EMC. Currently based in Reading, United Kingdom; he has worked in the UK, USA, and Germany. Rod is a British national and alumnus of Queen Mary University, London.

Rodrigo Liang is the CEO and co-founder of SambaNova, a company that has revolutionized the AI landscape since its inception in 2017. With a bold mission to democratize access to AI in the enterprise, SambaNova has successfully bridged the gap between cutting-edge technology and accessibility, empowering companies of every size to harness the power of AI with its comprehensive full-stack offering. This groundbreaking approach, which includes the world's most efficient chips, systems, and software, is a direct result of the pioneering work done by Liang and his co-founders and Stanford Professors Kunle Olukotun and Chris Ré, at the esteemed Stanford AI Lab.Born in Taipei, Taiwan, and raised in Brazil, Liang's diverse cultural background has been a driving force behind SambaNova's innovative spirit and its name, which in Portuguese translates to New Dance, symbolizing the company's commitment to innovation and disruption in the AI space

Rohit is a Partner of Tiger Global Management and a member of the Firm’s private equity Investment Committee.  Prior to joining the Firm in December 2022, he was a partner at TCV.  Prior to TCV, Rohit was a private equity associate and chief of staff to the CIO at TPG, and before that, an analyst at Morgan Stanley.  He received a BA in Economics and South Asian Studies from Columbia University and an MBA from Harvard Business School.

Roman Chernin is co-founder and chief business officer of Nebius, a full-stack AI company. He leads product direction and business strategy, including for Token Factory, Nebius’ enterprise-grade inference platform for deploying and scaling open-source models. Roman previously spent more than a decade at Yandex, where he led search, and later maps & navigation.
Ronan Chambers is the Co-Founder of The European Technology Network, a neo-news channel and Europe's fastest growing New Media Business.

Roxanne Varza is currently the director of Station F, the world's biggest startup campus based in Paris. She is also an angel investor and board member of media company NRJ Group. Prior to her current role, she worked at Microsoft and TechCrunch. She also cofounded Tech.eu (acquired by Webrazzi), StartHer (now part of SISTA) and Failcon Paris conference. She holds degrees from UCLA, Sciences Po Paris and LSE.

Russ d'Sa is the co-founder and CEO of LiveKit, the leading agents framework and infrastructure platform for voice, video, and physical AI. What began as a pandemic side project now powers voice AI for ChatGPT, xAI, Salesforce, Coursera, Spotify, and thousands of other applications, facilitating billions of calls annually. Russ started his first company in the 2007 Y Combinator batch, joined Twitter as the 2nd frontend engineer, helped build 23andMe, and founded a deep learning startup acquired by Medium in 2019.

Ryan is an entrepreneur building AI companies in healthcare. As the first employee and second-in-command at Tempus AI, he played an instrumental role in scaling the company from Day 1 through its IPO. He led teams that built one of the world's largest multi-modal healthcare datasets and commercialized solutions with thousands of physicians and hundreds of biopharmaceutical companies.Ryan is also the cofounder and the founding CEO of Pathos AI, which is revolutionizing drug development through artificial intelligence. Under his leadership, Pathos is using AI to uncover patient subgroups that are likely to respond it it’s therapeutics to dramatically improve phase 2 clinical trial success rates. In the first three years, Pathos has raised over $400M from top investors including NEA, while building a causal AI platform and advancing a clinical-stage pipeline of precision oncology therapeutics.

Saahil Jain is the CTO of You.com. He started at You.com in December 2020 as a Founding Engineer and most recently served as the Head of AI. Before You.com, Saahil was a graduate researcher in the Stanford Machine Learning Group under Professor Andrew Ng, where he researched topics related to deep learning and natural language processing (NLP) in resource-constrained domains like healthcare. His research work has been published in machine learning conferences such as EMNLP, NeurIPS Datasets & Benchmarks, and ACM-CHIL among others. He has publicly released various machine learning models, methods, and datasets, which have been used by researchers in both academic institutions and hospitals across the world, as part of an open-source movement at Stanford to democratize AI research in medicine. Prior to Stanford, Saahil worked as a product manager at Microsoft on Office 365. He received his B.S. and M.S. in Computer Science at Columbia University and Stanford University respectively.

Sachin Katti is Head of Industrial Compute at OpenAI, responsible for the strategy and execution of the compute infrastructure that powers frontier model training and global-scale inference. He previously served as Chief Technology & AI Officer at Intel, where he led the company’s AI strategy and technology direction. Sachin has also been a Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at Stanford University since 2010, where his research has focused on next-generation networks and systems. He received his PhD in EECS from MIT in 2009.

Sahir Azam is an early-stage and growth investor at Index Ventures. He partners with founders building category-defining companies in AI, data, infrastructure, and security. His current investments include Ando, Fireworks AI, LiveKit and Temporal. Prior to joining Index in 2025, Sahir built a unique operating career at the intersection of business and technology, mostly recently serving as Chief Product Officer at MongoDB for nearly 10 years.

Sam Rodriques is Founder and CEO of Edison Scientific, a company that is building an AI Scientist to automate scientific discovery. Sam received his PhD in Physics from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology in 2019, where he invented several new technologies ranging from nanofabrication to spatial transcriptomics. His work has been featured in Science and Nature magazines, and on numerous press outlets including CNN, TechCrunch, and the Economist. Sam has co-founded four other companies, and has received numerous national awards and fellowships, including being named one of Time Magazine's 100 most influential people in AI for 2025, and receiving the 2022 Allen Distinguished Investigator Award.

As CEO of WhiteFiber and Bit Digital, Inc., I help guide and inform stakeholders about our HPC and digital assets companies. We have operations in US, Canada, and Iceland. We also own facilities for datacenters across North America across the eastern seaboard. I started my career as an attorney at Skadden, Arps. I was also Head of Capital Strategy at Bank of America Merrill Lynch. I’ve also co-founded a blockchain company that was sold to Consensys. This company's activities became a published case study in the Harvard Business Review.

Sami Issa is the Co-founder, Director, and CEO of Global AI, the world’s first hyperscaler focused on sovereign AI infrastructure. An American executive and serial entrepreneur, Issa has built national-scale semiconductor, cloud, and AI capabilities across the United States and the Middle East, leading major industrial transformations and shaping government technology strategies. Issa previously served as Executive Director at Mubadala, where he co-founded GlobalFoundries, served on its board for eight years, and helped lead the company’s global expansion, including the landmark Fab 8 development in New York. He chaired the Abu Dhabi–Singapore and Abu Dhabi–Saxony semiconductor working groups, advanced national research and education initiatives, and deployed significant investments into semiconductor R&D. He also co-founded Cognit, the Mubadala–IBM joint venture that built the first Arabic AI platform based on IBM Watson. Earlier in his career, Issa held leadership roles at Intel, Broadcom, Texas Instruments, and National Semiconductor. He holds more than 30 patents and an M.S. in Electrical Engineering from Arizona State University.

Sara Achour is a Research Fellow and cofounder of Unconventional AI, with a background in building software for emerging hardware technologies. She earned her PhD in Computer Science from Massachusetts Institute of Technology and also serves as an Assistant Professor in Stanford University's Computer Science and Electrical Engineering Departments.

Sara Hooker is a co-founder of Adaption Labs, which builds intelligence that continuously evolves. Sara leads a large team of AI researchers and engineers that build extremely efficient, adaptable systems. Sara Hooker was previously VP of Research at Cohere, a $6.8 billion frontier AI company focused on generative AI for enterprise. Prior to Cohere, she built large systems in computer vision and NLP at Google Deepmind. Her work has been featured in mainstream news outlets including Techcrunch, New York Times, Washington Post, Axios, MIT Technology, The Atlantic. Sara is a frequent expert advisor to AI research and policy initiatives around the world- she is currently on Kaggle's ML Advisory Research Board and serves on the World Economic Forum council on the Future of Artificial Intelligence and the Future of Data Frontiers. She has been listed as one of AI's top 13 innovators by Fortune and one of Time100 Most Influential People in AI.

Sara Maldon is a dynamic and driven leader in the AI space, helping companies unlock the value of AI for their business. As Head of Business Automation & AI at Make by Celonis, she led the efforts to deliver impactful AI & automation projects across all departments at Make as well as driving AI adoption from 8 to 96% of employees in just 4 months. Prior to joining Make, Sara managed data projects at a Paris-based scale-up and led a cybersecurity program at BNP Paribas. She is also a passionate advocate for diversity and inclusion in tech, with a focus on supporting women in STEM.

Sarah Franklin is the Chief Executive Officer of Lattice. She brings 25+ years of experience leading and scaling companies in tech and has a unique blend of technical knowledge, business acumen, and marketing expertise. Prior to joining Lattice, She spent over 15 years at Salesforce in a variety of executive leadership roles including President Advisory Board, Chief Marketing Officer, EVP Platform and Trailhead. Before Salesforce, she worked at a variety of companies ranging from scaled businesses to scrappy startups.  She holds a dual degree in chemical engineering and biochemistry from Virginia Tech and has been awarded many accolades including Forbes’ Most Influential CMO, “CMO to Watch” by Business Insider, and recipient of the Brand Genius Award from Adweek.

Sassine Ghazi is President and CEO of Synopsys (NASDAQ- SNPS), a leader in silicon to systems design solutions, from electronic design automation (EDA) to silicon IP and system verification and validation. Used by the likes of NVIDIA, Tesla, AWS, Intel and Microsoft, Synopsys is a mission-critical partner to semiconductor and systems customers across a wide range of industries to maximize their R&D capability and productivity, powering innovation today that ignites the ingenuity of tomorrow. They exited 2024 with revenue of approximately $6.127B (USD).

Savio Tovar Dias is Managing Director, EMEA at Rafay, where he leads the company's growth and strategic initiatives across Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. With more than 20 years of experience spanning telecommunications, cloud computing, managed services, and enterprise technology, Savio has worked with service providers, enterprises, and technology partners to bring innovative digital infrastructure and cloud services to market.At Rafay, Savio helps organizations build and scale modern AI infrastructure platforms, working closely with telecommunications providers, NeoClouds, sovereign AI initiatives, and enterprise customers across the region. His focus is on helping organizations transform GPU investments into secure, self-service AI platforms and revenue-generating AI services while maintaining the governance, compliance, and operational control required for production environments. He is passionate about helping organizations unlock the economic potential of AI while building resilient, sovereign digital infrastructure that supports long-term innovation and growth.

Scott Woody leads engineering and product for Stripe's core monetization and billing infrastructure. Scott was formerly CEO and founder of Metronome, where he led the company through its acquisition by Stripe. Before that, he led Dropbox’s Growth & Monetization engineering team, where he drove new product launches and helped scale the business to over 10 million paying customers.

Scott Wu is the co-founder and CEO of Cognition, the company behind Devin, the world's first and most advanced autonomous AI software engineer. He leads the company’s mission to build the future of software engineering. Previously, Scott co-founded Lunchclub, an AI-powered professional network connecting founders, operators, and investors. He grew up in Louisiana, competing in international programming competitions from the age of 12 and winning three IOI gold medals. Scott studied computer science and economics at Harvard University.

Sébastien Chauffray is CTO at PayFit, a leading European scale-up simplifying and automating payroll and HR processes for SMBs. With over 20 years of experience, he started his career as a software engineer before moving into senior leadership roles including Head of Engineering, VP Engineering & Product, and CTO at companies such as Veepee and sunday.He holds a Master’s degree in Information Systems and Networks from the University of South Brittany, where he graduated with highest honors, and an Executive MBA in Management & Entrepreneurship from Epitech. Sébastien is passionate about building high-performing teams, scaling organizations, improving workflows, and using technology, data, and collective intelligence to create sustainable business value.

Seth joined ICONIQ four years ago as a General Partner to launch the London office and to lead investment efforts across EMEA. He’s led investments in DeepL, ElevenLabs, Legora, Monzo, Nevis, Pigment, Rillet, Swap and more. Prior to ICONIQ, Seth spent close to a decade as a Partner at Accel investing in early and growth stage technology companies across multiple sectors.

Shalev Hulio is an Israeli technology entrepreneur focused on the intersection of cybersecurity, artificial intelligence, and national security. He is the Co-Founder and CEO of Dream, a sovereign AI and cybersecurity company building systems for governments and critical infrastructure to operate AI securely within their own environments.Prior to Dream, Hulio co-founded and led NSO Group, where he worked closely with governments on intelligence and counter-terrorism capabilities.His work today centers on helping nations move from fragmented data to AI-driven decision-making, enabling them to defend critical systems and operate independently in an era of cyber and AI-driven threats
Shaun O’Meara, CTO at Mirantis, has worked with customers designing and building enterprise IT infrastructure for 20 years. He is a member of the Forbes Technology Council.

Shiv is an experienced product executive and builder obsessed with creating beautiful products. He is the President of Auth0 at Okta, managing a $1B ARR business and overseeing the company’s product, data, security, and technology strategy and execution. Before Auth0 and Okta, Shiv was the SVP of Product at DigitalOcean, leading DigitalOcean’s products, pricing, and partnerships. Additionally, Shiv has held product leadership positions at global brands, including Amazon, NBCUniversal, LiveIntent, and The Nielsen Company. Shiv was the recipient of the 2023 Top Global CPO Award by the Products That Count community.Beyond his current corporate focus, Shiv is deeply committed to fostering entrepreneurship and innovation, particularly in Africa. He is an active angel investor and startup advisor and also serves on the boards of Products That Count and Aiven.io.

Shubho Sengupta has been working on Deep Learning for the past 10 years; first at Baidu Silicon Valley AI Lab and then at Meta’s Fundamental AI Research Lab. He has worked on speech-to-text and text-to-speech models, large Reinforcement Learning models for playing Go, scaling deep learning model training and more recently on using LLMs to build automated software systems. Before that he spent 10 years developing the fundamental parallel algorithms used for general purpose programming on GPUs. He is currently the CTO of Axiom Math, building a self-improving super intelligent reasoner starting with an AI mathematician

Siadhal Magos is the Co-founder and CEO of Metaview, the AI company focused on recruiting.
