Metaview is the Agentic Recruiting Platform with AI agents that handle sourcing, application review, notes, reporting, and all the admin recruiters hate. The result- radically efficient hiring end to end for 5,000+ world-class companies including Brex, ElevenLabs, Affirm, Airtable, and Replit. Before founding Metaview, Siadhal was a product leader at Uber, where he experienced the challenges of hiring at scale firsthand.

Sid Sheth is an experienced entrepreneur, business builder and executive in the semiconductor industry having managed businesses spanning processors and connectivity for cloud, telecom, wireless infrastructure and enterprise. He brings over 20+ years of experience incubating and managing businesses as a start up and as part of a publicly traded company, that have grown to over 1B$ in revenue.Currently, Sid is Founder, President & CEO at d-Matrix Corp, a start-up he co-founded in 2019, building an accelerated computing platform for AI inference, with a focus on unparalleled levels of efficiency. The goal of the start-up is to help change the trajectory of efficient AI compute for large workloads in the datacenter.Prior to d-Matrix, as Sr. Vice President & GM for the Networking Business Unit (HSC BU) at Inphi Corporation (NASDAQ - IPHI), he incubated and grew the networking business into a 1B$+ business with a focus on the AI datacenter segment (cloud, enterprise, 5G), the fastest growing and largest business segment at Inphi. Later, Inphi was acquired by Marvell (NASDAQ-MRVL) in a $10B deal.Prior to Inphi, Mr. Sheth was at NetLogic Microsystems (now Broadcom Corp.), where he held worldwide marketing responsibility for NetLogic’s enterprise, telecom networking interconnect chips. At NetLogic, he pioneered the company’s 100G product portfolio and led NetLogic’s entry into the mobile and cloud markets eventually helping that business grow to over 100M$In 2001, Mr. Sheth was a founding team member of Aeluros, a mixed-signal networking IC company, where he held global marketing responsibility, until its successful acquisition by NetLogic in 2007.Mr. Sheth also spent many years at Intel Corp, where he held engineering and management positions in the Pentium microprocessor and network processor groups.Mr. Sheth is a regularly featured speaker at industry tradeshows and conferences, is a published author at ISSCC and other technical conferences and has an M.S.E.E from Purdue University.Executive management, marketing, PNL and sales experience for a profitable product line at a publicly traded company. Broad responsibility in a multitude of business functions - marketing, sales, business development, program management, customer applications, marketing communications.

Simon Muskett leads innovation, AI, and quantum strategy at Digital Realty, reporting into the CTO office with a global remit. His focus- building a simplified path for innovators, OEMs, and partners that empowers businesses to grow during this inflection point. Simon grows Digital Realty’s infrastructure ecosystem by translating emerging compute - from GPU-dense AI clusters to early quantum systems and novel approaches to silicon - into measurable infrastructure and energy efficiency outcomes for enterprise customers. Realising the opportunity ahead for AI and Quantum Computing.With over 20 years of technology leadership across Dell, Ericsson, Citrix, and Digital Realty, Simon bridges the gap between hyperscale infrastructure economics and next-generation workloads. He advises on AI training and inference architecture, optimal power delivery, early quantum technologies, and bringing next-generation compute into commercial deployment.Simon guest lectures at the London School of Economics on AI leadership and holds board-level positions in the technology sector.

Soyoung Lee is the co-founder of TwelveLabs, an AI video understanding company. Rooted in Korea, accelerated in Silicon Valley, TwelveLabs builds cutting-edge multimodal video foundation models. Lee leads the go-to-market function, partnering closely with enterprises across media and entertainment, sports, advertising, government, automotive, and more to integrate advanced AI capabilities—such as semantic video search, video summarization, and video-to-text interaction—into their workflows. TwelveLabs has been recognized by CB Insights as one of the 100 most promising AI companies and by Fast Company as one of the World’s Most Innovative Companies.
Steeve is the founder of ZML, the fastest and universal inference stack. He used to work on self driving cars, at Exalead, Google and most recently was the VP Engineering of Zenly, acquired by Snap.

Stephanie Cohen joined Cloudflare in 2024 as the company’s first-ever Chief Strategy Officer. A seasoned operating executive, she brings more than two decades of experience at Goldman Sachs where she advised the leadership and boards of some of the world's most important companies and institutions. In addition to her time in Investment Banking, she was Goldman Sachs's Chief Strategy Officer, ran two multi-billion dollar global segments and served as a member of their Management Committee. In her role at Cloudflare, Stephanie leads the company's efforts in expanding the breadth and depth of its presence in boardrooms and the C-suite on its path to $5B in revenue and beyond. She focuses on the most important initiatives driving Cloudflare's growth, including the future of the network, international expansion, AI and the developer platform. Stephanie has a Bachelor of Science in Finance from the University of Illinois. She is a trustee for the Economic Club of New York and Park City Day School, a member of the Board of Directors for Quill.org and Fiserv and an Ambassador for US Ski & Snowboard.

Once an aspiring lawyer turned college dropout, Stevie “KillCreek” Case started her tech career accidentally as the world’s first female pro gamer. Across a 20+ year career, Case has led sales teams and revenue organizations at 0 to 1 startups and big brands including Visa and Twilio. In her current role as Chief Revenue Officer at Cloud 100 ranked security compliance company Vanta, she leads all go to market efforts including sales, customer success, account management, and revenue operations. Stevie is part of a venture fund called 20SALES, along with seven other GTM leaders from leading SaaS standouts, investing in seed stage startups in AI and B2B SaaS. Stevie is also a mom to many plants, pets, and one human, as well as a certified sailboat skipper and electric boat owner, obsessed with the ocean after growing up in landlocked Kansas.

Stuart Williams is Chief Operating Officer at Intercontinental Exchange, Inc. (NYSE- ICE). He is responsible for the day-to-day operations and support of the global infrastructure, data centers, networks and corporate IT systems that support ICE and its subsidiaries. Williams is also responsible for the customer service teams and oversees all disaster recovery and business continuity planning and operations. He also serves on the Commodity Futures Trading Commission Global Markets Advisory Committee (GMAC).Williams previously held several leadership roles at ICE Futures Europe, the London-based global futures and options exchange, including President, Chief Operating Officer and Director of Corporate Development. Prior to joining ICE as a result of the acquisition of LIFFE by ICE in 2013, Williams spent 10 years consulting with Protiviti and Accenture, working on a broad range of initiatives with exchanges, clearing houses and other financial sector clients.Stuart holds a Bachelor of Engineering degree from the University of Pretoria

Sudip Roy is a co-founder of Adaption, where he leads engineering efforts to buildintelligence that continuously evolves. Sudip was previously the Senior Director ofEngineering at Cohere, where he led Inference, Finetuning, and Platform for thecompany's foundation models. During his tenure, he scaled the engineeringorganization tenfold while helping drive revenue growth from under $1M to $100Mthrough industry-leading advances in system efficiency and reliability. A specialist inboth systems for AI and AI for systems, Sudip spearheaded strategic technicalcollaborations with major partners including Nvidia, Google, Oracle, Amazon,Microsoft, and Groq to optimize AI deployment at scale. Prior to Cohere, he was aresearcher at Google DeepMind, where he contributed to the core MLinfrastructure—such as Pathways and TensorFlow Extended—that powers trainingand inference thousands of models in production including Gemini. He holds multiplepatents in machine learning systems and is a recognized leader in scaling foundationmodel infrastructure.

Sunghyun Park is the Co-Founder and CEO of Rebellions, Asia's leading AI chip startup. With a Ph.D. from MIT and past roles at Intel, SpaceX, and Morgan Stanley, he returned to Korea in 2020 to launch Rebellions with four co-founders. Under his leadership, the company successfully merged with SKT’s AI chip subsidiary in 2024, becoming Korea’s first AI semiconductor unicorn. Rebellions has since raised over $470 million and partners with global tech firms to power energy-efficient AI infrastructure across the APAC region.

Sunjay Pandey has operated at the intersection of technology and economic impact from both sides — as a founder and inside some of the world's largest organizations. He leads an 8,000-person global technology organization driving agentic AI transformation across bp's $100B energy business. Previously at Amazon, he launched Alexa Skills and scaled the ecosystem to 10,000 developers. At Capital One, he built the world's first fiduciary-grade AI financial advisor, achieving 60% beta conversion — 10x the industry norm. Before enterprise, he built ALTERthought from zero, delivered $250M in documented client benefits, and founded Woven Money to serve 65 million underserved Americans. His edge- translating AI into architecture, architecture into operating models, and operating models into P&L impact.

Suresh Vasudevan is a seasoned infrastructure technology CEO with a track record of building category-defining companies. As CEO of Clockwork.io, he is leading the company's mission to pioneer Software-Driven AI Fabrics™ — a hardware-agnostic software layer that dramatically increases GPU cluster utilization for enterprises and AI clouds. Most organisations typically convert only 20–25% of paid GPU capacity into useful work, with the largest losses driven by failures, network disruptions, and communication overhead. As SemiAnalysis has documented, the real measure of useful work done by a cluster per dollar spent is goodput. Clockwork increases goodput with nanosecond-level observability, AI fault tolerance, and performance optimization — transforming underutilised silicon into a reliable, high-performance fabric across heterogeneous AI infrastructure.Suresh has led Clockwork to deliver landmark advances that are already reshaping how leading organisations think about cluster economics. FleetIQ accelerates AI job performance and raises infrastructure reliability for customers including Uber, Nebius, DCAI, and Nscale. The recently launched TorchPass uses Live GPU Migration to keep large-scale AI workloads running through GPU failures and node crashes — reducing wasted progress by 95%. Independently benchmarked by SemiAnalysis as the only fault-tolerant solution that maintains full-speed performance, TorchPass reframes cluster reliability as a primary driver of AI economics.Prior to Clockwork, Suresh built a strong leadership record across consequential infrastructure platform shifts. He led Nimble Storage from startup through IPO and acquisition by HPE, and served as CEO of Sysdig, growing it into one of the cloud-native security industry's most recognised platforms. Earlier in his career, he held senior executive roles at NetApp, where he was a key architect of the company's expansion from a focused storage vendor into a broader enterprise and data management platform. He started his career at McKinsey & Co., and holds a B.E. in Electrical Engineering from BITS Pilani and an MBA from IIM Calcutta.

Surojit Chatterjee is the founder and CEO of Ema, a universal platform building enterprise AI employees that work across business functions, from end-to-end human resources, customer experience, sales, and marketing. Ema helps organizations use agentic AI responsibly and effectively to augment human teams at scale. Previously, Surojit served as the CPO of Coinbase and a VP at Google.

job_title: Executive Vice President, Chief Information & Data Officer
bio: Susan Doniz serves as the Chief Information & Data Officer for The Walt Disney Company. In this role, Doniz leads the company’s global enterprise technology organization and is responsible for the company’s data architecture and governance strategy, partnering closely with key tech and data stakeholders across the company.Before joining Disney in February 2025, Doniz served as the Chief Information and Data Analytics Officer for Boeing, where she led a global team in all aspects of information technology, information security, data and analytics. She also supported the growth of Boeing's business through IT and analytics-related revenue-generating programs and was a member ofthe company’s Executive Council.Prior to Boeing, Doniz was the Group CIO of Qantas Airways, where she expanded the airline’s digital ecosystem and adopted new technology to support the needs of the business and its customers. During a 17-year career at Procter & Gamble, she led IT programs in support of sales, research and development, the supply chain, as well as a cross-functional program todigitize the company. Doniz also worked at SAP, where she was a strategic adviser to the global chief executive officer on transformation and technology issues in support of customers.Doniz has been a board member of multiple non profit organizations, including The Women’s College Hospital Foundation, Salvation Army and Engineers Without Borders. She serves as an adviser to the Center for Digital Transformation at the University of California, Irvine, Paul Merage School of Business and international Panel on the Information Environment.Doniz is an honors graduate of the University of Toronto with a bachelor’s degree in applied science and engineering and studied post graduate in Industrial Engineering from Eindhoven University of Technology.

Partnering with global companies to build conversational AI agents that transform customer experiences. Prior to Sierra, he spent 17 years at Google with a focus on building and scaling new businesses and products. He helped launch new products like YouTube, video ads, Chromecast, Android TV, consumer devices and subscriptions.

Sylvie is a seasoned executive, board member and entrepreneur.She is the founder of Blue Bridge Group AI (BB.AI)- the System Integration platform company for the AI era. Neither another SaaS nor a people-based service company, Blue Bridge is a heavily AI-augmented System Integrator focusing on helping companies augment their employees with AI assistants for complex tasks and replace their employees by AI agents for the most fastidious tasks, in an agile, flexible and future proof-way, for an optimized ROI. One persona at a time, without rebuilding legacy systems landscapes or transforming companies’ operating models. Delivering value, today, at speed, in a mastered way, across systems, onto companies’ workflow. Blue Bridge combines a focused team of Best in Class data scientists, business analysts / value realization experts and orchestration software engineers, heavily supported by automation, AI building blocks and modular pipelines. She is board member of WNS (a business process management company listed on the NYSE) and Technion University (global brand committee, next gen donators committee and France antenna). Sylvie has a track record of building and growing successful global technology-centric businesses. As CEO Shared Platform for Publicis, the advertising global leader, she was notably in charge of AI and relationship with large technology partners (OpenAI, Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Adobe, Nvidia) but also in charge of selecting start-up partners (Runway, Bria, ElevenLab,TruePic, Heygen etc etc...). She drove actual adoption at scale of generative Ai for ideation, creation, production and advertising activation optimization but also drove inhouse deployment of smart AI agents throughout companies’ processes. Previously, based in Singapore, Sylvie was International President (world ex China) of Envision GreenTech “Net Zero thanks to technology” champion (AIoT, wind turbine and EV batteries). She expended the business by signing hardware (turbines and batteries) and software deals with Total, Renault, Nissan, Shell, BP, Singapore government, Keppel, Sembcorp, Port of Antwerp, Nike, Starbucks... and recruiting an international team, in US, Europe and Asia. Sylvie was eight years at Allianz- global CEO of Allianz Assistance (based in Singapore), the B2B2C travel, automotive, home and digital service leader and chairwoman of Allianz Managed Operations and Services (based in Munich). She launched a greenfield mobile phone insurance activities in partnership with phone OEMs and ecommerce platforms (Samsung, Oppo, Vivo, Xiami, Huawei, Aliexpress, Ebay, Amazon). She managed a cost take out of 1bn€ per year to redeploy 500M€ in technology investments. She was also in charge of leveraging Allianz Venture participations and dealing with the start-up / fintech ecosystem (Revolut, N26, Simplesurance, Amwell, Good Doctor...).Sylvie has been global COO of Accenture Management Consulting driving sales, revenues, profitability, offerings, investments and acquisitions. She spent twenty years at Accenture helping clients transform and grow in the pharmaceutical, automotive and construction industries. Sylvie served in the board of M6 / RTL (TV and radio broadcasting), Covivio (REIT) and Aperam, during IPO time. Sylvie graduated from Ecole Centrale Paris and holds an Accenture-sponsored Kellogg’s Northwestern Executive MBA. She is decorated from the French Légion d’Honneur and a member of Le Siècle.

Tanuja is Managing Director of Amazon Web Services (AWS) EMEA, responsible for setting EMEA strategy and guiding the company’s growth across the region. She leads multi-country, cross-functional teams who work closely with customers, from startups through to the world’s largest enterprises. These teams support digital transformation, providing insight on the technical and business aspects of cloud computing. The AWS EMEA team works alongside partners, including independent software vendors, advisory partners and system integrators, to help customers innovate.Tanuja has more than 25 years of strategic, commercial and operational experience. She joined AWS from management consulting firm McKinsey & Company, where, as a Partner, she was responsible for leading enterprise transformation projects in the technology and industrial sectors. Prior to this she served as CEO, UK & Ireland for Schneider Electric, the global energy management firm. Tanuja spent 10 years with Colt as CEO of UKI and Benelux, and was head of strategy for EMC in North America in her earlier career.Tanuja serves as non-executive director on the board of BusinessLDN and was previously a trustee for Save the Children UK. She is committed to diversity projects and founded the PowerWomen Network—a cross-industry network for senior women business leaders. She has been recognised for her work in inclusion and diversity as a top-50 Champions for Women in Business by the Financial Times’ HERoes in 2017 and 2018. More recently, she was ranked number 6 on the UK Tech50 2022 list of most influential people in IT and was recognised in the Yahoo Finance 2022 Heroes Women Role Model Lists within the category “Top 100 Women Executives List”. Tanuja also hosts a podcast, PowerWomen Speak, on what it takes for women to be successful in business.She is an international citizen. Born and educated in India, with a Master’s degree from Boston University, Tanuja has lived in London for the past 19 years.

Tarun Galagali is Lattice’s Senior Director of AI Product and founder of AI-native coaching platform Mandala (acquired by Lattice), where he trained over 3,000 leaders at organizations including Google, Microsoft, Grow Therapy, and Pendo. He is also the co-author of PAUSE, an upcoming book set for publication in 2027 with Hachette.Previously, he held leadership roles at Google and Verily (Alphabet’s life sciences division), and helped lead Talkspace’s B2B strategy through its IPO. He is a graduate of Harvard Business School and Dartmouth College, and writes about leadership and AI for Forbes.

Thomas Di Giacomo is Chief Technology and Product Officer for SUSE, where he leads our global Office of the CTO and Product and Solution Management. His team covers SUSE’s entire portfolio of products as well as innovation priorities. He is also responsible for driving and guiding SUSE's current and future technology and solutions, and for sharing SUSE’s vision with customers, partners, and key industry stakeholders.Prior to his role, Thomas was SUSE’s President of Engineering & Innovation and was responsible for the rapid growth of SUSE’s expanding portfolio. He has over 20 years of experience in the IT industry, serving in various global leadership and executive roles in engineering and product innovation.Thomas holds a Ph.D. in Computer Science from the University of Geneva, and lives in Switzerland.

job_title: CDO - Chief Data and AI Officer
Managing Director, Co-Head of EMEA TMT and Head of EMEA Technology Investment Banking
bio: Thomas Koehrer co-heads the European TMT practice and is Head of European Tech Investment Banking at Bank of America. He has over 20 years of investment banking experience, advising CEOs, CFOs, and Boards on multiple transactions across M&A, IPOs, equity, debt, and private capital raises. His core clients span across AI, Enterprise Software and Services. Thomas graduated from ESSEC Business School in Paris.

Thomas enjoys creating open-source and open-science products that catalyse the field of AI by making complex research, models and datasets widely accessible — for instance by creating and/or advising the Hugging Face Transformers, Datasets, Diffusers, Accelerate, DataTrove, smolagents and LeRobot libraries. When he's not building OSS libraries, he can be found pushing for open-science in AI/ML through projects like the BigScience Workshop on Large Language Models which led to the BLOOM model and dataset, the Ultra-Scale Playbook and FineWeb, or building the robotics effort at Hugging Face including projects around Reachy Mini and fostering AI for Science collaborations across academia and industry at huggingscience.co. His current research interests are directed toward whether AI systems can participate in generating genuinely new scientific knowledge — not just accelerating known workflows — which he writes about (not often enough) in his blog and substack. He also co-authored the reference book Natural Language Processing with Transformers published at O'Reilly, and produced educational content including The Ultra-Scale Playbook and videos like The Future of Natural Language Processing.

Tim Davis is the co-founder and President of Modular, building a unified AI compute layer that enables deployment across any hardware platform. Before Modular, Tim spent seven years at Google where he co-founded TensorFlow Lite and scaled it to billions of devices. As Group Product Leader for Machine Learning, he led the development of TensorFlow APIs, XLA, TPUs, and MLIR. A repeat entrepreneur, Tim has spent his career building products that make compute infrastructure more accessible including at Google Brain bringing ML to mobile and edge devices, and now at Modular bringing high-performance AI inference to every hardware platform.

Tom Mackenzie co-anchors “Opening Trade” on Bloomberg TV, bringing daily analysis of the biggest market and business events across the US, Asia and Europe. He also hosts Bloomberg Tech Europe, a monthly show focused on the biggest themes and names in the European tech space.Tom has interviewed some of the world’s most influential founders and executives including Sam Altman, Jamie Dimon, Demis Hassabis and the CEOs of Goldman Sachs, Arm, ASML, Ryanair and Klarna.Tom was previously Bloomberg TV’s China Correspondent, based in Beijing where he covered the outbreak of the Covid-19 pandemic, the US-China trade war and China’s burgeoning tech sector. He landed exclusive interviews with Alibaba’s founder Jack Ma, Baidu CEO Robin Li, Huawei founder Ren Zhengfei and the governor of the People’s Bank of China.

job_title: Head of BlackRock Fundamental Equities Global Technology
bio: Tony Kim, Managing Director, is a member of the Fundamental Equity division of BlackRock's Portfolio Management Group. Mr. Kim is a portfolio manager and head of the technology sector and runs the Global Technology funds. Mr. Kim joined BlackRock in 2013. He has over 26 years of experience in investment management and M&A, focused exclusively on the technology sector. He has worked at companies including Artisan Partners, Credit Suisse Asset Management, Neuberger Berman, Merrill Lynch, and SG Warburg. Mr. Kim has an MBA from Columbia Business School and a BS in Industrial Engineering from the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign. Mr. Kim is also on the DFI board at Columbia Business School.

Travers Nisbet is a co-founder at Parallel Web Systems, where he leads product, GTM, and operations. Parallel is building AI-native web infrastructure—search and research APIs designed for agents rather than humans—serving AI-native hyperscalers and enterprises across sales, insurance, finance, and professional services. The company is backed by Khosla Ventures, Index, and First Round. Before Parallel, Travers was a GM at Chime leading bank strategy and operations during hypergrowth, and spent four years at McKinsey in San Francisco working with leading tech companies and fintechs and worked on research around AI governance.

Tuhin Srivastava has been working in the tech industry since 2009. He began his career as an Investment Banker at Macquarie Group, then became a Machine Learning engineer, an experience that shaped his early conviction that production-grade AI infrastructure would become the most critical layer in AI technology. He founded Baseten in 2019, well before the market agreed with him. His philosophy- the most useful AI will be built by domain experts who stay obsessively close to their customers and own their own intelligence. Baseten is valued at $13 billion and has raised over $2 billion to date from investors, including Altimeter Capital, Battery Ventures, Blackbird, BOND, CapitalG, Conviction, Durable Capital Partners, Greylock, IVP, NVIDIA, Sands Capital, Spark Capital, Verified Capital, Wellington Management, 01A, and others.

Valentin (Val) Bercovici is WEKA's Chief AI Officer. He has extensive experience in the data infrastructure industry, having previously been the Chief Technology Officer at NetApp/SolidFire, where he drove innovation in cloud storage and data management solutions.Val co-authored the Windows Shadowcopy snapshots and has made significant contributions to the storage standards community. As Co-chair of the Storage Networking Industry Association's (SNIA) Solid State Storage Initiative, Val helped to establish the first NAND Flash SSD storage standards. Additionally, Val served as the Chair of the SNIA Cloud Storage Initiative (CSI), where he led the development of the international S3 standard CDMI (ISO 17826). He was also a founding member of the Kubernetes Cloud Native Computing Foundation’s Governing Board, helping to shape the global direction of container orchestration.Val holds patents in AI agent smart contracts, streaming data integrity, and augmented reality (AR) for data center maintenance. His work continues to push the boundaries of what’s possible at the intersection of AI, cloud, and emerging technologies.

Varun Anand is the Co-Founder and Head of Operations at Clay, a GTM development platform serving over 5,000 customers including OpenAI and Anthropic.Before founding Clay, he built Newfront's employee benefits business to eight figures and scaled Candid's retail operation from 0 to 27 stores in 14 months.Anand graduated summa cum laude from the University of Pennsylvania with a degree in Political Science before transitioning from politics to startups, where he found his passion for building and scaling businesses.

Venkat Pullela is currently the CTO, AI Infrastructure at Keysight, responsible for Strategy and Emerging Technologies. He is a member of the Technical Advisory Committee (TAC) at Ultra Ethernet Consortium (UEC) and the Chair of the Governing Board at Open Programmable Infrastructure (OPI), a Linux Foundation project.He was previously Sr. Technical Director at Broadcom developing programmable switching technologies like Trident 4, NPL, P4. Earlier he built 8 generations of switching engines for Cat6K switches at Cisco, as a Sr. Director, Engineering.He has been an invited speaker at industry and academic conferences like P4, ONFConnect, OCP, Supercomputing, Linux Foundation and SIGCOMM. He holds 25+ patents.He received the first Pioneer in Technology award from Cisco; Distinguished Alumni award from NIT Warangal. He earned a BS in ECE from NIT Warangal, an MS in CS from IIT, Kanpur and MBA from ISB, India.

Venkat Ramakrishan is the President and COO of NeuBird AI. In this role, Venkat runs all of NeuBirdʼs Go-to-market and Field Operations along with Operational oversight and management of other company functions and is intently focused on scaling NeuBirdʼs Enterprise and Commercial business and delivering outstanding customer experience. Venkat Ramakrishnan previously served as the General Manager of the Portworx Cloud Native Business Unit at Pure Storage. Since joining Portworx in 2016, he has played a pivotal role in pioneering a new category of data management, protection, and services tailored for Kubernetes and containers. As a business leader, Venkat boasts a proven track record of consistently delivering innovative products for both cloud and enterprise. His extensive experience encompasses all facets of products and business operations, and he places a strong emphasis on investing in people and relationships. Thriving in highly collaborative environments, Venkat firmly believes in the collective power of great teams accomplishing greater things. Venkat has successfully built, led, and coached teams in engineering, product development, marketing, sales,  customer success, and technical support at Portworx and Pure. These teams have been instrumental in developing industry-leading products and assisting customers in addressing their most critical challenges related to container adoption. Prior to his role at Portworx, Venkat gained extensive experience in public and private cloud offerings, cloud storage, and software-defined application orchestration and management through positions at Cisco, IBM, Fusion-IO, AMD, and Western Digital.

Vik Malyala is the Chief Business Officer. Vik joined Supermicro in 2009 and has played a key role in shaping Supermicro’s global strategy, growth, and innovation. He previously served as Senior Vice President of Technology & AI and President & Managing Director of EMEA at Supermicro. With nearly 30 years of experience in product engineering, ASIC development, and technology leadership, Vik brings deep expertise in semiconductors and high-performance systems. A former design engineer at Serverworks (later acquired by Broadcom), he has led Supermicro’s advancements in AI, HPC, and sustainable data center technologies, including energy-efficient solutions such as liquid cooling. Vik has played a meaningful part in fostering strategic partnerships with industry leaders to strengthen Supermicro’s Total IT Solutions portfolio. He holds an M.S. in Electrical Engineering from the University of Idaho and a B.S. in Electronics & Communication Engineering from Osmania University.

Vipul is the co-founder and CEO of Together AI, the AI Native Cloud. Previously, Vipul founded Topsy, one of the largest search systems for social media, which was acquired by Apple, where he served as a Sr. Director to lead search and AI efforts in Siri.

Vishal joined FedEx in August 2025 and brings nearly three decades of experience leading digital transformation and leveraging AI and technology to drive business growth.As CDIO, Talwar is responsible for driving the FedEx digital transformation, focusing on the development of innovative solutions powered by data and AI, advanced technology, robust enterprise architecture, and comprehensive cybersecurity measures. He leads a global team that harnesses data and technology to optimize the FedEx network, elevate customer experiences, and drive new digital revenue.He also leads FedEx Dataworks, which leverages the power of the FedEx network, data, and AI to deliver new digital capabilities and customer solutions.

Vivian is a Partner at ICONIQ. She joined ICONIQ six years ago to build out the Portfolio Analytics platform, which empowers companies to make informed business decisions via proprietary analytics, insights, and problem-solving frameworks.  She works closely with founders and operational leaders at software and AI companies on a range of projects related to efficient scaling, GTM strategy, AI strategy, and technical excellence.  Prior to ICONIQ, Vivian was a management consultant at Strategy&, overseeing tech-driven implementations for F500 clients across the consumer and healthcare landscape.

Vlad Tenev is Chief Executive Officer and President of Robinhood Markets and Chair of Robinhood’s Board of Directors. In 2013, he co-founded Robinhood with Baiju Bhatt to democratize finance for all. From 2013 to November 2020, Vlad served alongside Baiju as Co-CEO and Co-President. Before Robinhood, he started two finance companies in New York City. Vlad holds a B.S. in Mathematics from Stanford University, an M.S. in Mathematics from UCLA, and FINRA Series 3, 7, and 63 licenses.

Walter Goodwin is founder and CEO of Fractile. Fractile is developing hardware designed to enable the world’s most capable AI models to run faster, more efficiently and at the scale required for longer-horizon AI workloads. He believes the speed of inference at scale is to become the most important factor for AI impact. Walter founded Fractile in 2022 after completing a PhD in Artificial Intelligence and Robotics at the University of Oxford, where his research focused on using early LLMs and other foundation models to build general-purpose embodied intelligence.

Wayne Nelms is the Co-Founder and CTO of Ornn, where he architects the exchange infrastructure, index methodology, and structured products powering the compute derivatives market. Wayne previously traded equity options at Susquehanna International Group (SIG) and worked as a software engineer at Google. He holds a dual degree from MIT with a background in computer science and applied mathematics. He also was an internally-recognized semi-professional tennis player and led the MIT Tennis team to two conference championships.

Wen Sang is the Co-Founder and COO of Genspark.ai, a Palo Alto based technology companybuilding agentic AI for 1B+ global knowledge workers. Since the launch of its Super Agent suitein April 2025, Genspark grew exponentially, scaling from 0 to $250M ARR in 12 months. Thecompany has reached a $2.6B valuation, raised $645M from investors including EmergenceCapital Partners, LG, and UpHonest, and formed strategic partnerships with OpenAI, Anthropic,Microsoft, AWS, and other leading technology companies.Previously, Wen founded Smarking, a Y Combinator- and Khosla Ventures-backed enterpriseSaaS company, and led it through a successful acquisition. He holds a PhD in MechanicalEngineering from MIT.Outside of work, Wen is an avid swimmer and golfer, and a devoted husband and father of twoboys who remind him that parenting is the toughest job of al

job_title: Executive Vice President and Chief Commercial Officer
bio: Will leads sales, field engineering, and partner enablement at Arm, helping some of the world's most innovative organizations leverage the newest technologies to ready themselves for the next wave of digital transformation. From IP to AI, Will's unique insight has helped the world's technology leaders transform their products and operations.Will joined Arm in 2004 and has held a number of leadership roles, including General Manager of Arm's Physical Design group. Before joining Arm, he worked in product management positions at Celoxica, Infineon Technologies, and Loughborough Sound Images. He serves on the board of EnPro Industries and holds a BEng from Sheffield Hallam University, UK.

Will Bryk is on a mission to rebuild search for the age of AI. As co-founder and CEO of Exa, he's building the search engine that AI agents and LLMs actually use, backed by over $100M from Benchmark, Lightspeed, Nvidia, and Y Combinator.Will grew up in New York City, studied CS and physics at Harvard, and led the robotics club while going deep on machine learning research. He founded Exa on a simple but sweeping conviction- the future of civilization depends on the quality of information we consume.

William Falcon is the founder and CEO of Lightning AI. Originally from Venezuela, he began his career as a Naval Officer undergoing US Navy SEAL training before studying at Columbia University. Later, while completing a Ph.D. at NYU funded by Google DeepMind and the NSF, he created PyTorch Lightning, which has become one of the most widely-used machine learning research frameworks. He also worked as a researcher at Facebook AI and at Goldman Sachs, and co-founded the now-acquired AI startup NextGenVest.His research at NYU CILVR Lab, advised by Kyunghyun Cho and Yann LeCun, is at the intersection of artificial intelligence and neuroscience. Falcon focuses on biologically-inspired fundamental research in deep and reinforcement learning, with applications in addressing social problems, neuroscience, and natural language processing.

Winston Weinberg is the CEO and Co-Founder of Harvey, the leading professional services platform engineered with AI for law, tax, and finance. Before founding Harvey in August 2022, Winston was an attorney at O'Melveny & Myers LLP, specializing in antitrust and securities litigation. He earned his J.D. from USC Gould School of Law, where he contributed to the Southern California Law Review, and earned his Bachelor’s degree from Kenyon College.

Yevgeny Dibrov is the co-founder and CEO of Armis, one of the world’s fastest-growing cybersecurity companies. Today, Armis protects global enterprises across industries, including 7 of the Fortune 10 and 40% of the Fortune 500. In 2025, Armis was acquired by ServiceNow in a landmark deal valued at nearly $8 billion, after surpassing $300 million in annual recurring revenue and raising $435 million in funding, including a round led by Goldman Sachs. Beyond his role as an operator, Yevgeny is also an active angel investor, backing cybersecurity and enterprise software companies that have gone on to achieve significant exits.

Yeyi Yun is the Co-founder & President of MiniMax, where she leads company operations and drives global growth across its LLM and AI platform business. MiniMax is a leading, publicly traded AI company building state-of-the-art foundation models and AI-native products, powering over 150 million users and tens of thousands of enterprises worldwide. Yeyi has played a pivotal role in MiniMax’s expansion, helping translate cutting-edge research into real-world products and experiences at scale.

Yinon Costica is co-founder and VP of Product at Wiz. Wiz provides a unified cloud security platform that protects everything organizations build and run - from code to cloud and runtime. Trusted by over 50% of the Fortune 100, Wiz enables security and dev teams to rapidly identify and eliminate critical risks and threats. A veteran in building category-defining products, Yinon’s leadership has established Wiz to become the #1 cloud security product by customer choice. Previously, he was VP of Product at Adallom (acquired by Microsoft) and later built Microsoft’s cloud security business into a revenue powerhouse. He holds M.Sc. degrees from the Naval Postgraduate School and the National University of Singapore.

Yves Caseau is Michelin Group CDIO, in charge of Digital transformation, AI andInformation Systems. He joined Michelin in 2017 as CIO and has worked extensively onmodernization, data-driven transformation and quality of service.He was previously AXA Group Head of Digital, responsible for the development ofinnovative web and mobile experiences, as well as the digital coordination of InformationSystems.Yves Caseau has been Bouygues Telecom’s Executive Vice-President in charge ofTechnology, Services and Innovation from 2007 until 2013. His key responsibility was todesign and to build triple play consumer products such as internet gateways and set-topboxes.He was Bouygues Telecom's CIO from 2001 until 2006. He has taught the “InformationSystems Theory and Practice” lecture at Ecole Polytechnique for three years and is afrequent speaker on information systems architecture.Yves Caseau has spent the first part of his scientific career – which started at Alcatel-Alstom – on software engineering, object-oriented programming and artificial intelligence.He then turned to operations research in 1990, first at Telcordia (New Jersey) then laterat Bouygues, which he joined in 1994.A former student of the Ecole Normale Supérieure (82), he holds a PhD in ComputerScience from Paris XI University (87) and an habilitation (HDR) from Paris VII University(95), as well as a MBA from the College des Ingénieurs.Yves Caseau is a member of the National Academy of Technologies, and the author ofbooks about information systems, lean management, enterprise organization and digitaltransformation.

Zach has led investments in more than 35 early-stage companies and served on over 25 boards. He focuses on AI/ML, data infrastructure, vertical software, B2B marketplaces, and fintech, backing companies including Lambda, CentML, Stack AI, Rad AI, Openly, Toku, Syrup, Spade, Clarify, and Elsa. Prior to Gradient, Zach led acquisitions and strategic investments at Google Cloud. He began his career as a quant and data engineer at a hedge fund and later worked as an investment banker advising leading software companies. He holds a BS in Computer Science and Applied Mathematics from UVA, plus a JD and MBA from UCLA, and lives in San Francisco.

Zain Asgar is co-founder and CEO of Gimlet Labs. He is also an adjunct professor of computer science at Stanford University. Previously, he was a GPU architect at NVIDIA, an engineering lead at Google AI, and the co-founder and CEO of Pixie Labs, which was acquired by New Relic

Zhenya joined Accel in 2023 and has a particular interest in AI, developer tooling, and workplace automation businesses. He has led Accel’s investments in Axle Energy, Polars, Recraft and Viktor, and works closely with Framer and Lovable.Prior to Accel, Zhenya held roles in multiple Accel portfolio companies. Most recently, Zhenya was CRO at Miro where he ran a 700-person GTM team across 12 global offices. Prior to Miro, Zhenya was Segment’s COO. Here, he built and ran a GTM team of more than 200 people and expanded the product-market fit into the enterprise. This then led to Segment’s acquisition by Twilio for $3.2B. Before Segment, Zhenya led teams at Dropbox across a number of different functional areas.Zhenya holds a mathematics degree from Lomonosov Moscow State University and an MBA from Harvard Business School.

Shruti Tournatory is a Partner at ICONIQ, where she leads portfolio operations and strategic growth initiatives across EMEA. She partners closely with founders and executive teams to accelerate go-to-market execution, strengthen operating capabilities, and navigate critical growth inflection points across the firm's enterprise software companies.With more than fifteen years of experience scaling SaaS businesses, Shruti focuses on building durable go-to-market engines and leadership networks that drive sustained enterprise value. Prior to ICONIQ, she led global business development and go-to-market at Sapphire Ventures, where she built a leading enterprise CXO network and launched the firm's Go-to-Market Center of Excellence. Earlier, she held senior leadership roles at SAP across product, strategy, and cloud innovation.
